FORMAT_VERSION.d: This value is incremented when changes are made to the eeprom format

PILOT_THR_FILT.d: Throttle filter cutoff (Hz) - active whenever altitude control is inactive - 0 to disable

PILOT_THR_BHV.d: Bitmask containing various throttle stick options. TX with sprung throttle can set PILOT_THR_BHV to "1" so motor feedback when landed starts from mid-stick instead of bottom of stick.

GCS_PID_MASK.d: bitmask of PIDs to send MAVLink PID_TUNING messages for

RTL_CONE_SLOPE.d: Defines a cone above home which determines maximum climb

RTL_LOIT_TIME.d: Time (in milliseconds) to loiter above home before beginning final descent

RTL_ALT_TYPE.d: RTL altitude type.  Set to 1 for Terrain following during RTL and then set WP_RFND_USE=1 to use rangefinder or WP_RFND_USE=0 to use Terrain database

FS_GCS_ENABLE.d: Controls whether failsafe will be invoked (and what action to take) when connection with Ground station is lost for at least 5 seconds. See FS_OPTIONS param for additional actions, or for cases allowing Mission continuation, when GCS failsafe is enabled.
FS_GCS_ENABLE.r: 0:Disabled/NoAction,1:RTL,2:RTL or Continue with Mission in Auto Mode (Removed in 4.0+-see FS_OPTIONS),3:SmartRTL or RTL,4:SmartRTL or Land,5:Land,6:Auto DO_LAND_START/DO_RETURN_PATH_START or RTL,7:Brake or Land

options.label: RTL or Continue with Mission in Auto Mode (Removed in 4.0+-see FS_OPTIONS)

GPS_HDOP_GOOD.d: GPS Hdop value at or below this value represent a good position.  Used for pre-arm checks

SUPER_SIMPLE.d: Bitmask to enable Super Simple mode for some flight modes. Setting this to Disabled(0) will disable Super Simple Mode. The bitmask is for flight mode switch positions

WP_YAW_BEHAVIOR.d: Determines how the autopilot controls the yaw during missions and RTL
WP_YAW_BEHAVIOR.r: 0:Never change yaw,1:Face next waypoint,2:Face next waypoint except RTL,3:Face along GPS course

FS_THR_ENABLE.d: The throttle failsafe allows you to configure a software failsafe activated by a setting on the throttle input channel
FS_THR_ENABLE.r: 0:Disabled,1:Enabled always RTL,2:Enabled Continue with Mission in Auto Mode (Removed in 4.0+),3:Enabled always Land,4:Enabled always SmartRTL or RTL,5:Enabled always SmartRTL or Land,6:Enabled Auto DO_LAND_START/DO_RETURN_PATH_START or RTL,7:Enabled always Brake or Land

options.label: Enabled Continue with Mission in Auto Mode (Removed in 4.0+)

FS_THR_VALUE.d: The PWM level in microseconds on channel 3 below which throttle failsafe triggers

THR_DZ.d: The deadzone above and below mid throttle in PWM microseconds. Used in AltHold, Loiter, PosHold flight modes

FLTMODE1.d: Flight mode when pwm of Flightmode channel(FLTMODE_CH) is <= 1230

FLTMODE2.d: Flight mode when pwm of Flightmode channel(FLTMODE_CH) is >1230, <= 1360

FLTMODE3.d: Flight mode when pwm of Flightmode channel(FLTMODE_CH) is >1360, <= 1490

FLTMODE4.d: Flight mode when pwm of Flightmode channel(FLTMODE_CH) is >1490, <= 1620

FLTMODE5.d: Flight mode when pwm of Flightmode channel(FLTMODE_CH) is >1620, <= 1749

FLTMODE6.d: Flight mode when pwm of Flightmode channel(FLTMODE_CH) is >=1750

FLTMODE_CH.d: RC Channel to use for flight mode control

INITIAL_MODE.d: This selects the mode to start in on boot. This is useful for when you want to start in AUTO mode on boot without a receiver.

SIMPLE.d: Bitmask which holds which flight modes use simple heading mode (eg bit 0 = 1 means Flight Mode 0 uses simple mode). The bitmask is for flightmode switch positions.

LOG_BITMASK.d: Bitmap of what on-board log types to enable. This value is made up of the sum of each of the log types you want to be saved. It is usually best just to enable all basiclog types by setting this to 65535. Note that if you want to reduce log sizes you should consider using LOG_FILE_RATEMAX instead of disabling logging items with this parameter.

ESC_CALIBRATION.d: Controls whether ArduCopter will enter ESC calibration on the next restart.  Do not adjust this parameter manually.
ESC_CALIBRATION.r: 0:Normal Start-up,1:Start-up in ESC Calibration mode if throttle high,2:Start-up in ESC Calibration mode regardless of throttle,3:Start-up and automatically calibrate ESCs,9:Disabled

options.label: Start-up in ESC Calibration mode if throttle high
Start-up in ESC Calibration mode regardless of throttle

TUNE.d: Selects parameter (normally a PID gain) that is being tuned with an RC transmitter's knob. The RC input channel used is assigned by setting RCx_OPTION to 219.
TUNE.r: 0:None,1:Stab Roll/Pitch kP,4:Rate Roll/Pitch kP,5:Rate Roll/Pitch kI,21:Rate Roll/Pitch kD,3:Stab Yaw kP,6:Rate Yaw kP,26:Rate Yaw kD,56:Rate Yaw Filter,55:Motor Yaw Headroom,14:AltHold kP,7:Throttle Rate kP,34:Throttle Accel kP,35:Throttle Accel kI,36:Throttle Accel kD,12:Loiter Pos kP,22:Velocity XY kP,28:Velocity XY kI,10:WP Speed (4.6 and earlier),25:Acro Roll/Pitch deg/s,40:Acro Yaw deg/s,45:RC Feel,38:Declination,39:Circle Rate,46:Rate Pitch kP,47:Rate Pitch kI,48:Rate Pitch kD,49:Rate Roll kP,50:Rate Roll kI,51:Rate Roll kD,52:Rate Pitch FF,53:Rate Roll FF,54:Rate Yaw FF,58:SysID Magnitude,59:PSC Angle Max,60:Loiter Speed,61:WP Speed (m/s)

FRAME_TYPE.d: Controls motor mixing for multicopters.  Not used for Tri or Traditional Helicopters.

DISARM_DELAY.d: Delay before automatic disarm in seconds after landing touchdown detection. A value of zero disables auto disarm. If Emergency Motor stop active, delay time is half this value.

PHLD_BRK_RATE.d: PosHold flight mode's rotation rate during braking in deg/sec

LAND_REPOSITION.d: Enables user input during LAND mode, the landing phase of RTL, and auto mode landings.

FS_EKF_ACTION.d: Controls the action that will be taken when an EKF failsafe is invoked
FS_EKF_ACTION.r: 0:Report only,1:Switch to Land mode if current mode requires position,2:Switch to AltHold mode if current mode requires position,3:Switch to Land mode from all modes

options.label: Switch to Land mode if current mode requires position
Switch to AltHold mode if current mode requires position
Switch to Land mode from all modes

FS_EKF_THRESH.d: Allows setting the maximum acceptable compass, velocity, position and height variances. Used in arming check and EKF failsafe.

FS_CRASH_CHECK.d: This enables automatic crash checking. When enabled the motors will disarm if a crash is detected.

RC_SPEED.d: This is the speed in Hertz that your ESCs will receive updates

ACRO_BAL_ROLL.d: rate at which roll angle returns to level in acro and sport mode.  A higher value causes the vehicle to return to level faster. For helicopter sets the decay rate of the virtual flybar in the roll axis. A higher value causes faster decay of desired to actual attitude.

ACRO_BAL_PITCH.d: rate at which pitch angle returns to level in acro and sport mode.  A higher value causes the vehicle to return to level faster. For helicopter sets the decay rate of the virtual flybar in the pitch axis. A higher value causes faster decay of desired to actual attitude.

ACRO_TRAINER.d: Type of trainer used in acro mode

THROW_MOT_START.d: Used by Throw mode. Controls whether motors will run at the speed set by MOT_SPIN_MIN or will be stopped when armed and waiting for the throw.

THROW_ALT_MIN.d: Minimum altitude above which Throw mode will detect a throw or a drop - 0 to disable the check

THROW_ALT_MAX.d: Maximum altitude under which Throw mode will detect a throw or a drop - 0 to disable the check

THROW_ALT_DCSND.d: Target altitude to descend during a drop, (must be positive). This allows for rapidly clearing surrounding obstacles.

THROW_ALT_ACSND.d: Target altitude to ascend during a throw upwards (must be positive). This allows for rapidly clearing surrounding obstacles.

WP_NAVALT_MIN.d: Altitude in meters above which navigation will begin during auto takeoff

THROW_NEXTMODE.d: Vehicle will switch to this mode after the throw is successfully completed.  Default is to stay in throw mode (18)

THROW_TYPE.d: Used by Throw mode. Specifies whether Copter is thrown upward or dropped.

DEV_OPTIONS.d: Bitmask of developer options. The meanings of the bit fields in this parameter may vary at any time. Developers should check the source code for current meaning

FRAME_CLASS.d: Controls major frame class for multicopter component

TUNE_MIN.d: Transmitter Tuning minum value. The parameter being tuned will have its value set to this minimum value when the tuning knob is at its lowest position

TUNE_MAX.d: Transmitter Tuning maximum value. The parameter being tuned will have its value set to this maximum value when the tuning knob is at its highest position

FS_VIBE_ENABLE.d: This enables the vibration failsafe which will use modified altitude estimation and control during high vibrations

FS_OPTIONS.d: Bitmask of additional options for battery, radio, & GCS failsafes. 0 disables all options.

bitmask.label: Continue if in Auto on RC failsafe
Continue if in Auto on GCS failsafe
Continue if in Guided on RC failsafe
Continue if landing on any failsafe
Continue if in pilot controlled modes on GCS failsafe

ACRO_OPTIONS.d: A range of options that can be applied to change acro mode behaviour. Air-mode enables ATC_THR_MIX_MAN at all times (air-mode has no effect on helicopters). Rate Loop Only disables the use of angle stabilization and uses angular rate stabilization only.

AUTO_OPTIONS.d: A range of options that can be applied to change auto mode behaviour. Allow Arming allows the copter to be armed in Auto. Allow Takeoff Without Raising Throttle allows takeoff without the pilot having to raise the throttle. Ignore pilot yaw overrides the pilot's yaw stick being used while in auto.

GUID_OPTIONS.d: Options that can be applied to change guided mode behaviour

bitmask.label: Waypoint navigation used for position targets

FS_GCS_TIMEOUT.d: Timeout before triggering the GCS failsafe

RTL_OPTIONS.d: Options that can be applied to change RTL mode behaviour

RNGFND_FILT.d: Rangefinder filter to smooth distance.  Set to zero to disable filtering

GUID_TIMEOUT.d: Guided mode timeout after which vehicle will stop or return to level if no updates are received from caller. Only applicable during any combination of velocity, acceleration, angle control, and/or angular rate control

SURFTRAK_MODE.d: set which surface to track in surface tracking

FS_DR_ENABLE.d: Failsafe action taken immediately as deadreckoning starts. Deadreckoning starts when EKF loses position and velocity source and relies only on wind estimates
FS_DR_ENABLE.r: 0:Disabled/NoAction,1:Land,2:RTL,3:SmartRTL or RTL,4:SmartRTL or Land,6:Auto DO_LAND_START/DO_RETURN_PATH_START or RTL

FS_DR_TIMEOUT.d: DeadReckoning is available for this many seconds after losing position and/or velocity source.  After this timeout elapses the EKF failsafe will trigger in modes requiring a position estimate

ACRO_RP_RATE.d: Acro mode maximum roll and pitch rate.  Higher values mean faster rate of rotation

ACRO_RP_EXPO.d: Acro roll/pitch Expo to allow faster rotation when stick at edges

ACRO_RP_RATE_TC.d: Acro roll and pitch rate control input time constant.  Low numbers lead to sharper response, higher numbers to softer response

ACRO_Y_RATE.d: Acro mode maximum yaw rate.  Higher value means faster rate of rotation

ACRO_Y_EXPO.d: Acro yaw expo to allow faster rotation when stick at edges

ACRO_Y_RATE_TC.d: Acro yaw rate control input time constant.  Low numbers lead to sharper response, higher numbers to softer response

PILOT_Y_RATE.d: Pilot controlled yaw rate max.  Used in all pilot controlled modes except Acro

PILOT_Y_EXPO.d: Pilot controlled yaw expo to allow faster rotation when stick at edges

PILOT_Y_RATE_TC.d: Pilot yaw rate control input time constant.  Low numbers lead to sharper response, higher numbers to softer response

TKOFF_SLEW_TIME.d: Time to slew the throttle from minimum to maximum while checking for a successful takeoff.

TKOFF_RPM_MIN.d: Takeoff is not permitted until motors report at least this RPM.  Set to zero to disable check

PLDP_THRESH.d: Ratio of vertical thrust during decent below which payload touchdown will trigger.

PLDP_RNG_MAX.d: Maximum range finder altitude in m to trigger payload touchdown, set to zero to disable.

PLDP_DELAY.d: Delay after release, in seconds, before aircraft starts to climb back to starting altitude.

PLDP_SPEED_DN.d: The maximum vertical decent velocity in m/s. If 0 LAND_SPD_MS value is used.

SURFTRAK_TC.d: Time to achieve 63.2% of the surface altitude measurement change.  If 0 filtering is disabled

TKOFF_THR_MAX.d: Takeoff maximum throttle allowed before controllers assume the aircraft is airborne during the takeoff process.

TKOFF_RPM_MAX.d: Takeoff is not permitted until motors report no more than this RPM.  Set to zero to disable check

FS_EKF_FILT.d: EKF Failsafe filter cutoff frequency. EKF variances are filtered using this value to avoid spurious failsafes from transient high variances. A higher value means the failsafe is more likely to trigger.

FSTRATE_ENABLE.d: Enable the fast Rate thread. In the default case the fast rate divisor, which controls the update frequency of the thread, is dynamically scaled from FSTRATE_DIV to avoid overrun in the gyro sample buffer and main loop slow-downs. Other values can be selected to fix the divisor to FSTRATE_DIV on arming or always.

FSTRATE_DIV.d: Fast rate thread divisor used to control the maximum fast rate update rate. The actual rate is the gyro rate in Hz divided by this value. This value is scaled depending on the configuration of FSTRATE_ENABLE.

TUNE2_MIN.d: Minimum value that the parameter currently being tuned with the transmitter's TRANSMITTER_TUNING2 knob will be set to

TUNE2_MAX.d: Maximum value that the parameter currently being tuned with the transmitter's TRANSMITTER2_TUNING knob will be set to

TUNE2.d: Selects parameter (normally a PID gain) that is being tuned with an RC transmitter's knob. The RC input channel used is assigned by setting RCx_OPTION to 220.
TUNE2.r: 0:None,1:Stab Roll/Pitch kP,4:Rate Roll/Pitch kP,5:Rate Roll/Pitch kI,21:Rate Roll/Pitch kD,3:Stab Yaw kP,6:Rate Yaw kP,26:Rate Yaw kD,56:Rate Yaw Filter,55:Motor Yaw Headroom,14:AltHold kP,7:Throttle Rate kP,34:Throttle Accel kP,35:Throttle Accel kI,36:Throttle Accel kD,12:Loiter Pos kP,22:Velocity XY kP,28:Velocity XY kI,10:WP Speed (4.6 and earlier),25:Acro Roll/Pitch deg/s,40:Acro Yaw deg/s,45:RC Feel,38:Declination,39:Circle Rate,46:Rate Pitch kP,47:Rate Pitch kI,48:Rate Pitch kD,49:Rate Roll kP,50:Rate Roll kI,51:Rate Roll kD,52:Rate Pitch FF,53:Rate Roll FF,54:Rate Yaw FF,58:SysID Magnitude,59:PSC Angle Max,60:Loiter Speed,61:WP Speed (m/s)

PILOT_ACC_Z.d: The vertical acceleration used when pilot is controlling the altitude

PILOT_SPD_UP.d: The maximum vertical ascending velocity the pilot may request in m/s

PILOT_SPD_DN.d: The maximum vertical descending velocity the pilot may request in m/s.  If 0 PILOT_SPD_UP value is used.

PILOT_TKO_ALT_M.d: Altitude that altitude control modes will climb to when a takeoff is triggered with the throttle stick.

FLTMODE_GCSBLOCK.d: Bitmask of flight modes to disable for GCS selection. Mode can still be accessed via RC or failsafe.

VID1_CAMMODEL.r: 0:Unknown,1:Siyi A8,2:Siyi ZR10,3:Siyi ZR30,4:Siyi ZT30 Zoom,5:Siyi ZT30 Wide,6:Siyi ZT30 IR,7:Siyi ZT6 RGB,8:Siyi ZT6 IR,9:Herelink WifiAP,10:Herelink USB-tethering,11:Topotek 1080p,12:Topotek 480p,13:Viewpro

VID1_IPADDR0.d: Video stream IP Address first octet

VID1_IPADDR1.d: Video stream IP Address second octet

VID1_IPADDR2.d: Video stream IP Address third octet

VID1_IPADDR3.d: Video stream IP Address fourth octet

BTAG_CUR_CYCLES.d: this is the highest value for battery cycles for all connected batteries

FOLT_MAV_CHAN.d: MAVLink channel to which FOLLOW_TARGET should be sent

WINCH_RC_FUNC.d: RCn_OPTION number to use to control winch rate

PLND_ALT_CUTOFF.d: The altitude (rangefinder distance) below which we stop using the precision landing sensor and continue landing

DIST_CUTOFF.d: The distance from target beyond which the target is ignored

SLUP_VEL_P.d: Slung Payload Velocity P gain, higher values will result in faster movements in sync with payload

SLUP_DIST_MAX.d: Oscillation is suppressed when vehicle and payload are no more than this distance horizontally.  Set to 0 to always suppress

SLUP_SYSID.d: Slung Payload mavlink system id.  0 to use any/all system ids

SLUP_WP_POS_P.d: WP position P gain. higher values will result in vehicle moving more quickly back to the original waypoint

SLUP_RESTOFS_TC.d: payload's position estimator's time constant used to compensate for GPS errors and wind.  Higher values result in smoother estimate but slower response

SLUP_DEBUG.d: Slung payload debug output, set to 1 to enable debug

THR_KILL_FUNC.d: AUX function to kill engine. This can be activated either with a RCn_OPTION and a R/C switch or with a ground station auxilliary function

THR_KILL_CHAN.d: output channel to change on throttle kill, a value of zero disables the feature

THR_KILL_VAL.d: auxilliary value to kill throttle. Set to 2 to kill the throttle when the auxilliary is high. Set to 0 to kill when auxilliary is low

THR_KILL_DEF.d: throttle kill default value. The default auxilliary function position on boot

CGA_RATIO.d: The ratio between the front and back motor outputs during steady-state hover. Positive when the CoG is in front of the motors midpoint (front motors work harder).

RTUN_STR_FFRATIO.d: Ratio between measured response and FF gain. Raise this to get a higher FF gain

RTUN_STR_P_RATIO.d: Ratio between steering FF and P gains. Raise this to get a higher P gain, 0 to leave P unchanged

RTUN_STR_I_RATIO.d: Ratio between steering FF and I gains. Raise this to get a higher I gain, 0 to leave I unchanged

RTUN_SPD_FFRATIO.d: Ratio between measured response and CRUISE_THROTTLE value. Raise this to get a higher CRUISE_THROTTLE value

RTUN_SPD_P_RATIO.d: Ratio between speed FF and P gain. Raise this to get a higher P gain, 0 to leave P unchanged

RTUN_SPD_I_RATIO.d: Ratio between speed FF and I gain. Raise this to get a higher I gain, 0 to leave I unchanged

RTUN_AUTO_FILTER.d: When enabled the PID filter settings are automatically set based on INS_GYRO_FILTER

RTUN_AUTO_SAVE.d: Number of seconds after completion of tune to auto-save. This is useful when using a 2 position switch for quicktune

RTUN_RC_FUNC.d: RCn_OPTION number to use to control tuning stop/start/save

RTUN_SPEED_MIN.d: The mimimum speed in m/s required for tuning to start

ESRC_EXTN_THRESH.d: ExternalNav may be used if innovations are below this threshold

ESRC_EXTN_QUAL.d: ExternalNav may be used if quality is above this threshold

ESRC_FLOW_THRESH.d: OpticalFlow may be used if innovations are below this threshold

ESRC_FLOW_QUAL.d: OpticalFlow may be used if quality is above this threshold

ESRC_RNGFND_MAX.d: OpticalFlow may be used if rangefinder distance is below this threshold

WEB_BLOCK_SIZE.d: web server block size for download

WEB_SENDFILE_MIN.d: sendfile is an offloading mechanism for faster file download. If this is non-zero and the file is larger than this size then sendfile will be used for file download

TA_ACT_FN.d: Setting an RC channel's _OPTION to this value will use it for Terrain Avoidance enable/disable

TA_PTCH_DWN_MIN.d: If the downward distance is less than this value then start Pitching up to gain altitude.

TA_PTCH_FWD_MIN.d: If the farwardward distance is less than this value then start Pitching up to gain altitude.

TA_QUAD_DWN_MIN.d: If the downward distance is less than this value then start Quading up to gain altitude.

TA_QUAD_FWD_MIN.d: If the farwardward distance is less than this value then start Quading up to gain altitude.

TA_PTCH_GSP_MIN.d: Minimum Groundspeed (not airspeed) to be flying for Pitching to be used.

TA_PTCH_TIMEOUT.d: Minimum down or forward distance must be triggered for more than this many seconds to start Pitching

TA_HOME_DIST.d: Terrain avoidance will not be applied if the vehicle is less than this distance from home

TA_ALT_MAX.d: This is a limit on how high the terrain avoidane will take the vehicle. It acts a failsafe to prevent vertical flyaways.

TA_GSP_MAX.d: This is a limit on how fast in groundspeeed terrain avoidance will take the vehicle. This is to allow for reliable sensor readings. -1 for disabled.

TA_GSP_AIRBRAKE.d: This is the limit for triggering airbrake to slow groundspeed as a difference between the airspeed and groundspeed. -1 for disabled.

TA_CMTC_HGT.d: The minimum Height above terrain to maintain when following an AUTO mission or RTL. If zero(0) use TA_PTCH_DOW_MIN.

TA_CMTC_ENABLE.d: Whether to enable Can't Make That Climb while running Terrain Avoidance

TA_CMTC_RAD.d: Use this radius for the loiter when trying to gain altitude. If not set or <=0 use WP_LOITER_RAD

ALAND_WP_ALT.d: Altitude of final approach waypoint created by script

ALAND_WP_DIST.d: Distance from landing point (HOME) to final approach waypoint created by script in the opposite direction of initial takeoff

AHRS_ORIG_LAT.d: AHRS/EKF origin will be set to this latitude if not already set

AHRS_ORIG_LON.d: AHRS/EKF origin will be set to this longitude if not already set

AHRS_ORIG_ALT.d: AHRS/EKF origin will be set to this altitude (in meters above sea level) if not already set

QUIK_DOUBLE_TIME.d: Time to double a tuning parameter. Raise this for a slower tune.

QUIK_GAIN_MARGIN.d: Reduction in gain after oscillation detected. Raise this number to get a more conservative tune

QUIK_OSC_SMAX.d: Threshold for oscillation detection. A lower value will lead to a more conservative tune.

QUIK_YAW_P_MAX.d: Maximum value for yaw P gain

QUIK_YAW_D_MAX.d: Maximum value for yaw D gain

QUIK_RP_PI_RATIO.d: Ratio between P and I gains for roll and pitch. Raise this to get a lower I gain

QUIK_Y_PI_RATIO.d: Ratio between P and I gains for yaw. Raise this to get a lower I gain

QUIK_AUTO_FILTER.d: When enabled the PID filter settings are automatically set based on INS_GYRO_FILTER

QUIK_AUTO_SAVE.d: Number of seconds after completion of tune to auto-save. This is useful when using a 2 position switch for quicktune

QUIK_RC_FUNC.d: RCn_OPTION number to use to control tuning stop/start/save

QUIK_MAX_REDUCE.d: This controls how much quicktune is allowed to lower gains from the original gains. If the vehicle already has a reasonable tune and is not oscillating then you can set this to zero to prevent gain reductions. The default of 20% is reasonable for most vehicles. Using a maximum gain reduction lowers the chance of an angle P oscillation happening if quicktune gets a false positive oscillation at a low gain, which can result in very low rate gains and a dangerous angle P oscillation.

QUIK_OPTIONS.d: Additional options. When the Two Position Switch option is enabled then a high switch position will start the tune, low will disable the tune. you should also set a QUIK_AUTO_SAVE time so that you will be able to save the tune.

QUIK_ANGLE_MAX.d: If while tuning the angle error goes over this limit then the tune will aborts to prevent a bad oscillation in the case of the tuning algorithm failing. If you get an error "Tuning: attitude error ABORTING" and you think it is a false positive then you can either raise this parameter or you can try increasing the QUIK_DOUBLE_TIME to do the tune more slowly. A value of zero disables this check.

PREV_RC_FUNC.d: RCn_OPTION number to used to trigger parameter reversion

RCK_FORCEHL.d: Automatically enables High Latency mode if not already enabled

RCK_PERIOD.d: When in High Latency mode, send Rockblock updates every N seconds

RCK_DEBUG.d: Sends Rockblock debug text to GCS via statustexts

RCK_ENABLE.d: Enables the Rockblock sending and recieving

RCK_TIMEOUT.d: If RCK_FORCEHL=2, this is the number of seconds of GCS timeout until High Latency mode is auto-enabled

FOLLP_FAIL_MODE.d: Mode to switch to if the target is lost (no signal or > FOLL_DIST_MAX).

FOLLP_EXIT_MODE.d: Mode to switch to when follow mode is exited normally

FOLLP_ACT_FN.d: Setting an RC channel's _OPTION to this value will use it for Plane Follow enable/disable

FOLLP_TIMEOUT.d: How long to try reaquire a target if telemetry from the lead vehicle is lost.

FOLLP_OVRSHT_DEG.d: If the target is greater than this many degrees left or right, assume an overshoot

FOLLP_TURN_DEG.d: If the target is greater than this many degrees left or right, assume it's turning

FOLLP_DIST_CLOSE.d: When closer than this distance assume we track by heading

FOLLP_WIDE_TURNS.d: Use wide turns when following a turning target. Alternative is "cutting the corner"

FOLLP_ALT.d: When non zero, this altitude value (in FOLL_ALT_TYPE frame) overrides the value sent by the target vehicle

FOLLP_D_P.d: P gain for the speed PID controller distance component

FOLLP_D_I.d: I gain for the speed PID  distance component

FOLLP_D_D.d: D gain for the speed PID controller distance component

FOLLP_V_P.d: P gain for the speed PID controller velocity component

FOLLP_V_I.d: I gain for the speed PID controller velocity component

FOLLP_V_D.d: D gain for the speed PID controller velocity component

FOLLP_LkAHD.d: Time to "lookahead" when calculating distance errors

FOLLP_SIM_TLF_FN.d: Set this switch to simulate losing telemetry from the other vehicle

FOLLP_XT_P.d: P term for the crosstrack/heading PID controller

FOLLP_XT_I.d: I term for the crosstrack/heading PID controller

FOLLP_XT_D.d: D term for the crosstrack/heading PID controller

FOLLP_XT_MAX.d: maximum correction retured by the crosstrack/heading PID controller

FOLLP_XT_I_MAX.d: maximum I applied the crosstrack/heading PID controller

FOLLP_REFRESH.d: refresh rate for Plane Follow updates

POI_DIST_MAX.d: POI's max distance (in meters) from the vehicle

ARM_SYSID.d: Check that MAV_SYSID (or SYDID_THISMAV) has been set. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_FOLL_SYSID.d: If FOLL_ENABLE = 1, check that FOLL_SYSID has been set. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_FOLL_SYSID_X.d: If FOLL_ENABLE = 1, check that FOLL_SYSID is different to MAV_SYSID. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_FOLL_OFS_DEF.d: Follow offsets should not be left as default (zero) if FOLL_ENABLE = 1. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_MNTX_SYSID.d: If FOLL_ENABLE = 1 and MNTx_SYSID_DEFLT is set, check that FOLL_SYSID is equal MNTx. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_RTL_CLIMB.d: RTL_CLIMB_MIN should be < 120m (400ft). 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_ESTOP.d: Emergency Stop disables arming. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_FENCE.d: Fences loaded but no fence enabled. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_RALLY.d: Rally Point more than RALLY_LIMIT_KM kilometers away. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_C_RTL_ALT_M.d: RTL_ALT_M should be < 120m (400ft). 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_P_Q_FS_LAND.d: Notify the user that on failsafe a QuadPlan will land. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_P_Q_FS_RTL.d: Notify the user that on failsafe a QuadPlan will QRTL. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_P_AIRSPEED.d: Validate that AIRSPEED_STALL(if set) < MIN < CRUISE < MAX d. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_P_STALL.d: Validate that AIRSPEED_MIN is at least 25% above AIRSPEED_STALL(if set). 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_P_SCALING.d: Validate that SCALING_SPEED is within 20% of AIRSPEED_CRUISE. If SCALING_SPEED changes the vehicle may need to be retuned. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_P_RTL_ALT.d: RTL_ALTITITUDE should be < 120m (400ft). 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_P_QRTL_ALT.d: Q_RTL_ALT should be < 120m (400ft). 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_V_ALT_LEGAL.d: Legal max altitude for UAV/RPAS/drones in your jurisdiction

TERR_BRK_ALT.d: terrain brake altitude. The altitude above the ground below which BRAKE mode will be engaged if in LOITER mode.

TERR_BRK_HDIST.d: terrain brake home distance. The distance from home where the auto BRAKE will be enabled. When within this distance of home the script will not activate

TERR_BRK_SPD.d: terrain brake speed threshold. Don't trigger BRAKE if both horizontal speed and descent rate are below this threshold. By setting this to a small value this can be used to allow the user to climb up to a safe altitude in LOITER mode. A value of 0.5 is recommended if you want to use LOITER to recover from an emergency terrain BRAKE mode change.

RK9_FORCEHL.d: Automatically enables High Latency mode if not already enabled

RK9_PERIOD.d: When in High Latency mode, send Rockblock updates every N seconds

RK9_DEBUG.d: Sends Rockblock debug text to GCS via statustexts

RK9_ENABLE.d: Enables the Rockblock sending and recieving

RK9_TIMEOUT.d: If RK9_FORCEHL=2, this is the number of seconds of GCS timeout until High Latency mode is auto-enabled

RK9_SERPORT.d: Serial port number to which the Rockblock is connected.This is the index of the SERIALn_ ports that are set to 28 for "scripting"

RK9_SCRPORT.d: Scripting Serial port number to which the Rockblock is connected for HL2 messages This is the index of the SCR_SDEV ports that are set to 2 for "MavlinkHL"

RK9_RELAY.d: RELAYn output to control Rockblock power. This connects to I_EN on the Rockblock header.

RK9_BOOTED.d: SERVOn GPIO channel that reads the Rockblock booted state. This connects to I_BTD on the Rockblock header. Requires SERVON_FUNCTION=-1

DR_ENABLE_DIST.d: Distance from home (in meters) beyond which the dead reckoning will be enabled

DR_GPS_SACC_MAX.d: GPS speed accuracy maximum, above which deadreckoning home will begin (default is 0.8).  Lower values trigger with good GPS quality, higher values will allow poorer GPS before triggering. Set to 0 to disable use of GPS speed accuracy

DR_GPS_SAT_MIN.d: GPS satellite count threshold below which deadreckoning home will begin (default is 6).  Higher values trigger with good GPS quality, Lower values trigger with worse GPS quality. Set to 0 to disable use of GPS satellite count

DR_GPS_TRIGG_SEC.d: GPS checks must fail for this many seconds before dead reckoning will be triggered

DR_FLY_ALT_MIN.d: Copter will fly at at least this altitude (in meters) above home during deadreckoning

DR_FLY_TIMEOUT.d: Copter will attempt to switch to NEXT_MODE after this many seconds of deadreckoning.  If it cannot switch modes it will continue in Guided_NoGPS.  Set to 0 to disable timeout

DR_NEXT_MODE.d: Copter switch to this mode after GPS recovers or DR_FLY_TIMEOUT has elapsed.  Default is 6/RTL.  Set to -1 to return to mode used before deadreckoning was triggered

SHIP_LAND_ANGLE.d: Angle from the stern of the ship for landing approach. Use this to ensure that on a go-around that ship superstructure and cables are avoided. A value of zero means to approach from the rear of the ship. A value of 90 means the landing will approach from the port (left) side of the ship. A value of -90 will mean approaching from the starboard (right) side of the ship. A value of 180 will approach from the bow of the ship. This parameter is combined with the sign of the RTL_RADIUS parameter to determine the holdoff pattern. If RTL_RADIUS is positive then a clockwise loiter is performed, if RTL_RADIUS is negative then a counter-clockwise loiter is used.

SHIP_AUTO_OFS.d: Settings this parameter to one triggers an automatic follow offset calculation based on current position of the vehicle and the landing target. NOTE: This parameter will auto-reset to zero once the offset has been calculated.

AEROM_ANG_TC.d: This is the time over which we filter the desired roll to smooth it

AEROM_THR_PIT_FF.d: This controls how much extra throttle to add based on pitch ange. The value is for 90 degrees and is applied in proportion to pitch

AEROM_SPD_P.d: This controls how rapidly the throttle is raised to compensate for a speed error

AEROM_SPD_I.d: This controls how rapidly the throttle is raised to compensate for a speed error

AEROM_ROL_COR_TC.d: This is the time constant for correcting roll errors. A smaller value leads to faster roll corrections

AEROM_TIME_COR_P.d: This is the time constant for correcting path position errors

AEROM_ERR_COR_P.d: This controls how rapidly we correct back onto the desired path

AEROM_ERR_COR_D.d: This controls how rapidly we correct back onto the desired path

AEROM_ENTRY_RATE.d: This controls how rapidly we roll into a new orientation

AEROM_THR_LKAHD.d: This controls how far ahead we look in time along the path for the target throttle

AEROM_DEBUG.d: This controls the printing of extra debug information on paths

AEROM_THR_BOOST.d: This is the extra throttle added in schedule elements marked as needing a throttle boost

AEROM_YAW_ACCEL.d: This is maximum yaw acceleration to use

AEROM_LKAHD.d: This is how much time to look ahead in the path for calculating path rates

AEROM_PATH_SCALE.d: Scale factor for Path/Box size. 0.5 would half the distances in maneuvers. Radii are unaffected.

AEROM_STALL_THR.d: Amount of throttle to reduce to for a stall turn

AEROM_STALL_PIT.d: Pitch threashold for moving to final stage of stall turn

AEROM_KE_RUDD.d: Percent of rudder normally uses to sustain knife-edge at trick speed

AEROM_KE_RUDD_LK.d: Time to look ahead in the path to calculate rudder correction for bank angle

AEROM_ALT_ABORT.d: Maximum allowable loss in altitude during a trick or sequence from its starting altitude.

AEROM_TS_P.d: This controls how rapidly two aircraft are brought back into time sync

AEROM_TS_I.d: This controls how rapidly two aircraft are brought back into time sync

AEROM_TS_SPDMAX.d: This sets the maximum speed adjustment for time sync between aircraft

AEROM_TS_RATE.d: This sets the rate we send data for time sync between aircraft

AEROM_MIS_ANGLE.d: When set to a non-zero value, this is the assumed direction of the mission. Otherwise the waypoint angle is used

TRIK_ENABLE.d: Enables Tricks on Switch. TRIK params hidden until enabled

TRIK_SEL_FN.d: Setting an RC channel's _OPTION to this value will use it for trick selection

TRIK_ACT_FN.d: Setting an RC channel's _OPTION to this value will use it for trick action (abort,announce,execute)

TRIK_COUNT.d: Number of tricks which can be selected over the range of the trik selection RC channel

EFI_HFE_ECU_IDX.d: HFI EFI ECU index, 0 for automatic

EFI_HFE_FUEL_DTY.d: HFI EFI fuel density in gram per litre

EFI_H6K_CANDRV.d: Halo6000 CAN driver. Use 1 for first CAN scripting driver, 2 for 2nd driver

EFI_H6K_START_FN.d: The RC auxilliary function number for start/stop of the generator. Zero to disable start function

EFI_H6K_TELEM_RT.d: The rate that additional generator telemetry is sent

EFI_H6K_FUELTOT.d: The capacity of the tank in litres

EFI_2K_CANDRV.d: NMEA 2000 CAN driver. Use 1 for first CAN scripting driver, 2 for 2nd driver

EFI_SP_THR_FN.d: SkyPower EFI throttle function. This sets which SERVOn_FUNCTION to use for the target throttle. This should be 70 for fixed wing aircraft and 31 for helicopter rotor speed control

EFI_SP_THR_RATE.d: SkyPower EFI throttle rate. This sets rate at which throttle updates are sent to the engine

EFI_SP_START_FN.d: SkyPower EFI start function. This is the RCn_OPTION value to use to find the R/C channel used for controlling engine start

EFI_SP_GEN_FN.d: SkyPower EFI generator control function. This is the RCn_OPTION value to use to find the R/C channel used for controlling generator start/stop

EFI_SP_MIN_RPM.d: SkyPower EFI minimum RPM. This is the RPM below which the engine is considered to be stopped

EFI_SP_TLM_RT.d: SkyPower EFI telemetry rate. This is the rate at which extra telemetry values are sent to the GCS

EFI_SP_LOG_RT.d: SkyPower EFI log rate. This is the rate at which extra logging of the SkyPower EFI is performed

EFI_SP_ST_DISARM.d: SkyPower EFI allow start disarmed. This controls if starting the engine while disarmed is allowed

EFI_SP_RST_TIME.d: SkyPower EFI restart time. If engine should be running and it has stopped for this amount of time then auto-restart. To disable this feature set this value to zero.

EFI_DLA_LPS.d: EFI DLA litres of fuel per second of injection time

EFI_SVF_ARMCHECK.d: Check for Generator ARM state before arming

ENABLE.d: When set to 0 this script will not run. When set to 1 this script will run.

BATT_IDX.d: Ensure this battery is configured with `BATT*_MONITOR=29`.

CFACT.d: This is multiplicative factor to correct the measured flow. Set to <1 if your sensor measures too high.

MODE.d: 0: The sensor will save the fuel consumption across power resets. 1: The sensor will reset the power consumption.

PORT.d: Which Scripting serial port the sensor is connected at.

EFI_INF_IGN_AUX.d: EFI INF throttle ignition aux function

LTE_ENABLE.d: Enable or disable the LTE modem driver

LTE_SERPORT.d: Serial port to use for the LTE modem. This is the index of the SERIALn_ ports that are set to 28 for "scripting"

LTE_SCRPORT.d: Scripting Serial port to use for the LTE modem. This is the index of the SCR_SDEV ports that are set to 2 for "MAVLink2"

LTE_SERVER_IP0.d: First octet of the server IP address to connect to

LTE_SERVER_IP1.d: Second octet of the server IP address to connect to

LTE_SERVER_IP2.d: Third octet of the server IP address to connect to

LTE_SERVER_IP3.d: Fourth octet of the server IP address to connect to

LTE_SERVER_PORT.d: IPv4 Port of the server to connect to

LTE_BAUD.d: Baud rate for the serial port to the LTE modem when connected. Initial power on baudrate is in LTE_IBAUD

LTE_TIMEOUT.d: Timeout in seconds for the LTE connection. If no data is received for this time, the connection will be reset. A value of zero disables the timeout

LTE_PROTOCOL.d: The protocol that we will use in communication with the LTE modem. If this is PPP then the LTE_SERVER parameters are not used and instead a PPP connection will be established and you should use the NET_ parameters to enable network ports. If this is MAVLink2 then the LTE_SERVER parameters are used to create a TCP or UDP connection to a single server.

LTE_OPTIONS.d: Options to control the LTE modem driver. If VerboseSignalInfoGCS is set then additional NAMED_VALUE_FLOAT values are sent with verbose signal information

LTE_IBAUD.d: This is the initial baud rate on power on for the modem. This is set in the modem with the AT+IREX=baud command

LTE_MCCMNC.d: This allows selection of network operator

LTE_TX_RATE.d: Maximum data transmit rate to the modem in bytes/second. Use zero for unlimited

LTE_BAND.d: This allows selection of LTE band. A value of -1 means no band setting change is made. A value of 0 sets all bands. Otherwise the specified band is set.

ESC_HW_POLES.d: Number of motor poles for eRPM scaling

ESC_HW_OFS.d: Motor number offset of first ESC

TOFSENSE_PRX.d: Set 0 if sensor is to be used as a 1-D rangefinder (minimum of all distances will be sent, typically used for height detection). Set 1 if it should be used as a 3-D proximity device (Eg. Obstacle Avoidance)

TOFSENSE_NO.d: Number of TOFSENSE-M CAN sensors connected

TOFSENSE_MODE.d: TOFSENSE-M mode to be used. 0 for 8x8 mode. 1 for 4x4 mode

TOFSENSE_INST1.d: First TOFSENSE-M sensors backend Instance. Setting this to 1 will pick the first backend from PRX_ or RNG_ Parameters (Depending on TOFSENSE_PRX)

TOFSENSE_ID1.d: First TOFSENSE-M sensor ID. Leave this at 0 to accept all IDs and if only one sensor is present. You can change ID of sensor from NAssistant Software

TOFSENSE_INST2.d: Second TOFSENSE-M sensors backend Instance. Setting this to 2 will pick the second backend from PRX_ or RNG_ Parameters (Depending on TOFSENSE_PRX)

TOFSENSE_ID2.d: Second TOFSENSE-M sensor ID. This cannot be 0. You can change ID of sensor from NAssistant Software

TOFSENSE_INST3.d: Third TOFSENSE-M sensors backend Instance. Setting this to 3 will pick the second backend from PRX_ or RNG_ Parameters (Depending on TOFSENSE_PRX)

TOFSENSE_ID3.d: Third TOFSENSE-M sensor ID. This cannot be 0. You can change ID of sensor from NAssistant Software

TOFSENSE_S1_PRX.d: Set 0 if sensor is to be used as a 1-D rangefinder (minimum of all distances will be sent, typically used for height detection). Set 1 if it should be used as a 3-D proximity device (Eg. Obstacle Avoidance)

TOFSENSE_S1_SP.d: UART instance sensor is connected to. Set 1 if sensor is connected to the port with fist SERIALx_PROTOCOL = 28.

TOFSENSE_S1_BR.d: Serial Port baud rate. Sensor baud rate can be changed from Nassistant software

ADSB_TYPE.d: Type of ADS-B hardware for ADSB-in and ADSB-out configuration and operation. If any type is selected then MAVLink based ADSB-in messages will always be enabled

ADSB_LIST_MAX.d: ADSB list size of nearest vehicles. Longer lists take longer to refresh with lower SRx_ADSB values.

ADSB_LIST_RADIUS.d: ADSB vehicle list radius filter. Vehicles detected outside this radius will be completely ignored. They will not show up in the SRx_ADSB stream to the GCS and will not be considered in any avoidance calculations. A value of 0 will disable this filter.

ADSB_ICAO_ID.d: ICAO_ID unique vehicle identification number of this aircraft. This is an integer limited to 24bits. If set to 0 then one will be randomly generated. If set to -1 then static information is not sent, transceiver is assumed pre-programmed.

ADSB_EMIT_TYPE.d: ADSB classification for the type of vehicle emitting the transponder signal. Default value is 14 (UAV).

ADSB_LEN_WIDTH.d: Aircraft length and width dimension options in Length and Width in meters. In most cases, use a value of 1 for smallest size.

ADSB_OFFSET_LAT.d: GPS antenna lateral offset. This describes the physical location offset from center of the GPS antenna on the aircraft.

ADSB_OFFSET_LON.d: GPS antenna longitudinal offset. This is usually set to 1, Applied By Sensor

ADSB_RF_SELECT.d: Transceiver RF selection for Rx enable and/or Tx enable. This only effects devices that can Tx and/or Rx. Rx-only devices should override this to always be Rx-only.

ADSB_SQUAWK.d: VFR squawk (Mode 3/A) code is a pre-programmed default code when the pilot is flying VFR and not in contact with ATC. In the USA, the VFR squawk code is octal 1200 (hex 0x280, decimal 640) and in most parts of Europe the VFR squawk code is octal 7000. If an invalid octal number is set then it will be reset to 1200.

ADSB_RF_CAPABLE.d: Describes your hardware RF In/Out capabilities.

ADSB_LIST_ALT.d: ADSB vehicle list altitude filter. Vehicles detected more than this altitude above our own altitude will be completely ignored. They will not show up in the SRx_ADSB stream to the GCS and will not be considered in any avoidance calculations. A value of 0 will disable this filter.

ADSB_ICAO_SPECL.d: ICAO_ID of special vehicle that ignores ADSB_LIST_RADIUS and ADSB_LIST_ALT. The vehicle is always tracked. Use 0 to disable.

ADSB_LOG.d: 0: no logging, 1: log only special ID, 2:log all

ADSB_OPTIONS.d: Options for emergency failsafe codes and device capabilities

bitmask.label: Transmit in traditional Mode 3A/C only and inhibit Mode-S and ES (ADSB) transmissions

AFS_ENABLE.d: This enables the advanced failsafe system. If this is set to zero (disable) then all the other AFS options have no effect

AFS_MAN_PIN.d: This sets a digital output pin to set high when in manual mode.  See the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

AFS_HB_PIN.d: This sets a digital output pin which is cycled at 10Hz when termination is not activated. Note that if a FS_TERM_PIN is set then the heartbeat pin will continue to cycle at 10Hz when termination is activated, to allow the termination board to distinguish between autopilot crash and termination. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

AFS_WP_COMMS.d: Waypoint number to navigate to on comms loss

AFS_WP_GPS_LOSS.d: Waypoint number to navigate to on GPS lock loss

AFS_TERMINATE.d: Can be set in flight to force termination of the heartbeat signal

AFS_TERM_ACTION.d: This can be used to force an action on flight termination. Normally this is handled by an external failsafe board, but you can setup ArduPilot to handle it here. Please consult the wiki for more information on the possible values of the parameter

AFS_TERM_PIN.d: This sets a digital output pin to set high on flight termination. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

AFS_AMSL_LIMIT.d: This sets the AMSL (above mean sea level) altitude limit. If the pressure altitude determined by QNH exceeds this limit then flight termination will be forced. Note that this limit is in meters, whereas pressure altitude limits are often quoted in feet. A value of zero disables the pressure altitude limit.

AFS_AMSL_ERR_GPS.d: This sets margin for error in GPS derived altitude limit. This error margin is only used if the barometer has failed. If the barometer fails then the GPS will be used to enforce the AMSL_LIMIT, but this margin will be subtracted from the AMSL_LIMIT first, to ensure that even with the given amount of GPS altitude error the pressure altitude is not breached. OBC users should set this to comply with their D2 safety case. A value of -1 will mean that barometer failure will lead to immediate termination.

AFS_QNH_PRESSURE.d: This sets the QNH pressure in millibars to be used for pressure altitude in the altitude limit. A value of zero disables the altitude limit.

AFS_MAX_GPS_LOSS.d: Maximum number of GPS loss events before the aircraft stops returning to mission on GPS recovery. Use zero to allow for any number of GPS loss events.

AFS_MAX_COM_LOSS.d: Maximum number of comms loss events before the aircraft stops returning to mission on comms recovery. Use zero to allow for any number of comms loss events.

AFS_GEOFENCE.d: This enables the geofence part of the AFS. Will only be in effect if AFS_ENABLE is also 1

AFS_RC.d: This enables the RC part of the AFS. Will only be in effect if AFS_ENABLE is also 1

AFS_RC_MAN_ONLY.d: If this parameter is set to 1, then an RC loss will only cause the plane to terminate in manual control modes. If it is 0, then the plane will terminate in any flight mode.

AFS_DUAL_LOSS.d: This enables the dual loss termination part of the AFS system. If this parameter is 1 and both GPS and the ground control station fail simultaneously, this will be considered a "dual loss" and cause termination.

AFS_RC_FAIL_TIME.d: This is the time in seconds in manual mode that failsafe termination will activate if RC input is lost. For the OBC rules this should be (1.5). Use 0 to disable.

AFS_MAX_RANGE.d: This is the maximum range of the vehicle in kilometers from first arming. If the vehicle goes beyond this range then the TERM_ACTION is performed. A value of zero disables this feature.

AFS_OPTIONS.d: See description for each bitmask bit description

bitmask.label: Continue the mission even after comms are recovered (does not go to the mission item at the time comms were lost)
Enable AFS for all autonomous modes (not just AUTO)
Option to not jump to AFS_WP_COMMS if already in the return path

AFS_GCS_TIMEOUT.d: The time (in seconds) of persistent data link loss before GCS failsafe occurs.

AHRS_GPS_GAIN.d: This controls how much to use the GPS to correct the attitude. This should never be set to zero for a plane as it would result in the plane losing control in turns. For a plane please use the default value of 1.0.

AHRS_GPS_USE.d: This controls whether to use dead-reckoning or GPS based navigation. If set to 0 then the GPS won't be used for navigation, and only dead reckoning will be used. A value of zero should never be used for normal flight. Currently this affects only the DCM-based AHRS: the EKF uses GPS according to its own parameters. A value of 2 means to use GPS for height as well as position - both in DCM estimation and when determining altitude-above-home.
AHRS_GPS_USE.r: 0:Disabled,1:Use GPS for DCM position,2:Use GPS for DCM position and height

options.label: Use GPS for DCM position and height

AHRS_YAW_P.d: This controls the weight the compass or GPS has on the heading. A higher value means the heading will track the yaw source (GPS or compass) more rapidly.

AHRS_RP_P.d: This controls how fast the accelerometers correct the attitude

AHRS_WIND_MAX.d: This sets the maximum allowable difference between ground speed and airspeed. A value of zero means to use the airspeed as is. This allows the plane to cope with a failing airspeed sensor by clipping it to groundspeed plus/minus this limit. See ARSPD_OPTIONS and ARSPD_WIND_MAX to disable airspeed sensors.

AHRS_TRIM_X.d: Compensates for the roll angle difference between the control board and the frame. Positive values make the vehicle roll right.

AHRS_TRIM_Y.d: Compensates for the pitch angle difference between the control board and the frame. Positive values make the vehicle pitch up/back.

AHRS_ORIENTATION.d: Overall board orientation relative to the standard orientation for the board type. This rotates the IMU and compass readings to allow the board to be oriented in your vehicle at any 90 or 45 degree angle. The label for each option is specified in the order of rotations for that orientation. This option takes affect on next boot. After changing you will need to re-level your vehicle. Firmware versions 4.2 and prior can use a CUSTOM (100) rotation to set the AHRS_CUSTOM_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles for AHRS orientation. Later versions provide two general custom rotations which can be used, Custom 1 and Custom 2, with CUST_ROT1_ROLL/PIT/YAW or CUST_ROT2_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles.

AHRS_COMP_BETA.d: This controls the time constant for the cross-over frequency used to fuse AHRS (airspeed and heading) and GPS data to estimate ground velocity. Time constant is 0.1/beta. A larger time constant will use GPS data less and a small time constant will use air data less.

AHRS_GPS_MINSATS.d: Minimum number of satellites visible to use GPS for velocity based corrections attitude correction. This defaults to 6, which is about the point at which the velocity numbers from a GPS become too unreliable for accurate correction of the accelerometers.

AHRS_EKF_TYPE.d: This controls which NavEKF Kalman filter version is used for attitude and position estimation

AHRS_CUSTOM_ROLL.d: Autopilot mounting position roll offset. Positive values = roll right, negative values = roll left. This parameter is only used when AHRS_ORIENTATION is set to CUSTOM.

AHRS_CUSTOM_PIT.d: Autopilot mounting position pitch offset. Positive values = pitch up, negative values = pitch down. This parameter is only used when AHRS_ORIENTATION is set to CUSTOM.

AHRS_CUSTOM_YAW.d: Autopilot mounting position yaw offset. Positive values = yaw right, negative values = yaw left. This parameter is only used when AHRS_ORIENTATION is set to CUSTOM.

AHRS_OPTIONS.d: This controls optional AHRS behaviour. Setting DisableDCMFallbackFW will change the AHRS behaviour for fixed wing aircraft in fly-forward flight to not fall back to DCM when the EKF stops navigating. Setting DisableDCMFallbackVTOL will change the AHRS behaviour for fixed wing aircraft in non fly-forward (VTOL) flight to not fall back to DCM when the EKF stops navigating. Setting DontDisableAirspeedUsingEKF disables the EKF based innovation check for airspeed consistency. Setting AutoRecordOrigin will auto-save the EKF origin to parameters when it becomes valid.

AHRS_ORIGIN_LAT.d: AHRS last origin latitude in degrees

AHRS_ORIGIN_LON.d: AHRS last origin longitude in degrees

AHRS_ORIGIN_ALT.d: AHRS last origin altitude in meters

AIS_LIST_MAX.d: AIS list size of nearest vessels. Longer lists take longer to refresh with lower SRx_ADSB values.

AIS_TIME_OUT.d: if no updates are received in this time a vessel will be removed from the list

ARMING_ACCTHRESH.d: Accelerometer error threshold used to determine inconsistent accelerometers. Compares this error range to other accelerometers to detect a hardware or calibration error. Lower value means tighter check and harder to pass arming check. Not all accelerometers are created equal.

ARMING_RUDDER.d: Allow arm/disarm by rudder input. When enabled arming can be done with right rudder, disarming with left rudder. Rudder arming only works with throttle at zero +- deadzone (RCx_DZ). Depending on vehicle type, arming in certain modes is prevented. See the wiki for each vehicle. Caution is recommended when arming if it is allowed in an auto-throttle mode!

ARMING_MIS_ITEMS.d: Bitmask of mission items that are required to be planned in order to arm the aircraft

ARMING_OPTIONS.d: Options that can be applied to change arming behaviour

bitmask.label: Do not send status text on state change
Skip IMU consistency checks when ICE motor running

ARMING_MAGTHRESH.d: Compass magnetic field strength error threshold vs earth magnetic model.  X and y axis are compared using this threhold, Z axis uses 2x this threshold.  0 to disable check

ARMING_CRSDP_IGN.d: Must have value "1" if crashdump data is present on the system, or a prearm failure will be raised.  Do not set this parameter unless the risks of doing so are fully understood.  The presence of a crash dump means that the firmware currently installed has suffered a critical software failure which resulted in the autopilot immediately rebooting.  The crashdump file gives diagnostic information which can help in finding the issue, please contact the ArduPIlot support team.  If this crashdump data is present, the vehicle is likely unsafe to fly.  Check the ArduPilot documentation for more details.
ARMING_CRSDP_IGN.r: 0:Crash Dump arming check active,1:Crash Dump arming check deactivated

ARMING_NEED_LOC.d: Require that the vehicle have an absolute position before it arms.  This can help ensure that the vehicle can Return To Launch.

ARMING_SKIPCHK.d: Checks to skip prior to arming motor. This is a bitmask of checks before allowing arming that will be skipped. For most users it is recommended to leave this at the default of 0 (no checks skipped). In extreme circumstances, a value of -1 can be used to skip all non-mandatory current and future checks.

AROT_ENABLE.d: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the autonomous autorotation capability.

AROT_HS_P.d: Increase value to increase sensitivity of head speed controller during autonomous autorotation.

AROT_HS_SET_PT.d: The target head speed in RPM during autorotation. Start by setting to desired hover speed and tune from there as necessary.

AROT_FWD_SP_TARG.d: Target ground speed in cm/s for the autorotation controller to try and achieve/ maintain during the glide phase.

AROT_COL_FILT_E.d: Cut-off frequency for collective low pass filter. For the entry phase. Acts as a following trim.  Must be higher than AROT_COL_FILT_G.

AROT_COL_FILT_G.d: Cut-off frequency for collective low pass filter. For the glide phase. Acts as a following trim.  Must be lower than AROT_COL_FILT_E.

AROT_XY_ACC_MAX.d: Maximum body frame acceleration allowed in the in speed controller. This limit defines a circular constraint in accel. Minimum used is 0.5 m/s/s.

AROT_HS_SENSOR.d: Allocate the RPM sensor instance to use for measuring head speed. RPM1 = 0.  RPM2 = 1.

AROT_FWD_P.d: Converts the difference between desired forward speed and actual speed into an acceleration target that is passed to the pitch angle controller.

AROT_FWD_I.d: Corrects long-term difference in desired velocity to a target acceleration.

AROT_FWD_IMAX.d: Constrains the target acceleration that the I gain will output.

AROT_FWD_FF.d: Produces an output that is proportional to the magnitude of the target.

AROT_FWD_FLTE.d: This filter low pass filter is applied to the input for P and I terms.

AROT_FWD_FLTD.d: This filter low pass filter is applied to the input for D terms.

ARSPD_TUBE_ORDER.d: This parameter allows you to control whether the order in which the tubes are attached to your pitot tube matters. If you set this to 0 then the first (often the top) connector on the sensor needs to be the stagnation pressure (the pressure at the tip of the pitot tube). If set to 1 then the second (often the bottom) connector needs to be the stagnation pressure. If set to 2 (the default) then the airspeed driver will accept either order. The reason you may wish to specify the order is it will allow your airspeed sensor to detect if the aircraft is receiving excessive pressure on the static port compared to the stagnation port such as during a stall, which would otherwise be seen as a positive airspeed.

ARSPD_PRIMARY.d: This selects which airspeed sensor will be the primary if multiple sensors are found

ARSPD_OPTIONS.d: This parameter and function is not used by this vehicle. Always set to 0.

ARSPD_WIND_MAX.d: This parameter and function is not used by this vehicle. Always set to 0.

ARSPD_WIND_WARN.d: This parameter and function is not used by this vehicle. Always set to 0.

ARSPD_WIND_GATE.d: This parameter and function is not used by this vehicle.

ARSPD2_USE.d: This parameter is not used by this vehicle. Always set to 0.

ARSPD2_RATIO.d: Calibrates pitot tube pressure to velocity. Increasing this value will indicate a higher airspeed at any given dynamic pressure.

ARSPD2_PIN.d: The pin number that the airspeed sensor is connected to for analog sensors. Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

ARSPD2_AUTOCAL.d: Enables automatic adjustment of airspeed ratio during a calibration flight based on estimation of ground speed and true airspeed. New ratio saved every 2 minutes if change is > 5%. Should not be left enabled.

ARSPD2_TUBE_ORDR.d: This parameter allows you to control whether the order in which the tubes are attached to your pitot tube matters. If you set this to 0 then the first (often the top) connector on the sensor needs to be the stagnation pressure (the pressure at the tip of the pitot tube). If set to 1 then the second (often the bottom) connector needs to be the stagnation pressure. If set to 2 (the default) then the airspeed driver will accept either order. The reason you may wish to specify the order is it will allow your airspeed sensor to detect if the aircraft is receiving excessive pressure on the static port compared to the stagnation port such as during a stall, which would otherwise be seen as a positive airspeed.

ARSPD2_SKIP_CAL.d: This parameter allows you to skip airspeed offset calibration on startup, instead using the offset from the last calibration or requiring a manual calibration. This may be desirable if the offset variance between flights for your sensor is low and you want to avoid having to cover the pitot tube on each boot.
ARSPD2_SKIP_CAL.r: 0:Disable,1:Do not require offset calibration before flight. Manual calibration should be performed during initial setup.,2:Do not calibrate on start up. Manual calibration must be performed once per boot.

options.label: Do not require offset calibration before flight. Manual calibration should be performed during initial setup.
Do not calibrate on start up. Manual calibration must be performed once per boot.

ARSPD2_PSI_RANGE.d: This parameter allows you to set the PSI (pounds per square inch) range for your sensor. You should not change this unless you examine the datasheet for your device

ARSPD2_BUS.d: Bus number of the I2C bus where the airspeed sensor is connected. May not correspond to board's I2C bus number labels. Retry another bus and reboot if airspeed sensor fails to initialize.

ARSPD2_DEVID.d: Airspeed sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

ATC_RATE_FF_ENAB.d: Controls whether body-frame rate feedforward is enabled or disabled

ATC_ANGLE_BOOST.d: Angle Boost increases output throttle as the vehicle leans to reduce loss of altitude

ATC_ANG_RLL_P.d: Roll axis angle controller P gain.  Converts the error between the desired roll angle and actual angle to a desired roll rate

ATC_ANG_PIT_P.d: Pitch axis angle controller P gain.  Converts the error between the desired pitch angle and actual angle to a desired pitch rate

ATC_ANG_YAW_P.d: Yaw axis angle controller P gain.  Converts the error between the desired yaw angle and actual angle to a desired yaw rate

ATC_ANG_LIM_TC.d: Angle Limit (to maintain altitude) Time Constant

ATC_RATE_R_MAX.d: Maximum angular velocity in roll axis

ATC_RATE_P_MAX.d: Maximum angular velocity in pitch axis

ATC_RATE_Y_MAX.d: Maximum angular velocity in yaw axis

ATC_INPUT_TC.d: Attitude control input time constant.  Low numbers lead to sharper response, higher numbers to softer response

ATC_LAND_R_MULT.d: Roll gain multiplier active when landed. A factor of 1.0 means no reduction in gain while landed. Reduce this factor to reduce ground oscitation in the roll axis.

ATC_LAND_P_MULT.d: Pitch gain multiplier active when landed. A factor of 1.0 means no reduction in gain while landed. Reduce this factor to reduce ground oscitation in the pitch axis.

ATC_LAND_Y_MULT.d: Yaw gain multiplier active when landed. A factor of 1.0 means no reduction in gain while landed. Reduce this factor to reduce ground oscitation in the yaw axis.

ATC_ANGLE_MAX.d: Maximum lean angle in all flight modes

ATC_RATE_WPY_MAX.d: Maximum rate the yaw target can be updated in Auto, Guided, Circle, Follow, RTL, SmartRTL, Throw and ZigZag flight modes

ATC_RAT_RLL_P.d: Roll axis rate controller P gain.  Corrects in proportion to the difference between the desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

ATC_RAT_RLL_I.d: Roll axis rate controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

ATC_RAT_RLL_IMAX.d: Roll axis rate controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum that the I term will output

ATC_RAT_RLL_D.d: Roll axis rate controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

ATC_RAT_RLL_FF.d: Roll axis rate controller feed forward

ATC_RAT_RLL_FLTT.d: Roll axis rate controller target frequency in Hz

ATC_RAT_RLL_FLTE.d: Roll axis rate controller error frequency in Hz

ATC_RAT_RLL_FLTD.d: Roll axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz

ATC_RAT_RLL_SMAX.d: Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

ATC_RAT_RLL_PDMX.d: Roll axis rate controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

ATC_RAT_RLL_D_FF.d: FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

ATC_RAT_PIT_P.d: Pitch axis rate controller P gain.  Corrects in proportion to the difference between the desired pitch rate vs actual pitch rate

ATC_RAT_PIT_I.d: Pitch axis rate controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired pitch rate vs actual pitch rate

ATC_RAT_PIT_IMAX.d: Pitch axis rate controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum that the I term will output

ATC_RAT_PIT_D.d: Pitch axis rate controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired pitch rate vs actual pitch rate

ATC_RAT_PIT_FF.d: Pitch axis rate controller feed forward

ATC_RAT_PIT_FLTT.d: Pitch axis rate controller target frequency in Hz

ATC_RAT_PIT_FLTE.d: Pitch axis rate controller error frequency in Hz

ATC_RAT_PIT_FLTD.d: Pitch axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz

ATC_RAT_PIT_SMAX.d: Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

ATC_RAT_PIT_PDMX.d: Pitch axis rate controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

ATC_RAT_PIT_D_FF.d: FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

ATC_RAT_YAW_P.d: Yaw axis rate controller P gain.  Corrects in proportion to the difference between the desired yaw rate vs actual yaw rate

ATC_RAT_YAW_I.d: Yaw axis rate controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired yaw rate vs actual yaw rate

ATC_RAT_YAW_IMAX.d: Yaw axis rate controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum that the I term will output

ATC_RAT_YAW_D.d: Yaw axis rate controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired yaw rate vs actual yaw rate

ATC_RAT_YAW_FF.d: Yaw axis rate controller feed forward

ATC_RAT_YAW_FLTT.d: Yaw axis rate controller target frequency in Hz

ATC_RAT_YAW_FLTE.d: Yaw axis rate controller error frequency in Hz

ATC_RAT_YAW_FLTD.d: Yaw axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz

ATC_RAT_YAW_SMAX.d: Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

ATC_RAT_YAW_PDMX.d: Yaw axis rate controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

ATC_RAT_YAW_D_FF.d: FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

ATC_THR_MIX_MIN.d: Throttle vs attitude control prioritisation used when landing (higher values mean we prioritise attitude control over throttle)

ATC_THR_MIX_MAX.d: Throttle vs attitude control prioritisation used during active flight (higher values mean we prioritise attitude control over throttle)

ATC_THR_MIX_MAN.d: Throttle vs attitude control prioritisation used during manual flight (higher values mean we prioritise attitude control over throttle)

ATC_THR_G_BOOST.d: Throttle-gain boost ratio. A value of 0 means no boosting is applied, a value of 1 means full boosting is applied. Describes the ratio increase that is applied to angle P and PD on pitch and roll.

ATC_HOVR_ROL_TRM.d: Trim the hover roll angle to counter tail rotor thrust in a hover

ATC_RAT_RLL_ILMI.d: Point below which I-term will not leak down

ATC_RAT_PIT_ILMI.d: Point below which I-term will not leak down

ATC_RAT_YAW_ILMI.d: Point below which I-term will not leak down

AUTOTUNE_AGGR.d: Autotune aggressiveness. Defines the bounce back used to detect size of the D term.

AUTOTUNE_GMBK.d: Fraction by which tuned P and D gains are reduced after rate and angle AutoTune steps complete. This provides extra stability margin by reducing gains slightly from the optimal values found during tuning. A value of 0.0 applies no reduction. A value of 0.25 reduces tuned gains by 25%.

AUTOTUNE_SEQ.d: 2-byte bitmask to select what tuning should be performed.  Max gain automatically performed if Rate D is selected. Values: 7:All,1:VFF Only,2:Rate D/Rate P Only(incl max gain),4:Angle P Only,8:Max Gain Only,16:Tune Check,3:VFF and Rate D/Rate P(incl max gain),5:VFF and Angle P,6:Rate D/Rate P(incl max gain) and angle P

AUTOTUNE_FRQ_MIN.d: Defines the start frequency for sweeps and dwells

AUTOTUNE_FRQ_MAX.d: Defines the end frequency for sweeps and dwells

AUTOTUNE_GN_MAX.d: Defines the response gain (output/input) to tune

AUTOTUNE_VELXY_P.d: Velocity xy P gain used to hold position during Max Gain, Rate P, and Rate D frequency sweeps

AUTOTUNE_ACC_MAX.d: maximum angular acceleration in deg/s/s allowed during autotune maneuvers

AUTOTUNE_RAT_MAX.d: maximum angular rate in deg/s allowed during autotune maneuvers

AVD_F_ACTION.d: Specifies aircraft behaviour when a collision is imminent

AVD_W_ACTION.d: Specifies aircraft behaviour when a collision may occur

AVD_F_RCVRY.d: Determines what the aircraft will do after a fail event is resolved
AVD_F_RCVRY.r: 0:Remain in AVOID_ADSB,1:Resume previous flight mode,2:RTL,3:Resume if AUTO else Loiter

AVD_OBS_MAX.d: Maximum number of obstacles to track

AVD_W_TIME.d: Aircraft velocity vectors are multiplied by this time to determine closest approach.  If this results in an approach closer than W_DIST_XY or W_DIST_Z then W_ACTION is undertaken (assuming F_ACTION is not undertaken)

AVD_F_TIME.d: Aircraft velocity vectors are multiplied by this time to determine closest approach.  If this results in an approach closer than F_DIST_XY or F_DIST_Z then F_ACTION is undertaken

AVD_W_DIST_XY.d: Closest allowed projected distance before W_ACTION is undertaken

AVD_F_DIST_XY.d: Closest allowed projected distance before F_ACTION is undertaken

AVD_W_DIST_Z.d: Closest allowed projected distance before BEHAVIOUR_W is undertaken

AVD_F_DIST_Z.d: Closest allowed projected distance before BEHAVIOUR_F is undertaken

AVD_F_ALT_MIN.d: Minimum AMSL (above mean sea level) altitude for ADS-B avoidance. If the vehicle is below this altitude, no avoidance action will take place. Useful to prevent ADS-B avoidance from activating while below the tree line or around structures. Default of 0 is no minimum.

AVOID_DIST_MAX.d: Distance from object at which obstacle avoidance will begin in non-GPS modes

AVOID_MARGIN.d: Vehicle will attempt to stay at least this distance (in meters) from objects while in GPS modes

AVOID_BACKUP_SPD.d: Maximum speed that will be used to back away from obstacles horizontally in position control modes (m/s). Set zero to disable horizontal backup.

AVOID_ALT_MIN.d: Minimum altitude above which proximity based avoidance will start working. This requires a valid downward facing rangefinder reading to work. Set zero to disable

AVOID_ACCEL_MAX.d: Maximum acceleration with which obstacles will be avoided with. Set zero to disable acceleration limits

AVOID_BACKUP_DZ.d: Distance beyond AVOID_MARGIN parameter, after which vehicle will backaway from obstacles. Increase this parameter if you see vehicle going back and forth in front of obstacle.

AVOID_BACKZ_SPD.d: Maximum speed that will be used to back away from obstacles vertically in height control modes (m/s). Set zero to disable vertical backup.

AVOID_ANG_MAX.d: Max lean angle used to avoid obstacles while in non-GPS modes.  Set to zero to disable lean-based avoidance

BARO_GND_TEMP.d: User provided ambient ground temperature in degrees Celsius. This is used to improve the calculation of the altitude the vehicle is at. This parameter is not persistent and will be reset to 0 every time the vehicle is rebooted. A value of 0 means use the internal measurement ambient temperature.

BARO_ALT_OFFSET.d: altitude offset in meters added to barometric altitude. This is used to allow for automatic adjustment of the base barometric altitude by a ground station equipped with a barometer. The value is added to the barometric altitude read by the aircraft. It is automatically reset to 0 when the barometer is calibrated on each reboot or when a preflight calibration is performed.

BARO_PRIMARY.d: This selects which barometer will be the primary if multiple barometers are found

BARO_EXT_BUS.d: This selects the bus number for looking for an I2C barometer. When set to -1 it will probe all external i2c buses based on the BARO_PROBE_EXT parameter.

BARO_FLTR_RNG.d: This sets the range around the average value that new samples must be within to be accepted. This can help reduce the impact of noise on sensors that are on long I2C cables. The value is a percentage from the average value. A value of zero disables this filter.

BARO_PROBE_EXT.d: This sets which types of external i2c barometer to look for. It is a bitmask of barometer types. The I2C buses to probe is based on BARO_EXT_BUS. If BARO_EXT_BUS is -1 then it will probe all external buses, otherwise it will probe just the bus number given in BARO_EXT_BUS.

BARO1_DEVID.d: Barometer sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

BARO2_DEVID.d: Barometer2 sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

BARO3_DEVID.d: Barometer3 sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

BARO_FIELD_ELV.d: User provided field elevation in meters. This is used to improve the calculation of the altitude the vehicle is at. This parameter is not persistent and will be reset to 0 every time the vehicle is rebooted. Changes to this parameter will only be used when disarmed. A value of 0 means the EKF origin height is used for takeoff height above sea level.

BARO_ALTERR_MAX.d: This is the maximum acceptable altitude discrepancy between GPS altitude and barometric presssure altitude calculated against a standard atmosphere for arming checks to pass. If you are getting an arming error due to this parameter then you may have a faulty or substituted barometer. A common issue is vendors replacing a MS5611 in a "Pixhawk" with a MS5607. If you have that issue then please see BARO_OPTIONS parameter to force the MS5611 to be treated as a MS5607. This check is disabled if the value is zero.

BARO1_THST_SCALE.d: Thrust scaling in Pascals. This value scaled by the normalized thrust is subtracted from the barometer pressure. This is used to adjust linearly based on the thrust output for local pressure difference induced by the props.

BARO_THST_FILT.d: Low-pass filter cutoff frequency for thrust compensation. This smooths the throttle input to prevent rapid baro altitude changes during throttle transients. Set to 0 to disable filtering.

BARO1_WCF_ENABLE.d: This enables the use of wind coefficients for barometer compensation

BARO1_WCF_FWD.d: This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a positive wind relative velocity along the X body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during forwards flight, then this will be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

BARO1_WCF_BCK.d: This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a negative wind relative velocity along the X body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during backwards flight, then this will be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

BARO1_WCF_RGT.d: This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a positive wind relative velocity along the Y body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during sideways flight to the right, then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

BARO1_WCF_LFT.d: This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a negative wind relative velocity along the Y body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during sideways flight to the left, then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

BARO1_WCF_UP.d: This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a positive wind relative velocity along the Z body axis. If the baro height estimate rises above truth height during climbing flight (or forward flight with a high forwards lean angle), then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

BARO1_WCF_DN.d: This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a negative wind relative velocity along the Z body axis. If the baro height estimate rises above truth height during descending flight (or forward flight with a high backwards lean angle, eg braking manoeuvre), then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.
