BATT2_MONITOR.d: Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current
BATT2_MONITOR.r: 0:Disabled,28:AD7091R5,31:Analog Current Only,3:Analog Voltage Only,4:Analog Voltage and Current,6:Bebop,8:DroneCAN-BatteryInfo,27:EFI,9:ESC,11:FuelFlow,24:FuelLevelAnalog,12:FuelLevelPWM,17:Generator-Elec,18:Generator-Fuel,26:INA239_SPI,21:INA2XX (INA226 INA228 INA238 INA231 INA260),30:INA3221,22:LTC2946,20:MPPT,15:NeoDesign,19:Rotoye,13:SMBUS-SUI3,14:SMBUS-SUI6,7:SMBus-Generic,16:SMBus-Maxell,29:Scripting,5:Solo,10:Sum Of Selected Monitors,25:Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage,32:TIBQ76952-I2C (Periph only),23:Torqeedo

BATT2_CAPACITY.d: Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

BATT2_SERIAL_NUM.d: Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.

BATT2_LOW_TIMER.d: This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

BATT2_FS_VOLTSRC.d: Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

BATT2_LOW_VOLT.d: Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT2_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT2_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATT2_LOW_MAH.d: Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT2_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATT2_CRT_VOLT.d: Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT2_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT2_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT2_CRT_MAH.d: Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT2_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT2_FS_LOW_ACT.d: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe
BATT2_FS_LOW_ACT.r: 0:Warn only,1:Land,2:RTL,3:SmartRTL or RTL,4:SmartRTL or Land,5:Terminate,6:Auto DO_LAND_START/DO_RETURN_PATH_START or RTL,7:Brake or Land

BATT2_FS_CRT_ACT.d: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe
BATT2_FS_CRT_ACT.r: 0:Warn only,1:Land,2:RTL,3:SmartRTL or RTL,4:SmartRTL or Land,5:Terminate,6:Auto DO_LAND_START/DO_RETURN_PATH_START or RTL,7:Brake or Land

BATT2_ARM_VOLT.d: Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

BATT2_ARM_MAH.d: Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATT2_ARM_VOLT parameter.

BATT2_OPTIONS.d: This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

bitmask.label: MPPT reports input voltage and current
Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
Battery is for internal autopilot use only
Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average
Allow DroneCAN dynamic node update on hot-swap

BATT2_ESC_INDEX.d: ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

BATT2_VOLT_PIN.d: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATT2_CURR_PIN.d: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for Current monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATT2_VOLT_MULT.d: Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATT2_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT).

BATT2_AMP_PERVLT.d: Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to.

BATT2_AMP_OFFSET.d: Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor

BATT2_VLT_OFFSET.d: Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied

BATT2_I2C_ADDR.d: Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses

BATT2_SUM_MASK.d: 0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

BATT2_CURR_MULT.d: Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

BATT2_FL_VLT_MIN.d: The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT2_FL_V_MULT.d: Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT2_FL_FLTR.d: Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

BATT2_FL_PIN.d: Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

BATT2_MAX_VOLT.d: Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage

BATT2_MAX_AMPS.d: This controls the maximum current the INA239 sensor will work with.

BATT2_SHUNT.d: This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

BATT2_ESC_MASK.d: If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

BATT2_CHANNEL.d: INA3221 channel to return data for

BATT5_MONITOR.d: Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current
BATT5_MONITOR.r: 0:Disabled,28:AD7091R5,31:Analog Current Only,3:Analog Voltage Only,4:Analog Voltage and Current,6:Bebop,8:DroneCAN-BatteryInfo,27:EFI,9:ESC,11:FuelFlow,24:FuelLevelAnalog,12:FuelLevelPWM,17:Generator-Elec,18:Generator-Fuel,26:INA239_SPI,21:INA2XX (INA226 INA228 INA238 INA231 INA260),30:INA3221,22:LTC2946,20:MPPT,15:NeoDesign,19:Rotoye,13:SMBUS-SUI3,14:SMBUS-SUI6,7:SMBus-Generic,16:SMBus-Maxell,29:Scripting,5:Solo,10:Sum Of Selected Monitors,25:Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage,32:TIBQ76952-I2C (Periph only),23:Torqeedo

BATT5_CAPACITY.d: Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

BATT5_SERIAL_NUM.d: Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.

BATT5_LOW_TIMER.d: This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

BATT5_FS_VOLTSRC.d: Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

BATT5_LOW_VOLT.d: Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT5_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT5_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATT5_LOW_MAH.d: Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT5_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATT5_CRT_VOLT.d: Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT5_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT5_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT5_CRT_MAH.d: Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT5_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT5_FS_LOW_ACT.d: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe
BATT5_FS_LOW_ACT.r: 0:Warn only,1:Land,2:RTL,3:SmartRTL or RTL,4:SmartRTL or Land,5:Terminate,6:Auto DO_LAND_START/DO_RETURN_PATH_START or RTL,7:Brake or Land

BATT5_FS_CRT_ACT.d: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe
BATT5_FS_CRT_ACT.r: 0:Warn only,1:Land,2:RTL,3:SmartRTL or RTL,4:SmartRTL or Land,5:Terminate,6:Auto DO_LAND_START/DO_RETURN_PATH_START or RTL,7:Brake or Land

BATT5_ARM_VOLT.d: Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

BATT5_ARM_MAH.d: Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATT5_ARM_VOLT parameter.

BATT5_OPTIONS.d: This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

bitmask.label: MPPT reports input voltage and current
Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
Battery is for internal autopilot use only
Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average
Allow DroneCAN dynamic node update on hot-swap

BATT5_ESC_INDEX.d: ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

BATT5_VOLT_PIN.d: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATT5_CURR_PIN.d: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for Current monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATT5_VOLT_MULT.d: Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATT5_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT).

BATT5_AMP_PERVLT.d: Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to.

BATT5_AMP_OFFSET.d: Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor

BATT5_VLT_OFFSET.d: Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied

BATT5_I2C_ADDR.d: Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses

BATT5_SUM_MASK.d: 0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

BATT5_CURR_MULT.d: Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

BATT5_FL_VLT_MIN.d: The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT5_FL_V_MULT.d: Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT5_FL_FLTR.d: Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

BATT5_FL_PIN.d: Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

BATT5_MAX_VOLT.d: Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage

BATT5_MAX_AMPS.d: This controls the maximum current the INA239 sensor will work with.

BATT5_SHUNT.d: This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

BATT5_ESC_MASK.d: If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

BATT5_CHANNEL.d: INA3221 channel to return data for

BATT9_CRT_VOLT.d: Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT9_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT9_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT9_CRT_MAH.d: Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT9_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT9_FS_LOW_ACT.d: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe
BATT9_FS_LOW_ACT.r: 0:Warn only,1:Land,2:RTL,3:SmartRTL or RTL,4:SmartRTL or Land,5:Terminate,6:Auto DO_LAND_START/DO_RETURN_PATH_START or RTL,7:Brake or Land

BATT9_FS_CRT_ACT.d: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe
BATT9_FS_CRT_ACT.r: 0:Warn only,1:Land,2:RTL,3:SmartRTL or RTL,4:SmartRTL or Land,5:Terminate,6:Auto DO_LAND_START/DO_RETURN_PATH_START or RTL,7:Brake or Land

BATT9_ARM_VOLT.d: Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

BATT9_ARM_MAH.d: Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATT9_ARM_VOLT parameter.

BATT9_OPTIONS.d: This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

bitmask.label: MPPT reports input voltage and current
Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
Battery is for internal autopilot use only
Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average
Allow DroneCAN dynamic node update on hot-swap

BATT9_ESC_INDEX.d: ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

BATT9_VOLT_PIN.d: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATT9_CURR_PIN.d: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for Current monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATT9_VOLT_MULT.d: Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATT9_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT).

BATT9_AMP_PERVLT.d: Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to.

BATT9_AMP_OFFSET.d: Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor

BATT9_VLT_OFFSET.d: Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied

BATT9_I2C_ADDR.d: Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses

BATT9_SUM_MASK.d: 0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

BATT9_CURR_MULT.d: Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

BATT9_FL_VLT_MIN.d: The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT9_FL_V_MULT.d: Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT9_FL_FLTR.d: Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

BATT9_FL_PIN.d: Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

BATT9_MAX_VOLT.d: Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage

BATT9_MAX_AMPS.d: This controls the maximum current the INA239 sensor will work with.

BATT9_SHUNT.d: This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

BATT9_ESC_MASK.d: If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

BATT9_CHANNEL.d: INA3221 channel to return data for

bitmask.label: MPPT reports input voltage and current
Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
Battery is for internal autopilot use only
Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average
Allow DroneCAN dynamic node update on hot-swap

BATTD_ESC_INDEX.d: ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

BATTD_VOLT_PIN.d: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATTD_CURR_PIN.d: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for Current monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATTD_VOLT_MULT.d: Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATTD_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT).

BATTD_AMP_PERVLT.d: Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to.

BATTD_AMP_OFFSET.d: Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor

BATTD_VLT_OFFSET.d: Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied

BATTD_I2C_ADDR.d: Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses

BATTD_SUM_MASK.d: 0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

BATTD_CURR_MULT.d: Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

BATTD_FL_VLT_MIN.d: The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATTD_FL_V_MULT.d: Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATTD_FL_FLTR.d: Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

BATTD_FL_PIN.d: Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

BATTD_MAX_VOLT.d: Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage

BATTD_MAX_AMPS.d: This controls the maximum current the INA239 sensor will work with.

BATTD_SHUNT.d: This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

BATTD_ESC_MASK.d: If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

BATTD_CHANNEL.d: INA3221 channel to return data for

BATTE_MONITOR.d: Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current
BATTE_MONITOR.r: 0:Disabled,28:AD7091R5,31:Analog Current Only,3:Analog Voltage Only,4:Analog Voltage and Current,6:Bebop,8:DroneCAN-BatteryInfo,27:EFI,9:ESC,11:FuelFlow,24:FuelLevelAnalog,12:FuelLevelPWM,17:Generator-Elec,18:Generator-Fuel,26:INA239_SPI,21:INA2XX (INA226 INA228 INA238 INA231 INA260),30:INA3221,22:LTC2946,20:MPPT,15:NeoDesign,19:Rotoye,13:SMBUS-SUI3,14:SMBUS-SUI6,7:SMBus-Generic,16:SMBus-Maxell,29:Scripting,5:Solo,10:Sum Of Selected Monitors,25:Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage,32:TIBQ76952-I2C (Periph only),23:Torqeedo

BATTE_CAPACITY.d: Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

BATTE_SERIAL_NUM.d: Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.

BATTE_LOW_TIMER.d: This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

BATTE_FS_VOLTSRC.d: Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

BATTE_LOW_VOLT.d: Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTE_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTE_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATTE_LOW_MAH.d: Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTE_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATTE_CRT_VOLT.d: Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTE_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTE_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATTE_CRT_MAH.d: Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTE_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATTE_FS_LOW_ACT.d: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe
BATTE_FS_LOW_ACT.r: 0:Warn only,1:Land,2:RTL,3:SmartRTL or RTL,4:SmartRTL or Land,5:Terminate,6:Auto DO_LAND_START/DO_RETURN_PATH_START or RTL,7:Brake or Land

BATTE_FS_CRT_ACT.d: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe
BATTE_FS_CRT_ACT.r: 0:Warn only,1:Land,2:RTL,3:SmartRTL or RTL,4:SmartRTL or Land,5:Terminate,6:Auto DO_LAND_START/DO_RETURN_PATH_START or RTL,7:Brake or Land

BATTE_ARM_VOLT.d: Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

BATTE_ARM_MAH.d: Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATTE_ARM_VOLT parameter.

BATT_FL_PIN.d: Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

BATT_MAX_VOLT.d: Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage

BATT_MAX_AMPS.d: This controls the maximum current the INA239 sensor will work with.

BATT_SHUNT.d: This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

BATT_ESC_MASK.d: If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

BATT_CHANNEL.d: INA3221 channel to return data for

BCN_TYPE.d: What type of beacon based position estimation device is connected

BCN_ALT.d: Beacon origin's altitude above sealevel in meters

BCN_ORIENT_YAW.d: Beacon systems rotation from north in degrees

BRD_SER1_RTSCTS.d: Enable flow control on serial 1 (telemetry 1). You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup. Note that the PX4v1 does not have hardware flow control pins on this port, so you should leave this disabled.

BRD_SER2_RTSCTS.d: Enable flow control on serial 2 (telemetry 2). You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SER3_RTSCTS.d: Enable flow control on serial 3. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SER4_RTSCTS.d: Enable flow control on serial 4. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SER5_RTSCTS.d: Enable flow control on serial 5. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SER6_RTSCTS.d: Enable flow control on serial 6. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SER7_RTSCTS.d: Enable flow control on serial 7. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SER8_RTSCTS.d: Enable flow control on serial 8. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SAFETY_DEFLT.d: This controls the default state of the safety switch at startup. When set to 1 the safety switch will start in the safe state (flashing) at boot. When set to zero the safety switch will start in the unsafe state (solid) at startup. Note that if a safety switch is fitted the user can still control the safety state after startup using the switch. The safety state can also be controlled in software using a MAVLink message.

BRD_SBUS_OUT.d: This sets the SBUS output frame rate in Hz

BRD_SERIAL_NUM.d: User-defined serial number of this vehicle, it can be any arbitrary number you want and has no effect on the autopilot

BRD_SAFETY_MASK.d: A bitmask which controls what outputs can move while the safety switch has not been pressed

BRD_HEAT_TARG.d: Board heater target temperature for boards with controllable heating units. Set to -1 to disable the heater, please reboot after setting to -1.

BRD_TYPE.d: This allows selection of a PX4 or VRBRAIN board type. If set to zero then the board type is auto-detected (PX4)

BRD_IO_ENABLE.d: This allows for the IO co-processor on boards with an IOMCU to be disabled. Setting to 2 will enable the IOMCU but not attempt to update firmware on startup

BRD_SAFETYOPTION.d: This controls the activation of the safety button. It allows you to control if the safety button can be used for safety enable and/or disable, and whether the button is only active when disarmed

bitmask.label: Force safety on when the aircraft disarms

BRD_VBUS_MIN.d: Minimum voltage on the autopilot power rail to allow the aircraft to arm. 0 to disable the check.

BRD_VSERVO_MIN.d: Minimum voltage on the servo rail to allow the aircraft to arm. 0 to disable the check.

BRD_SD_SLOWDOWN.d: This is a scaling factor to slow down microSD operation. It can be used on flight board and microSD card combinations where full speed is not reliable. For normal full speed operation a value of 0 should be used.

BRD_PWM_VOLT_SEL.d: This sets the voltage max for PWM output pulses. 0 for 3.3V and 1 for 5V output. On boards with an IOMCU that support this parameter this option only affects the 8 main outputs, not the 6 auxiliary outputs. Using 5V output can help to reduce the impact of ESC noise interference corrupting signals to the ESCs.

bitmask.label: Write protect firmware flash on reboot
Write protect bootloader flash on reboot
Disable board arming gpio output change on arm/disarm

BRD_BOOT_DELAY.d: This adds a delay in milliseconds to boot to ensure peripherals initialise fully

BRD_ALT_CONFIG.d: Select an alternative hardware configuration. A value of zero selects the default configuration for this board. Other values are board specific. Please see the documentation for your board for details on any alternative configuration values that may be available.

BRD_HEAT_LOWMGN.d: Arming check will fail if temp is lower than this margin below BRD_HEAT_TARG. 0 disables the low temperature check

BRD_SD_MISSION.d: This sets the amount of storage in kilobytes reserved on the microsd card in mission.stg for waypoint storage. Each waypoint uses 15 bytes.

BRD_SD_FENCE.d: This sets the amount of storage in kilobytes reserved on the microsd card in fence.stg for fence storage.

BRD_IO_DSHOT.d: This loads the DShot firmware on the IO co-processor

BRD_IDLE_STATS.d: Capture and calculate true CPU load using idle threads

BRD_RADIO_TYPE.d: This enables support for direct attached radio receivers

BRD_RADIO_SIGCH.d: Channel to show receive RSSI signal strength, or zero for disabled

BRD_RADIO_PPSCH.d: Channel to show received packet-per-second rate, or zero for disabled

BRD_RADIO_TELEM.d: If this is non-zero then telemetry packets will be sent over DSM

BRD_RADIO_TXPOW.d: Set telemetry transmit power. This is the power level (from 1 to 8) for telemetry packets sent from the RX to the TX

BRD_RADIO_FCCTST.d: If this is enabled then the radio will continuously transmit as required for FCC testing. The transmit channel is set by the value of the parameter. The radio will not work for RC input while this is enabled

BRD_RADIO_STKMD.d: This selects between different stick input modes. The default is mode2, which has throttle on the left stick and pitch on the right stick. You can instead set mode1, which has throttle on the right stick and pitch on the left stick.

BRD_RADIO_TESTCH.d: This sets the radio to a fixed test channel for factory testing. Using a fixed channel avoids the need for binding in factory testing.

BRD_RADIO_TSIGCH.d: Channel to show telemetry RSSI value as received by TX

BRD_RADIO_TPPSCH.d: Channel to show telemetry packets-per-second value, as received at TX

BRD_RADIO_TXMAX.d: Set transmitter maximum transmit power (from 1 to 8)

BRD_RADIO_BZOFS.d: Set transmitter buzzer note adjustment (adjust frequency up)

BRD_RADIO_ABTIME.d: When non-zero this sets the time with no transmitter packets before we start looking for auto-bind packets.

BRD_RADIO_ABLVL.d: This sets the minimum RSSI of an auto-bind packet for it to be accepted. This should be set so that auto-bind will only happen at short range to minimise the change of an auto-bind happening accidentially

BRD_RTC_TYPES.d: Specifies which sources of UTC time will be accepted

BRD_RTC_TZ_MIN.d: Adds offset in +- minutes from UTC to calculate local time

BTN_ENABLE.d: This enables the button checking module. When this is disabled the parameters for setting button inputs are not visible

BTN_PIN1.d: Digital pin number for first button input.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

BTN_PIN2.d: Digital pin number for second button input.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

BTN_PIN3.d: Digital pin number for third button input.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

BTN_PIN4.d: Digital pin number for fourth button input. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

BTN_REPORT_SEND.d: The duration in seconds that a BUTTON_CHANGE report is repeatedly sent to the GCS regarding a button changing state. Note that the BUTTON_CHANGE message is MAVLink2 only.

BTN_OPTIONS1.d: Options for Pin 1. PWM input detects PWM above or below 1800/1200us instead of logic level. If PWM is not detected or is less than 800us or above 2200us the button will interpreted as low. Invert changes HIGH state to be logic low voltage on pin, or below 1200us, if PWM input.

BTN_OPTIONS2.d: Options for Pin 2. PWM input detects PWM above or below 1800/1200us instead of logic level. If PWM is not detected or is less than 800us or above 2200us the button will interpreted as low. Invert changes HIGH state to be logic low voltage on pin, or below 1200us, if PWM input.

BTN_OPTIONS3.d: Options for Pin 3. PWM input detects PWM above or below 1800/1200us instead of logic level. If PWM is not detected or is less than 800us or above 2200us the button will interpreted as low. Invert changes HIGH state to be logic low voltage on pin, or below 1200us, if PWM input.

BTN_OPTIONS4.d: Options for Pin 4. PWM input detects PWM above or below 1800/1200us instead of logic level. If PWM is not detected or is less than 800us or above 2200us the button will interpreted as low. Invert changes HIGH state to be logic low voltage on pin, or below 1200us, if PWM input.

CAM_MAX_ROLL.d: Postpone shooting if roll is greater than limit. (0=Disable, will shoot regardless of roll).

CAM_AUTO_ONLY.d: When enabled, trigging by distance is done in AUTO mode only.

CAM1_TYPE.d: how to trigger the camera to take a picture

CAM1_DURATION.d: Duration in seconds that the camera shutter is held open

CAM1_SERVO_ON.d: PWM value in microseconds to move servo to when shutter is activated

CAM1_SERVO_OFF.d: PWM value in microseconds to move servo to when shutter is deactivated

CAM1_TRIGG_DIST.d: Distance in meters between camera triggers. If this value is non-zero then the camera will trigger whenever the position changes by this number of meters regardless of what mode the APM is in. Note that this parameter can also be set in an auto mission using the DO_SET_CAM_TRIGG_DIST command, allowing you to enable/disable the triggering of the camera during the flight.

CAM1_RELAY_ON.d: This sets whether the relay goes high or low when it triggers. Note that you should also set RELAY_DEFAULT appropriately for your camera

CAM1_INTRVAL_MIN.d: Postpone shooting if previous picture was taken less than this many seconds ago

CAM1_FEEDBAK_PIN.d: pin number to use for save accurate camera feedback messages. If set to -1 then don't use a pin flag for this, otherwise this is a pin number which if held high after a picture trigger order, will save camera messages when camera really takes a picture. A universal camera hot shoe is needed. The pin should be held high for at least 2 milliseconds for reliable trigger detection.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot. See also the CAMx_FEEDBCK_POL option.

CAM1_FEEDBAK_POL.d: Polarity for feedback pin. If this is 1 then the feedback pin should go high on trigger. If set to 0 then it should go low

bitmask.label: Recording Starts at arming and stops at disarming

CAM1_MNT_INST.d: Mount instance camera is associated with. 0 means camera and mount have identical instance numbers e.g. camera1 and mount1

CAM1_HFOV.d: Camera horizontal field of view. 0 if unknown

CAM1_VFOV.d: Camera vertical field of view. 0 if unknown

CAM1_RC_TYPE.d: RunCam device type used to determine OSD menu structure and shutter options.
CAM1_RC_TYPE.r: 0:Disabled,1:RunCam Split Micro/RunCam with UART,2:RunCam Split,3:RunCam Split4 4k,4:RunCam Hybrid/RunCam Thumb Pro,5:Runcam 2 4k

CAM1_RC_FEATURES.d: The available features of the attached RunCam device. If 0 then the RunCam device will be queried for the features it supports, otherwise this setting is used.

CAM1_RC_BT_DELAY.d: Time it takes for the RunCam to become fully ready in ms. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.

CAM1_RC_BTN_DELY.d: Time it takes for the a RunCam button press to be actived in ms. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.

CAM1_RC_MDE_DELY.d: Time it takes for the a RunCam mode button press to be actived in ms. If a mode change first requires a video recording change then double this value is used. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.

CAM1_RC_CONTROL.d: Specifies the allowed actions required to enter the OSD menu and other option like autorecording

CAM2_TYPE.d: how to trigger the camera to take a picture

CAM2_DURATION.d: Duration in seconds that the camera shutter is held open

CAM2_SERVO_ON.d: PWM value in microseconds to move servo to when shutter is activated

CAM2_SERVO_OFF.d: PWM value in microseconds to move servo to when shutter is deactivated

CAM2_TRIGG_DIST.d: Distance in meters between camera triggers. If this value is non-zero then the camera will trigger whenever the position changes by this number of meters regardless of what mode the APM is in. Note that this parameter can also be set in an auto mission using the DO_SET_CAM_TRIGG_DIST command, allowing you to enable/disable the triggering of the camera during the flight.

CAM2_RELAY_ON.d: This sets whether the relay goes high or low when it triggers. Note that you should also set RELAY_DEFAULT appropriately for your camera

CAM2_INTRVAL_MIN.d: Postpone shooting if previous picture was taken less than this many seconds ago

CAM2_FEEDBAK_PIN.d: pin number to use for save accurate camera feedback messages. If set to -1 then don't use a pin flag for this, otherwise this is a pin number which if held high after a picture trigger order, will save camera messages when camera really takes a picture. A universal camera hot shoe is needed. The pin should be held high for at least 2 milliseconds for reliable trigger detection.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot. See also the CAMx_FEEDBCK_POL option.

CAM2_FEEDBAK_POL.d: Polarity for feedback pin. If this is 1 then the feedback pin should go high on trigger. If set to 0 then it should go low

bitmask.label: Recording Starts at arming and stops at disarming

CAM2_MNT_INST.d: Mount instance camera is associated with. 0 means camera and mount have identical instance numbers e.g. camera1 and mount1

CAM2_HFOV.d: Camera horizontal field of view. 0 if unknown

CAM2_VFOV.d: Camera vertical field of view. 0 if unknown

CAM2_RC_TYPE.d: RunCam device type used to determine OSD menu structure and shutter options.
CAM2_RC_TYPE.r: 0:Disabled,1:RunCam Split Micro/RunCam with UART,2:RunCam Split,3:RunCam Split4 4k,4:RunCam Hybrid/RunCam Thumb Pro,5:Runcam 2 4k

CAM2_RC_FEATURES.d: The available features of the attached RunCam device. If 0 then the RunCam device will be queried for the features it supports, otherwise this setting is used.

CAM2_RC_BT_DELAY.d: Time it takes for the RunCam to become fully ready in ms. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.

CAM2_RC_BTN_DELY.d: Time it takes for the a RunCam button press to be actived in ms. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.

CAM2_RC_MDE_DELY.d: Time it takes for the a RunCam mode button press to be actived in ms. If a mode change first requires a video recording change then double this value is used. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.

CAM2_RC_CONTROL.d: Specifies the allowed actions required to enter the OSD menu and other option like autorecording

CAN_LOGLEVEL.d: Loglevel for recording initialisation and debug information from CAN Interface

CAN_D1_PROTOCOL.d: Enabling this option starts selected protocol that will use this virtual driver

CAN_D1_PROTOCOL2.d: Secondary protocol with 11 bit CAN addressing

CAN_D1_PC_ESC_BM.d: Bitmask defining which ESC (motor) channels are to be transmitted over Piccolo CAN

CAN_D1_PC_ESC_RT.d: Output rate of ESC command messages

CAN_D1_PC_SRV_BM.d: Bitmask defining which servo channels are to be transmitted over Piccolo CAN

CAN_D1_PC_SRV_RT.d: Output rate of servo command messages

CAN_D1_PC_ECU_ID.d: Node ID to send ECU throttle messages to. Set to zero to disable ECU throttle messages. Set to 255 to broadcast to all ECUs.

CAN_D1_PC_ECU_RT.d: Output rate of ECU command messages

CAN_D1_UC_NODE.d: DroneCAN node ID used by the driver itself on this network

CAN_D1_UC_SRV_BM.d: Bitmask with one set for channel to be transmitted as a servo command over DroneCAN

CAN_D1_UC_ESC_BM.d: Bitmask with one set for channel to be transmitted as a ESC command over DroneCAN

CAN_D1_UC_SRV_RT.d: Maximum transmit rate for servo outputs

CAN_D1_UC_NTF_RT.d: Maximum transmit rate for Notify State Message

CAN_D1_UC_ESC_OF.d: Offset for ESC numbering in DroneCAN ESC RawCommand messages. This allows for more efficient packing of ESC command messages. If your ESCs are on servo outputs 5 to 8 and you set this parameter to 4 then the ESC RawCommand will be sent with the first 4 slots filled. This can be used for more efficient usage of CAN bandwidth

CAN_D1_UC_POOL.d: Amount of memory in bytes to allocate for the DroneCAN memory pool. More memory is needed for higher CAN bus loads

CAN_D1_UC_ESC_RV.d: Bitmask with one set for each output channel that uses a reversible ESC over DroneCAN. Reversible ESCs use both positive and negative values in RawCommands, with positive commanding the forward direction and negative commanding the reverse direction.

CAN_D1_UC_RLY_RT.d: Maximum transmit rate for relay outputs, note that this rate is per message each message does 1 relay, so if with more relays will take longer to update at the same rate, a extra message will be sent when a relay changes state

CAN_D1_UC_S1_NOD.d: CAN remote node number for serial port

CAN_D1_UC_S1_IDX.d: Serial port number on remote CAN node

CAN_D1_UC_S1_BD.d: Serial baud rate on remote CAN node

CAN_P1_DRIVER.d: Enabling this option enables use of CAN buses.

CAN_P1_BITRATE.d: Bit rate can be set up to from 10000 to 1000000

CAN_P1_FDBITRATE.d: Bit rate can be set up to from 1000000 to 8000000

CAN_P3_FDBITRATE.d: Bit rate can be set up to from 1000000 to 8000000

CAN_SLCAN_CPORT.d: CAN Interface ID to be routed to SLCAN, 0 means no routing

CAN_SLCAN_SERNUM.d: Serial Port ID to be used for temporary SLCAN iface, -1 means no temporary serial. This parameter is automatically reset on reboot or on timeout. See CAN_SLCAN_TIMOUT for timeout details

CAN_SLCAN_TIMOUT.d: Duration of inactivity after which SLCAN is switched back to original driver in seconds.

CAN_SLCAN_SDELAY.d: Duration after which slcan starts after setting SERNUM in seconds.

CC_TYPE.d: Custom control type to be used

CC_AXIS_MASK.d: Custom Controller bitmask to chose which axis to run

CC2_ANG_RLL_P.d: Roll axis angle controller P gain.  Converts the error between the desired roll angle and actual angle to a desired roll rate

CC2_ANG_PIT_P.d: Pitch axis angle controller P gain.  Converts the error between the desired pitch angle and actual angle to a desired pitch rate

CC2_ANG_YAW_P.d: Yaw axis angle controller P gain.  Converts the error between the desired yaw angle and actual angle to a desired yaw rate

CC2_RAT_RLL_P.d: Roll axis rate controller P gain. Corrects in proportion to the difference between the desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

CC2_RAT_RLL_I.d: Roll axis rate controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

CC2_RAT_RLL_IMAX.d: Roll axis rate controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum that the I term will output

CC2_RAT_RLL_D.d: Roll axis rate controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

CC2_RAT_RLL_FF.d: Roll axis rate controller feed forward

CC2_RAT_RLL_FLTT.d: Roll axis rate controller target frequency in Hz

CC2_RAT_RLL_FLTE.d: Roll axis rate controller error frequency in Hz

CC2_RAT_RLL_FLTD.d: Roll axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz

CC2_RAT_RLL_SMAX.d: Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

CC2_RAT_RLL_PDMX.d: Roll axis rate controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

CC2_RAT_RLL_D_FF.d: FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

CC2_RAT_PIT_P.d: Pitch axis rate controller P gain.  Corrects in proportion to the difference between the desired pitch rate vs actual pitch rate

CC2_RAT_PIT_I.d: Pitch axis rate controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired pitch rate vs actual pitch rate

CC2_RAT_PIT_IMAX.d: Pitch axis rate controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum that the I term will output

CC2_RAT_PIT_D.d: Pitch axis rate controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired pitch rate vs actual pitch rate

CC2_RAT_PIT_FF.d: Pitch axis rate controller feed forward

CC2_RAT_PIT_FLTT.d: Pitch axis rate controller target frequency in Hz

CC2_RAT_PIT_FLTE.d: Pitch axis rate controller error frequency in Hz

CC2_RAT_PIT_FLTD.d: Pitch axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz

CC2_RAT_PIT_SMAX.d: Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

CC2_RAT_PIT_PDMX.d: Pitch axis rate controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

CC2_RAT_PIT_D_FF.d: FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

CC2_RAT_YAW_P.d: Yaw axis rate controller P gain.  Corrects in proportion to the difference between the desired yaw rate vs actual yaw rate

CC2_RAT_YAW_I.d: Yaw axis rate controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired yaw rate vs actual yaw rate

CC2_RAT_YAW_IMAX.d: Yaw axis rate controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum that the I term will output

CC2_RAT_YAW_D.d: Yaw axis rate controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired yaw rate vs actual yaw rate

CC2_RAT_YAW_FF.d: Yaw axis rate controller feed forward

CC2_RAT_YAW_FLTT.d: Yaw axis rate controller target frequency in Hz

CC2_RAT_YAW_FLTE.d: Yaw axis rate controller error frequency in Hz

CC2_RAT_YAW_FLTD.d: Yaw axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz

CC2_RAT_YAW_SMAX.d: Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

CC2_RAT_YAW_PDMX.d: Yaw axis rate controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

CC2_RAT_YAW_D_FF.d: FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

CHUTE_TYPE.d: Parachute release mechanism type (relay number in versions prior to 4.5, or servo). Values 0-3 all are relay. Relay number used for release is set by RELAYx_FUNCTION in 4.5 or later.

CHUTE_SERVO_ON.d: Parachute Servo PWM value in microseconds when parachute is released

CHUTE_SERVO_OFF.d: Parachute Servo PWM value in microseconds when parachute is not released

CHUTE_ALT_MIN.d: Parachute min altitude above home.  Parachute will not be released below this altitude.  0 to disable alt check.

CHUTE_DELAY_MS.d: Delay in millseconds between motor stop and chute release

CHUTE_CRT_SINK.d: Release parachute when critical sink rate is reached

CIRCLE_RATE.d: Circle mode's maximum turn rate in deg/sec.  Positive to turn clockwise, negative for counter clockwise. Final circle rate is also limited by speed and acceleration settings.

CIRCLE_OPTIONS.d: 0:Enable or disable using the pitch/roll stick control circle mode's radius and rate

bitmask.label: Start at center rather than on perimeter
Make Mount ROI the center of the circle

CIRCLE_RADIUS_M.d: Radius of the circle the vehicle will fly when in Circle flight mode

COMPASS_OFS_X.d: Offset to be added to the compass x-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS_Y.d: Offset to be added to the compass y-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS_Z.d: Offset to be added to the compass z-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_DEC.d: An angle to compensate between the true north and magnetic north

COMPASS_LEARN.d: Enable or disable the automatic learning of compass offsets. You can enable learning either using a compass-only method that is suitable only for fixed wing aircraft or using the offsets learnt by the active EKF state estimator. If this option is enabled then the learnt offsets are saved when you disarm the vehicle. If InFlight learning is enabled then the compass with automatically start learning once a flight starts (must be armed). While InFlight learning is running you cannot use position control modes.

COMPASS_USE.d: Enable or disable the use of the compass (instead of the GPS) for determining heading

COMPASS_AUTODEC.d: Enable or disable the automatic calculation of the declination based on gps location

COMPASS_MOTCT.d: Set motor interference compensation type to disabled, throttle or current.  Do not change manually.

COMPASS_MOT_X.d: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to the compass's x-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT_Y.d: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to the compass's y-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT_Z.d: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to the compass's z-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_ORIENT.d: The orientation of the first external compass relative to the vehicle frame. This value will be ignored unless this compass is set as an external compass. When set correctly in the northern hemisphere, pointing the nose and right side down should increase the MagX and MagY values respectively. Rolling the vehicle upside down should decrease the MagZ value. For southern hemisphere, switch increase and decrease. NOTE: For internal compasses, AHRS_ORIENT is used. The label for each option is specified in the order of rotations for that orientation. Firmware versions 4.2 and prior can use a CUSTOM (100) rotation to set the COMPASS_CUS_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles for Compass orientation. Later versions provide two general custom rotations which can be used, Custom 1 and Custom 2, with CUST_1_ROLL/PIT/YAW or CUST_2_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles.

COMPASS_EXTERNAL.d: Configure compass so it is attached externally. This is auto-detected on most boards. Set to 1 if the compass is externally connected. When externally connected the COMPASS_ORIENT option operates independently of the AHRS_ORIENTATION board orientation option. If set to 0 or 1 then auto-detection by bus connection can override the value. If set to 2 then auto-detection will be disabled.

COMPASS_OFS2_X.d: Offset to be added to compass2's x-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS2_Y.d: Offset to be added to compass2's y-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS2_Z.d: Offset to be added to compass2's z-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_MOT2_X.d: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass2's x-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT2_Y.d: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass2's y-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT2_Z.d: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass2's z-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_OFS3_X.d: Offset to be added to compass3's x-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS3_Y.d: Offset to be added to compass3's y-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS3_Z.d: Offset to be added to compass3's z-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_MOT3_X.d: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass3's x-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT3_Y.d: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass3's y-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT3_Z.d: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass3's z-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_DEV_ID.d: Compass device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID2.d: Second compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID3.d: Third compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_USE2.d: Enable or disable the secondary compass for determining heading.

COMPASS_ORIENT2.d: The orientation of a second external compass relative to the vehicle frame. This value will be ignored unless this compass is set as an external compass. When set correctly in the northern hemisphere, pointing the nose and right side down should increase the MagX and MagY values respectively. Rolling the vehicle upside down should decrease the MagZ value. For southern hemisphere, switch increase and decrease. NOTE: For internal compasses, AHRS_ORIENT is used. The label for each option is specified in the order of rotations for that orientation. Firmware versions 4.2 and prior can use a CUSTOM (100) rotation to set the COMPASS_CUS_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles for Compass orientation. Later versions provide two general custom rotations which can be used, Custom 1 and Custom 2, with CUST_1_ROLL/PIT/YAW or CUST_2_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles.

COMPASS_EXTERN2.d: Configure second compass so it is attached externally. This is auto-detected on most boards. If set to 0 or 1 then auto-detection by bus connection can override the value. If set to 2 then auto-detection will be disabled.

COMPASS_USE3.d: Enable or disable the tertiary compass for determining heading.

COMPASS_ORIENT3.d: The orientation of a third external compass relative to the vehicle frame. This value will be ignored unless this compass is set as an external compass. When set correctly in the northern hemisphere, pointing the nose and right side down should increase the MagX and MagY values respectively. Rolling the vehicle upside down should decrease the MagZ value. For southern hemisphere, switch increase and decrease. NOTE: For internal compasses, AHRS_ORIENT is used. The label for each option is specified in the order of rotations for that orientation. Firmware versions 4.2 and prior can use a CUSTOM (100) rotation to set the COMPASS_CUS_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles for Compass orientation. Later versions provide two general custom rotations which can be used, Custom 1 and Custom 2, with CUST_1_ROLL/PIT/YAW or CUST_2_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles.

COMPASS_EXTERN3.d: Configure third compass so it is attached externally. This is auto-detected on most boards. If set to 0 or 1 then auto-detection by bus connection can override the value. If set to 2 then auto-detection will be disabled.

COMPASS_DIA_X.d: DIA_X in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA_Y.d: DIA_Y in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA_Z.d: DIA_Z in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI_X.d: ODI_X in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI_Y.d: ODI_Y in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI_Z.d: ODI_Z in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA2_X.d: DIA_X in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA2_Y.d: DIA_Y in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA2_Z.d: DIA_Z in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI2_X.d: ODI_X in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI2_Y.d: ODI_Y in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI2_Z.d: ODI_Z in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA3_X.d: DIA_X in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA3_Y.d: DIA_Y in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA3_Z.d: DIA_Z in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI3_X.d: ODI_X in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI3_Y.d: ODI_Y in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI3_Z.d: ODI_Z in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_CAL_FIT.d: This controls the fitness level required for a successful compass calibration. A lower value makes for a stricter fit (less likely to pass). This is the value used for the primary magnetometer. Other magnetometers get double the value.

COMPASS_OFFS_MAX.d: This sets the maximum allowed compass offset in calibration and arming checks

COMPASS_DISBLMSK.d: This is a bitmask of driver types to disable. If a driver type is set in this mask then that driver will not try to find a sensor at startup

COMPASS_FLTR_RNG.d: This sets the range around the average value that new samples must be within to be accepted. This can help reduce the impact of noise on sensors that are on long I2C cables. The value is a percentage from the average value. A value of zero disables this filter.

COMPASS_AUTO_ROT.d: When enabled this will automatically check the orientation of compasses on successful completion of compass calibration. If set to 2 then external compasses will have their orientation automatically corrected.
COMPASS_AUTO_ROT.r: 0:Disabled,1:CheckOnly,2:CheckAndFix,3:use same tolerance to auto rotate 45 deg rotations

options.label: use same tolerance to auto rotate 45 deg rotations

COMPASS_PRIO1_ID.d: Compass device id with 1st order priority, set automatically if 0. Reboot required after change.

COMPASS_PRIO2_ID.d: Compass device id with 2nd order priority, set automatically if 0. Reboot required after change.

COMPASS_PRIO3_ID.d: Compass device id with 3rd order priority, set automatically if 0. Reboot required after change.

COMPASS_ENABLE.d: Setting this to Enabled(1) will enable the compass. Setting this to Disabled(0) will disable the compass. Note that this is separate from COMPASS_USE. This will enable the low level senor, and will enable logging of magnetometer data. To use the compass for navigation you must also set COMPASS_USE to 1.

COMPASS_SCALE.d: Scaling factor for first compass to compensate for sensor scaling errors. If this is 0 then no scaling is done

COMPASS_SCALE2.d: Scaling factor for 2nd compass to compensate for sensor scaling errors. If this is 0 then no scaling is done

COMPASS_SCALE3.d: Scaling factor for 3rd compass to compensate for sensor scaling errors. If this is 0 then no scaling is done

COMPASS_OPTIONS.d: This sets options to change the behaviour of the compass

bitmask.label: Allow missing DroneCAN compasses to be automaticaly replaced (calibration still required)

COMPASS_DEV_ID4.d: Extra 4th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID5.d: Extra 5th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID6.d: Extra 6th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID7.d: Extra 7th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID8.d: Extra 8th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_CUS_ROLL.d: Compass mounting position roll offset. Positive values = roll right, negative values = roll left. This parameter is only used when COMPASS_ORIENT/2/3 is set to CUSTOM.

COMPASS_CUS_PIT.d: Compass mounting position pitch offset. Positive values = pitch up, negative values = pitch down. This parameter is only used when COMPASS_ORIENT/2/3 is set to CUSTOM.

COMPASS_CUS_YAW.d: Compass mounting position yaw offset. Positive values = yaw right, negative values = yaw left. This parameter is only used when COMPASS_ORIENT/2/3 is set to CUSTOM.

COMPASS_PMOT_EXP.d: This is the exponential correction for the power output of the motor for per-motor compass correction

COMPASS_PMOT1_X.d: Compensation for X axis of motor1

COMPASS_PMOT1_Y.d: Compensation for Y axis of motor1

COMPASS_PMOT1_Z.d: Compensation for Z axis of motor1

COMPASS_PMOT2_X.d: Compensation for X axis of motor2

COMPASS_PMOT2_Y.d: Compensation for Y axis of motor2

COMPASS_PMOT2_Z.d: Compensation for Z axis of motor2

COMPASS_PMOT3_X.d: Compensation for X axis of motor3

COMPASS_PMOT3_Y.d: Compensation for Y axis of motor3

COMPASS_PMOT3_Z.d: Compensation for Z axis of motor3

COMPASS_PMOT4_X.d: Compensation for X axis of motor4

COMPASS_PMOT4_Y.d: Compensation for Y axis of motor4

COMPASS_PMOT4_Z.d: Compensation for Z axis of motor4

CUST_ROT1_ROLL.d: Custom euler roll, euler 321 (yaw, pitch, roll) ordering

CUST_ROT1_PITCH.d: Custom euler pitch, euler 321 (yaw, pitch, roll) ordering

CUST_ROT1_YAW.d: Custom euler yaw, euler 321 (yaw, pitch, roll) ordering

CUST_ROT2_ROLL.d: Custom euler roll, euler 321 (yaw, pitch, roll) ordering

CUST_ROT2_PITCH.d: Custom euler pitch, euler 321 (yaw, pitch, roll) ordering

CUST_ROT2_YAW.d: Custom euler yaw, euler 321 (yaw, pitch, roll) ordering

DDS_TIMEOUT_MS.d: The time in milliseconds the DDS client will wait for a response from the XRCE agent before reattempting.

DDS_MAX_RETRY.d: The maximum number of times the DDS client will attempt to ping the XRCE agent before exiting. Set to 0 to allow unlimited retries.

DDS_USE_NS.d: When enabled, ROS 2 topic and service names include a v<MAV_SYSID> segment

DID_MAVPORT.d: Serial port number to send OpenDroneID MAVLink messages to. Can be -1 if using DroneCAN.

DID_CANDRIVER.d: DroneCAN driver index, 0 to disable DroneCAN

DID_BARO_ACC.d: Barometer Vertical Accuracy when installed in the vehicle. Note this is dependent upon installation conditions and thus disabled by default

bitmask.label: Vector Nav use uncompensated values for accel gyro and mag

EFI_TYPE.d: What method of communication is used for EFI #1

EFI_COEF1.d: Used to calibrate fuel flow for MS protocol (Slope). This should be calculated from a log at constant fuel usage rate. Plot (ECYL[0].InjT*EFI.Rpm)/600.0 to get the duty_cycle. Measure actual fuel usage in cm^3/min, and set EFI_COEF1 = fuel_usage_cm3permin / duty_cycle

EFI_COEF2.d: Used to calibrate fuel flow for MS protocol (Offset). This can be used to correct for a non-zero offset in the fuel consumption calculation of EFI_COEF1

EFI_THRLIN_COEF1.d: First Order Polynomial Coefficient. (=1, if throttle is first order polynomial trendline)

EFI_THRLIN_COEF2.d: Second Order Polynomial Coefficient (=0, if throttle is second order polynomial trendline)

EFI_THRLIN_COEF3.d: Third Order Polynomial Coefficient. (=0, if throttle is third order polynomial trendline)

EK2_ENABLE.d: This enables EKF2. Enabling EKF2 only makes the maths run, it does not mean it will be used for flight control. To use it for flight control set AHRS_EKF_TYPE=2. A reboot or restart will need to be performed after changing the value of EK2_ENABLE for it to take effect.

EK2_GPS_TYPE.d: This controls use of GPS measurements : 0 = use 3D velocity & 2D position, 1 = use 2D velocity and 2D position, 2 = use 2D position, 3 = Inhibit GPS use - this can be useful when flying with an optical flow sensor in an environment where GPS quality is poor and subject to large multipath errors.
EK2_GPS_TYPE.r: 0:GPS 3D Vel and 2D Pos,1:GPS 2D vel and 2D pos,2:GPS 2D pos,3:No GPS

EK2_VELNE_M_NSE.d: This sets a lower limit on the speed accuracy reported by the GPS receiver that is used to set horizontal velocity observation noise. If the model of receiver used does not provide a speed accurcy estimate, then the parameter value will be used. Increasing it reduces the weighting of the GPS horizontal velocity measurements.

EK2_VELD_M_NSE.d: This sets a lower limit on the speed accuracy reported by the GPS receiver that is used to set vertical velocity observation noise. If the model of receiver used does not provide a speed accurcy estimate, then the parameter value will be used. Increasing it reduces the weighting of the GPS vertical velocity measurements.

EK2_VEL_I_GATE.d: This sets the percentage number of standard deviations applied to the GPS velocity measurement innovation consistency check. Decreasing it makes it more likely that good measurements will be rejected. Increasing it makes it more likely that bad measurements will be accepted.

EK2_POSNE_M_NSE.d: This sets the GPS horizontal position observation noise. Increasing it reduces the weighting of GPS horizontal position measurements.

EK2_POS_I_GATE.d: This sets the percentage number of standard deviations applied to the GPS position measurement innovation consistency check. Decreasing it makes it more likely that good measurements will be rejected. Increasing it makes it more likely that bad measurements will be accepted.

EK2_GLITCH_RAD.d: This controls the maximum radial uncertainty in position between the value predicted by the filter and the value measured by the GPS before the filter position and velocity states are reset to the GPS. Making this value larger allows the filter to ignore larger GPS glitches but also means that non-GPS errors such as IMU and compass can create a larger error in position before the filter is forced back to the GPS position.

EK2_ALT_SOURCE.d: Primary height sensor used by the EKF. If a sensor other than Baro is selected and becomes unavailable, then the Baro sensor will be used as a fallback. NOTE: The EK2_RNG_USE_HGT parameter can be used to switch to range-finder when close to the ground in conjunction with EK2_ALT_SOURCE = 0 or 2 (Baro or GPS).
EK2_ALT_SOURCE.r: 0:Use Baro,1:Use Range Finder,2:Use GPS,3:Use Range Beacon

EK2_ALT_M_NSE.d: This is the RMS value of noise in the altitude measurement. Increasing it reduces the weighting of the baro measurement and will make the filter respond more slowly to baro measurement errors, but will make it more sensitive to GPS and accelerometer errors.

EK2_HGT_I_GATE.d: This sets the percentage number of standard deviations applied to the height measurement innovation consistency check. Decreasing it makes it more likely that good measurements will be rejected. Increasing it makes it more likely that bad measurements will be accepted.

EK2_HGT_DELAY.d: This is the number of msec that the Height measurements lag behind the inertial measurements.

EK2_MAG_M_NSE.d: This is the RMS value of noise in magnetometer measurements. Increasing it reduces the weighting on these measurements.

EK2_MAG_CAL.d: This determines when the filter will use the 3-axis magnetometer fusion model that estimates both earth and body fixed magnetic field states, when it will use a simpler magnetic heading fusion model that does not use magnetic field states and when it will use an alternative method of yaw determination to the magnetometer. The 3-axis magnetometer fusion is only suitable for use when the external magnetic field environment is stable. EK2_MAG_CAL = 0 uses heading fusion on ground, 3-axis fusion in-flight, and is the default setting for Plane users. EK2_MAG_CAL = 1 uses 3-axis fusion only when manoeuvring. EK2_MAG_CAL = 2 uses heading fusion at all times, is recommended if the external magnetic field is varying and is the default for rovers. EK2_MAG_CAL = 3 uses heading fusion on the ground and 3-axis fusion after the first in-air field and yaw reset has completed, and is the default for copters. EK2_MAG_CAL = 4 uses 3-axis fusion at all times. NOTE: The fusion mode can be forced to 2 for specific EKF cores using the EK2_MAG_MASK parameter. NOTE: limited operation without a magnetometer or any other yaw sensor is possible by setting all COMPASS_USE, COMPASS_USE2, COMPASS_USE3, etc parameters to 0 with COMPASS_ENABLE set to 1. If this is done, the EK2_GSF_RUN and EK2_GSF_USE masks must be set to the same as EK2_IMU_MASK.
EK2_MAG_CAL.r: 0:When flying,1:When manoeuvring,2:Never,3:After first climb yaw reset,4:Always

EK2_MAG_I_GATE.d: This sets the percentage number of standard deviations applied to the magnetometer measurement innovation consistency check. Decreasing it makes it more likely that good measurements will be rejected. Increasing it makes it more likely that bad measurements will be accepted.

EK2_EAS_M_NSE.d: This is the RMS value of noise in equivalent airspeed measurements used by planes. Increasing it reduces the weighting of airspeed measurements and will make wind speed estimates less noisy and slower to converge. Increasing also increases navigation errors when dead-reckoning without GPS measurements.

EK2_EAS_I_GATE.d: This sets the percentage number of standard deviations applied to the airspeed measurement innovation consistency check. Decreasing it makes it more likely that good measurements will be rejected. Increasing it makes it more likely that bad measurements will be accepted.

EK2_RNG_M_NSE.d: This is the RMS value of noise in the range finder measurement. Increasing it reduces the weighting on this measurement.

EK2_RNG_I_GATE.d: This sets the percentage number of standard deviations applied to the range finder innovation consistency check. Decreasing it makes it more likely that good measurements will be rejected. Increasing it makes it more likely that bad measurements will be accepted.

EK2_MAX_FLOW.d: This sets the magnitude maximum optical flow rate in rad/sec that will be accepted by the filter

EK2_FLOW_M_NSE.d: This is the RMS value of noise and errors in optical flow measurements. Increasing it reduces the weighting on these measurements.

EK2_FLOW_I_GATE.d: This sets the percentage number of standard deviations applied to the optical flow innovation consistency check. Decreasing it makes it more likely that good measurements will be rejected. Increasing it makes it more likely that bad measurements will be accepted.

EK2_FLOW_DELAY.d: This is the number of msec that the optical flow measurements lag behind the inertial measurements. It is the time from the end of the optical flow averaging period and does not include the time delay due to the 100msec of averaging within the flow sensor.

EK2_GYRO_P_NSE.d: This control disturbance noise controls the growth of estimated error due to gyro measurement errors excluding bias. Increasing it makes the flter trust the gyro measurements less and other measurements more.

EK2_ACC_P_NSE.d: This control disturbance noise controls the growth of estimated error due to accelerometer measurement errors excluding bias. Increasing it makes the flter trust the accelerometer measurements less and other measurements more.

EK2_GBIAS_P_NSE.d: This state  process noise controls growth of the gyro delta angle bias state error estimate. Increasing it makes rate gyro bias estimation faster and noisier.

EK2_GSCL_P_NSE.d: This noise controls the rate of gyro scale factor learning. Increasing it makes rate gyro scale factor estimation faster and noisier.

EK2_ABIAS_P_NSE.d: This noise controls the growth of the vertical accelerometer delta velocity bias state error estimate. Increasing it makes accelerometer bias estimation faster and noisier.

EK2_WIND_P_NSE.d: This state process noise controls the growth of wind state error estimates. Increasing it makes wind estimation faster and noisier.

EK2_WIND_PSCALE.d: This controls how much the process noise on the wind states is increased when gaining or losing altitude to take into account changes in wind speed and direction with altitude. Increasing this parameter increases how rapidly the wind states adapt when changing altitude, but does make wind velocity estimation noiser.

EK2_GPS_CHECK.d: This is a 1 byte bitmap controlling which GPS preflight checks are performed. Set to 0 to bypass all checks. Set to 255 perform all checks. Set to 3 to check just the number of satellites and HDoP. Set to 31 for the most rigorous checks that will still allow checks to pass when the copter is moving, eg launch from a boat.

EK2_IMU_MASK.d: 1 byte bitmap of IMUs to use in EKF2. A separate instance of EKF2 will be started for each IMU selected. Set to 1 to use the first IMU only (default), set to 2 to use the second IMU only, set to 3 to use the first and second IMU. Additional IMU's can be used up to a maximum of 6 if memory and processing resources permit. There may be insufficient memory and processing resources to run multiple instances. If this occurs EKF2 will fail to start.

EK2_CHECK_SCALE.d: This scales the thresholds that are used to check GPS accuracy before it is used by the EKF. A value of 100 is the default. Values greater than 100 increase and values less than 100 reduce the maximum GPS error the EKF will accept. A value of 200 will double the allowable GPS error.

EK2_NOAID_M_NSE.d: This sets the amount of position variation that the EKF allows for when operating without external measurements (eg GPS or optical flow). Increasing this parameter makes the EKF attitude estimate less sensitive to vehicle manoeuvres but more sensitive to IMU errors.

EK2_YAW_M_NSE.d: This is the RMS value of noise in yaw measurements from the magnetometer. Increasing it reduces the weighting on these measurements.

EK2_YAW_I_GATE.d: This sets the percentage number of standard deviations applied to the magnetometer yaw measurement innovation consistency check. Decreasing it makes it more likely that good measurements will be rejected. Increasing it makes it more likely that bad measurements will be accepted.

EK2_TAU_OUTPUT.d: Sets the time constant of the output complementary filter/predictor in centi-seconds.

EK2_MAGE_P_NSE.d: This state process noise controls the growth of earth magnetic field state error estimates. Increasing it makes earth magnetic field estimation faster and noisier.

EK2_MAGB_P_NSE.d: This state process noise controls the growth of body magnetic field state error estimates. Increasing it makes magnetometer bias error estimation faster and noisier.

EK2_RNG_USE_HGT.d: Range finder can be used as the primary height source when below this percentage of its maximum range (see RNGFND*_MAX). This will not work unless Baro or GPS height is selected as the primary height source vis EK2_ALT_SOURCE = 0 or 2 respectively.  This feature should not be used for terrain following as it is designed  for vertical takeoff and landing with climb above  the range finder use height before commencing the mission, and with horizontal position changes below that height being limited to a flat region around the takeoff and landing point.

EK2_TERR_GRAD.d: Specifies the maximum gradient of the terrain below the vehicle assumed when it is fusing range finder or optical flow to estimate terrain height.

EK2_BCN_M_NSE.d: This is the RMS value of noise in the range beacon measurement. Increasing it reduces the weighting on this measurement.

EK2_BCN_I_GTE.d: This sets the percentage number of standard deviations applied to the range beacon measurement innovation consistency check. Decreasing it makes it more likely that good measurements will be rejected. Increasing it makes it more likely that bad measurements will be accepted.

EK2_BCN_DELAY.d: This is the number of msec that the range beacon measurements lag behind the inertial measurements. It is the time from the end of the optical flow averaging period and does not include the time delay due to the 100msec of averaging within the flow sensor.

EK2_RNG_USE_SPD.d: The range finder will not be used as the primary height source when the horizontal ground speed is greater than this value.

EK2_MAG_MASK.d: 1 byte bitmap of EKF cores that will disable magnetic field states and use simple magnetic heading fusion at all times. This parameter enables specified cores to be used as a backup for flight into an environment with high levels of external magnetic interference which may degrade the EKF attitude estimate when using 3-axis magnetometer fusion. NOTE : Use of a different magnetometer fusion algorithm on different cores makes unwanted EKF core switches due to magnetometer errors more likely.

EK2_OGN_HGT_MASK.d: When a height sensor other than GPS is used as the primary height source by the EKF, the position of the zero height datum is defined by that sensor and its frame of reference.
