GRIP_GRAB.d: PWM value in microseconds sent to Gripper to initiate grabbing the cargo

GRIP_RELEASE.d: PWM value in microseconds sent to Gripper to release the cargo

GRIP_NEUTRAL.d: PWM value in microseconds sent to grabber when not grabbing or releasing

GRIP_REGRAB.d: Time in seconds that EPM gripper will regrab the cargo to ensure grip has not weakened; 0 to disable

GRIP_AUTOCLOSE.d: Time in seconds that gripper close the gripper after opening; 0 to disable

H_TAIL_TYPE.d: Tail type selection. Servo Only uses tail rotor pitch to provide yaw control (including stabilization) via an output assigned to Motor4.  Servo with External Gyro uses an external gyro to control tail rotor pitch via a servo.  Yaw control without stabilization is passed to the external gyro via the output assigned to Motor4.  Direct Drive Variable Pitch(DDVP) is used for tails that have a motor whose ESC is connected to an output with function HeliTailRSC. Tail pitch is still accomplished with a servo on an output assigned to Motor4 function.  Direct Drive Fixed Pitch (DDFP) CW is used for helicopters with a rotor that spins clockwise when viewed from above with a motor whose ESC is controlled by an output whose function is Motor4. Direct Drive Fixed Pitch (DDFP) CCW is used for helicopters with a rotor that spins counter clockwise when viewed from above with a motor whose ESC is controlled by an output whose function is Motor4. In both DDFP cases, no servo is used for the tail and the tail motor esc on Motor4 output is used to control the yaw axis using motor speed.
H_TAIL_TYPE.r: 0:Servo only,2:DirectDrive VarPitch,3:DirectDrive FixedPitch CW,4:DirectDrive FixedPitch CCW

H_TAIL_SPEED.d: Direct drive, variable pitch tail ESC speed in percent output to the tail motor esc (HeliTailRSC Servo) when motor interlock enabled (throttle hold off) and speed fully ramped up after spoolup.

H_SW_TYPE.d: H3 is generic, three-servo only. H3_120/H3_140 plates have Motor1 left side, Motor2 right side, Motor3 elevator in rear. HR3_120/HR3_140 have Motor1 right side, Motor2 left side, Motor3 elevator in front - use H3_120/H3_140 and reverse servo and collective directions as necessary. For all H3_90 swashplates use H4_90 and don't use servo output for the missing servo. For H4-90 Motors1&2 are left/right respectively, Motors3&4 are rear/front respectively. For H4-45 Motors1&2 are LF/RF, Motors3&4 are LR/RR

H_SW_COL_DIR.d: Direction collective moves for positive pitch. 0 for Normal, 1 for Reversed

H_SW_LIN_SVO.d: This linearizes the swashplate 1 servo's mechanical output to account for nonlinear output due to arm rotation.  This requires a specific setup procedure to work properly.  The servo arm must be centered on the mechanical throw at the servo trim position and the servo trim position kept as close to 1500 as possible. Leveling the swashplate can only be done through the pitch links.  See the ardupilot wiki for more details on setup.

H_SW_PHANG.d: Phase angle compensation can be used to correct control coupling issues.  If pitching the swash forward induces a roll, this can be correct the problem.

H_SW_H3_ENABLE.d: Automatically set when H3 generic swash type is selected for swashplate 1. Do not set manually.

H_SW_H3_SV1_POS.d: Azimuth position on swashplate for servo 1 with the front of the heli being 0 deg

H_SW_H3_SV2_POS.d: Azimuth position on swashplate 1 for servo 2 with the front of the heli being 0 deg

H_SW_H3_SV3_POS.d: Azimuth position on swashplate 1 for servo 3 with the front of the heli being 0 deg

H_COL2YAW.d: Feed-forward compensation to automatically add rudder input when collective pitch is increased. Can be positive or negative depending on mechanics.

H_DDFP_THST_EXPO.d: Tail rotor DDFP motor thrust curve exponent (0.0 for linear to 1.0 for second order curve)

H_DDFP_SPIN_MIN.d: Point at which the thrust starts expressed as a number from 0 to 1 in the entire output range.

H_DDFP_SPIN_MAX.d: Point at which the thrust saturates expressed as a number from 0 to 1 in the entire output range

H_DDFP_BAT_IDX.d: Which battery monitor should be used for doing compensation

H_DDFP_BAT_V_MAX.d: Battery voltage compensation maximum voltage (voltage above this will have no additional scaling effect on thrust).  Recommend 4.2 * cell count, 0 = Disabled

H_DDFP_BAT_V_MIN.d: Battery voltage compensation minimum voltage (voltage below this will have no additional scaling effect on thrust).  Recommend 3.3 * cell count, 0 = Disabled

H_YAW_TRIM.d: Fixed offset applied to yaw output to minimize yaw I-term contribution needed to counter rotor drag. Currently only works for DDFP tails (H_TAIL_TYPE = 3 or H_TAIL_TYPE = 4). If using the H_COL2YAW compensation this trim is used to compensate for the main rotor profile drag. If H_COL2YAW is not used, this value can be set to reduce the yaw I-term to zero when in a steady hover. In this case, move trim in the direction of the I-term found in PIDY.I log message (e.g. negative I means you want negative trim).

H_DUAL_MODE.d: Sets the dual mode of the heli, either as tandem, transverse, or intermeshing/coaxial.

H_DCP_SCALER.d: Scaling factor applied to the differential-collective-pitch

H_DCP_YAW.d: Feed-forward compensation to automatically add yaw input when differential collective pitch is applied.  Disabled for intermeshing mode.

H_YAW_SCALER.d: Scaler for mixing yaw into roll or pitch.

H_COL2_MIN.d: Lowest possible servo position in PWM microseconds for swashplate 2

H_COL2_MAX.d: Highest possible servo position in PWM microseconds for swashplate 2

H_SW2_TYPE.d: H3 is generic, three-servo only. H3_120/H3_140 plates have Motor1 left side, Motor2 right side, Motor3 elevator in rear. HR3_120/HR3_140 have Motor1 right side, Motor2 left side, Motor3 elevator in front - use H3_120/H3_140 and reverse servo and collective directions as necessary. For all H3_90 swashplates use H4_90 and don't use servo output for the missing servo. For H4-90 Motors1&2 are left/right respectively, Motors3&4 are rear/front respectively. For H4-45 Motors1&2 are LF/RF, Motors3&4 are LR/RR

H_SW2_COL_DIR.d: Direction collective moves for positive pitch. 0 for Normal, 1 for Reversed

H_SW2_LIN_SVO.d: This linearizes the swashplate 2 servo's mechanical output to account for nonlinear output due to arm rotation.  This requires a specific setup procedure to work properly.  The servo arm must be centered on the mechanical throw at the servo trim position and the servo trim position kept as close to 1500 as possible. Leveling the swashplate can only be done through the pitch links.  See the ardupilot wiki for more details on setup.

H_SW2_PHANG.d: Phase angle compensation can be used to correct control coupling issues.  If pitching the swash forward induces a roll, this can be correct the problem.

H_SW2_H3_ENABLE.d: Automatically set when H3 generic swash type is selected for swashplate 2. Do not set manually.

H_SW2_H3_SV1_POS.d: Azimuth position on swashplate for servo 1 with the front of the heli being 0 deg

H_SW2_H3_SV2_POS.d: Azimuth position on swashplate 2 for servo 2 with the front of the heli being 0 deg

H_SW2_H3_SV3_POS.d: Azimuth position on swashplate 2 for servo 3 with the front of the heli being 0 deg

H_DCP_TRIM.d: Removes I term bias due to center of gravity offsets or discrepancies between rotors in swashplate setup. If DCP axis has I term bias while hovering in calm winds, use value of bias in DCP_TRIM to re-center I term.

H_YAW_REV_EXPO.d: For intermeshing mode only. Yaw revereser smoothing exponent, smoothen transition near zero collective region. Increase this parameter to shink smoothing range. Set to -1 to disable reverser.

H_COL_MIN.d: Lowest possible servo position in PWM microseconds for the swashplate

H_COL_MAX.d: Highest possible servo position in PWM microseconds for the swashplate

H_SV_MAN.d: Manual servo override for swash set-up. Must be 0 (Disabled) for flight!

H_CYC_MAX.d: Maximum cyclic pitch angle of the swash plate.  There are no units to this parameter.  This should be adjusted to get the desired cyclic blade pitch for the pitch and roll axes.  Typically this should be 6-7 deg (measured blade pitch angle difference between stick centered and stick max deflection.

H_SV_TEST.d: Number of cycles to run servo test on boot-up

H_COL_HOVER.d: Collective needed to hover expressed as a number from 0 to 1 where 0 is H_COL_MIN and 1 is H_COL_MAX

H_HOVER_LEARN.d: Enable/Disable automatic learning of hover collective

H_OPTIONS.d: Bitmask of heli options.  Bit 0 changes how the pitch, roll, and yaw axis integrator term is managed for low speed and takeoff/landing. In AC 4.0 and earlier, scheme uses a leaky integrator for ground speeds less than 5 m/s and won't let the steady state integrator build above ILMI. The integrator is allowed to build to the ILMI value when it is landed.  The other integrator management scheme bases integrator limiting on takeoff and landing.  Whenever the aircraft is landed the integrator is set to zero.  When the aicraft is airborne, the integrator is only limited by IMAX.

H_COL_ANG_MIN.d: Minimum collective blade pitch angle in deg that corresponds to the PWM set for minimum collective pitch (H_COL_MIN).

H_COL_ANG_MAX.d: Maximum collective blade pitch angle in deg that corresponds to the PWM set for maximum collective pitch (H_COL_MAX).

H_COL_ZERO_THRST.d: Collective blade pitch angle at zero thrust in degrees. For symetric airfoil blades this value is zero deg. For chambered airfoil blades this value is typically negative.

H_COL_LAND_MIN.d: Minimum collective blade pitch angle when landed in degrees for non-manual collective modes (i.e. modes that use altitude hold).

H_RSC_SETPOINT.d: Throttle (HeliRSC Servo) output in percent to the external motor governor when motor interlock enabled (throttle hold off).

H_RSC_MODE.d: Selects the type of rotor speed control used to determine throttle output to the HeliRSC servo channel when motor interlock is enabled (throttle hold off). RC Passthrough sends the input from the RC Motor Interlock channel as throttle output.  External Gov SetPoint sends the RSC SetPoint parameter value as throttle output.  Throttle Curve uses the 5 point throttle curve to determine throttle output based on the collective output.  AutoThrottle requires a rotor speed sensor, contains an advanced autothrottle governor and is primarily for piston and turbine engines. WARNING: Throttle ramp time and throttle curve MUST be tuned properly using Throttle Curve mode before using AutoThrottle

H_RSC_RAMP_TIME.d: Time in seconds for throttle output (HeliRSC servo) to ramp from ground idle (RSC_IDLE) to flight idle throttle setting when motor interlock is enabled (throttle hold off).

H_RSC_RUNUP_TIME.d: Actual time in seconds for the main rotor to reach full speed after motor interlock is enabled (throttle hold off). Must be at least one second longer than the Throttle Ramp Time that is set with RSC_RAMP_TIME. WARNING: For AutoThrottle users with piston and turbine engines it is VERY important to know how long it takes to warm up your engine and reach full rotor speed when throttle switch is turned ON. This timer should be set for at least the amount of time it takes to get your helicopter to full flight power, ready for takeoff. Failure to heed this warning could result in the auto-takeoff mode attempting to lift up into hover before the engine has reached full power, and subsequent loss of control

H_RSC_CRITICAL.d: Percentage of normal rotor speed where flight is no longer possible. However currently the rotor runup/rundown is estimated using the RSC_RUNUP_TIME parameter.   Estimated rotor speed increases/decreases between 0 (rotor stopped) to 1 (rotor at normal speed) in the RSC_RUNUP_TIME in seconds. This parameter should be set so that the estimated rotor speed goes below critical in approximately 3 seconds.  So if you had a 10 second runup time then set RSC_CRITICAL to 70%.

H_RSC_IDLE.d: Throttle output (HeliRSC Servo) in percent while armed but motor interlock is disabled (throttle hold on). FOR COMBUSTION ENGINES. Sets the engine ground idle throttle percentage with clutch disengaged. This must be set to zero for electric helicopters under most situations. If the ESC has an autorotation window this can be set to keep the autorotation window open in the ESC. Consult the operating manual for your ESC to set it properly for this purpose

H_RSC_SLEWRATE.d: This controls the maximum rate at which the throttle output (HeliRSC servo) can change, as a percentage per second. A value of 100 means the throttle can change over its full range in one second. A value of zero gives unlimited slew rate.

H_RSC_THRCRV_0.d: Sets the throttle output (HeliRSC servo) in percent for the throttle curve at the minimum collective pitch position. The 0 percent collective is defined by H_COL_MIN. Example: if the setup has -2 degree to +10 degree collective pitch setup, this setting would correspond to -2 degree of pitch.

H_RSC_THRCRV_25.d: Sets the throttle output (HeliRSC servo) in percent for the throttle curve at 25% of full collective travel where he 0 percent collective is defined by H_COL_MIN and 100 percent collective is defined by H_COL_MAX.  Example: if the setup has -2 degree to +10 degree collective pitch setup, the total range is 12 degrees. 25% of 12 degrees is 3 degrees, so this setting would correspond to +1 degree of pitch.

H_RSC_THRCRV_50.d: Sets the throttle output (HeliRSC servo) in percent for the throttle curve at 50% of full collective travel where he 0 percent collective is defined by H_COL_MIN and 100 percent collective is defined by H_COL_MAX.  Example: if the setup has -2 degree to +10 degree collective pitch setup, the total range is 12 degrees. 50% of 12 degrees is 6 degrees, so this setting would correspond to +4 degree of pitch.

H_RSC_THRCRV_75.d: Sets the throttle output (HeliRSC servo) in percent for the throttle curve at 75% of full collective travel where he 0 percent collective is defined by H_COL_MIN and 100 percent collective is defined by H_COL_MAX.  Example: if the setup has -2 degree to +10 degree collective pitch setup, the total range is 12 degrees. 75% of 12 degrees is 9 degrees, so this setting would correspond to +7 degree of pitch.

H_RSC_THRCRV_100.d: Sets the throttle output (HeliRSC servo) in percent for the throttle curve at the minimum collective pitch position. The 100 percent collective is defined by H_COL_MAX.  Example: if the setup has -2 degree to +10 degree collective pitch setup, this setting would correspond to +10 degree of pitch.

H_RSC_GOV_RANGE.d: RPM range +/- governor rpm reference setting where governor is operational. If speed sensor fails or rpm falls outside of this range, the governor will disengage and return to throttle curve. Recommended range is 100

H_RSC_CLDWN_TIME.d: Will provide a fast idle for engine cooldown by raising the Ground Idle speed setting by 50% for the number of seconds the timer is set for. A setting of zero disables the fast idle. This feature will only apply after the runup complete has been declared. This will not extend the time before ground idle is declared, which triggers engine shutdown for autonomous landings.

H_RSC_GOV_COMP.d: Adjusts the autothrottle governor torque compensator that determines how fast the governor will adjust the base torque reference to compensate for changes in density altitude. If RPM is low or high by more than 2-5 RPM, increase this setting by 1% at a time until the governor speed matches your RPM setting. Setting the compensator too high can result in surging and throttle "hunting". Do not make large adjustments at one time

H_RSC_GOV_DROOP.d: AutoThrottle governor droop response under load, normal settings of 0-50%. Higher value is quicker response to large speed changes due to load but may cause surging. Adjust this to be as aggressive as possible without getting surging or RPM over-run when the governor responds to large load changes on the rotor system

H_RSC_GOV_FF.d: Feedforward governor gain to throttle response during sudden loading/unloading of the rotor system. If RPM drops excessively during full collective climb with the droop response set correctly, increase the governor feedforward.

H_RSC_GOV_RPM.d: Main rotor RPM that governor maintains when engaged

H_RSC_GOV_TORQUE.d: Adjusts the engine's percentage of torque rise on autothrottle during ramp-up to governor speed. The torque rise will determine how fast the rotor speed will ramp up when rotor speed reaches 50% of the rotor RPM setting. The sequence of events engaging the governor is as follows: Throttle ramp time will engage the clutch and start the main rotor turning. The collective should be at flat pitch and the throttle curve set to provide at least 50% of normal RPM at flat pitch. The autothrottle torque limiter will automatically activate and start accelerating the main rotor. If the autothrottle consistently fails to accelerate the main rotor during ramp-in due to engine tune or other factors, then increase the torque limiter setting. NOTE: throttle ramp time and throttle curve should be tuned using RSC_MODE Throttle Curve before using RSC_MODE AutoThrottle

H_RSC_AROT_ENBL.d: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the autorotation functionality within the Rotor Speed Controller.

H_RSC_AROT_RAMP.d: When exiting an autorotation in a bailout manoeuvre, this is the time in seconds for the throttle output (HeliRSC servo) to ramp from idle (H_RSC_AROT_IDLE) to flight throttle setting when motor interlock is re-enabled. When using an ESC with an autorotation bailout function, this parameter should be set to 0.1 (minimum value).

H_RSC_AROT_IDLE.d: Idle throttle used for during autotoration. For external governors, this would be set to a value that is within the autorotation window of the governer/ESC to enable fast spool-up, when bailing out of an autorotation.  Set 0 to disable.

H_RSC_AROT_RUNUP.d: When exiting an autorotation in a bailout manoeuvre, this is the expected time in seconds for the main rotor to reach full speed after motor interlock is enabled. Must be at least one second longer than the H_RSC_AROT_RAMP time that is set. This timer should be set for at least the amount of time it takes to get your helicopter to full flight power. Failure to heed this warning could result in early entry into autonomously controlled collective modes (e.g. alt hold, loiter, etc), whereby the collective could be raised before the engine has reached full power, with a subsequently dangerous slowing of head speed.

IM_ACRO_COL_EXP.d: Used to soften collective pitch inputs near center point in Acro mode.

IM_STB_COL_1.d: Helicopter's minimum collective pitch setting at zero collective stick input in Stabilize mode.  Set this as a percent of collective range given by H_COL_MAX minus H_COL_MIN.

IM_STB_COL_2.d: Helicopter's collective pitch setting at mid-low (40%) collective stick input in Stabilize mode. Set this as a percent of collective range given by H_COL_MAX minus H_COL_MIN.

IM_STB_COL_3.d: Helicopter's collective pitch setting at mid-high (60%) collective stick input in Stabilize mode. Set this as a percent of collective range given by H_COL_MAX minus H_COL_MIN.

IM_STB_COL_4.d: Helicopter's maximum collective pitch setting at full collective stick input in Stabilize mode. Set this as a percent of collective range given by H_COL_MAX minus H_COL_MIN.

INS_GYROFFS_X.d: Gyro sensor offsets of X axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS_GYROFFS_Y.d: Gyro sensor offsets of Y axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS_GYROFFS_Z.d: Gyro sensor offsets of Z axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS_GYR2OFFS_X.d: Gyro2 sensor offsets of X axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS_GYR2OFFS_Y.d: Gyro2 sensor offsets of Y axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS_GYR2OFFS_Z.d: Gyro2 sensor offsets of Z axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS_GYR3OFFS_X.d: Gyro3 sensor offsets of X axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS_GYR3OFFS_Y.d: Gyro3 sensor offsets of Y axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS_GYR3OFFS_Z.d: Gyro3 sensor offsets of Z axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS_ACCSCAL_X.d: Accelerometer scaling of X axis.  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS_ACCSCAL_Y.d: Accelerometer scaling of Y axis  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS_ACCSCAL_Z.d: Accelerometer scaling of Z axis  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS_ACCOFFS_X.d: Accelerometer offsets of X axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS_ACCOFFS_Y.d: Accelerometer offsets of Y axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS_ACCOFFS_Z.d: Accelerometer offsets of Z axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS_ACC2SCAL_X.d: Accelerometer2 scaling of X axis.  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS_ACC2SCAL_Y.d: Accelerometer2 scaling of Y axis  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS_ACC2SCAL_Z.d: Accelerometer2 scaling of Z axis  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS_ACC2OFFS_X.d: Accelerometer2 offsets of X axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS_ACC2OFFS_Y.d: Accelerometer2 offsets of Y axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS_ACC2OFFS_Z.d: Accelerometer2 offsets of Z axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS_ACC3SCAL_X.d: Accelerometer3 scaling of X axis.  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS_ACC3SCAL_Y.d: Accelerometer3 scaling of Y axis  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS_ACC3SCAL_Z.d: Accelerometer3 scaling of Z axis  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS_ACC3OFFS_X.d: Accelerometer3 offsets of X axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS_ACC3OFFS_Y.d: Accelerometer3 offsets of Y axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS_ACC3OFFS_Z.d: Accelerometer3 offsets of Z axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS_GYRO_FILTER.d: Filter cutoff frequency for gyroscopes. This can be set to a lower value to try to cope with very high vibration levels in aircraft. A value of zero means no filtering (not recommended!)

INS_ACCEL_FILTER.d: Filter cutoff frequency for accelerometers. This can be set to a lower value to try to cope with very high vibration levels in aircraft. A value of zero means no filtering (not recommended!)

INS_USE.d: Use first IMU for attitude, velocity and position estimates

INS_USE2.d: Use second IMU for attitude, velocity and position estimates

INS_USE3.d: Use third IMU for attitude, velocity and position estimates

INS_STILL_THRESH.d: Threshold to tolerate vibration to determine if vehicle is motionless. This depends on the frame type and if there is a constant vibration due to motors before launch or after landing. Total motionless is about 0.05. Suggested values: Planes/rover use 0.1, multirotors use 1, tradHeli uses 5

INS_GYR_CAL.d: Conrols when automatic gyro calibration is performed

INS_TRIM_OPTION.d: Specifies how the accel cal routine determines the trims
INS_TRIM_OPTION.r: 0:Don't adjust the trims,1:Assume first orientation was level,2:Assume ACC_BODYFIX is perfectly aligned to the vehicle

options.label: Assume ACC_BODYFIX is perfectly aligned to the vehicle

INS_ACC_BODYFIX.d: The body-fixed accelerometer to be used for trim calculation

INS_POS1_X.d: X position of the first IMU Accelerometer in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS_POS1_Y.d: Y position of the first IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS_POS1_Z.d: Z position of the first IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS_POS2_X.d: X position of the second IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS_POS2_Y.d: Y position of the second IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS_POS2_Z.d: Z position of the second IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS_POS3_X.d: X position of the third IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS_POS3_Y.d: Y position of the third IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS_POS3_Z.d: Z position of the third IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS_GYR_ID.d: Gyro sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

INS_GYR2_ID.d: Gyro2 sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

INS_GYR3_ID.d: Gyro3 sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

INS_ACC_ID.d: Accelerometer sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

INS_ACC2_ID.d: Accelerometer2 sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

INS_ACC3_ID.d: Accelerometer3 sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

INS_FAST_SAMPLE.d: Mask of IMUs to enable fast sampling on, if available

INS_ENABLE_MASK.d: Bitmask of IMUs to enable. It can be used to prevent startup of specific detected IMUs

INS_GYRO_RATE.d: Gyro rate for IMUs with fast sampling enabled. The gyro rate is the sample rate at which the IMU filters operate and needs to be at least double the maximum filter frequency. If the sensor does not support the selected rate the next highest supported rate will be used. For IMUs which do not support fast sampling this setting is ignored and the default gyro rate of 1Khz is used.

INS_ACC1_CALTEMP.d: Temperature that the 1st accelerometer was calibrated at

INS_GYR1_CALTEMP.d: Temperature that the 1st gyroscope was calibrated at

INS_ACC2_CALTEMP.d: Temperature that the 2nd accelerometer was calibrated at

INS_GYR2_CALTEMP.d: Temperature that the 2nd gyroscope was calibrated at

INS_ACC3_CALTEMP.d: Temperature that the 3rd accelerometer was calibrated at

INS_GYR3_CALTEMP.d: Temperature that the 3rd gyroscope was calibrated at

INS_TCAL_OPTIONS.d: This enables optional temperature calibration features. Setting of the Persist bits will save the temperature and/or accelerometer calibration parameters in the bootloader sector on the next update of the bootloader.

INS4_USE.d: Use first IMU for attitude, velocity and position estimates

INS4_ACC_ID.d: Accelerometer sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

INS4_ACCSCAL_X.d: Accelerometer scaling of X axis.  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS4_ACCSCAL_Y.d: Accelerometer scaling of Y axis  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS4_ACCSCAL_Z.d: Accelerometer scaling of Z axis  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS4_ACCOFFS_X.d: Accelerometer offsets of X axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS4_ACCOFFS_Y.d: Accelerometer offsets of Y axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS4_ACCOFFS_Z.d: Accelerometer offsets of Z axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS4_ACC_CALTEMP.d: Temperature that the accelerometer was calibrated at

INS4_GYR_ID.d: Gyro sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

INS4_GYROFFS_X.d: Gyro sensor offsets of X axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS4_GYROFFS_Y.d: Gyro sensor offsets of Y axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS4_GYROFFS_Z.d: Gyro sensor offsets of Z axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS4_GYR_CALTEMP.d: Temperature that the gyroscope was calibrated at

INS4_TCAL_ENABLE.d: Enable the use of temperature calibration parameters for this IMU. For automatic learning set to 2 and also set the INS_TCALn_TMAX to the target temperature, then reboot

INS4_TCAL_TMIN.d: The minimum temperature that the calibration is valid for

INS4_TCAL_TMAX.d: The maximum temperature that the calibration is valid for. This must be at least 10 degrees above TMIN for calibration

INS4_TCAL_ACC1_X.d: This is the 1st order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_ACC1_Y.d: This is the 1st order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_ACC1_Z.d: This is the 1st order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS5_USE.d: Use first IMU for attitude, velocity and position estimates

INS5_ACC_ID.d: Accelerometer sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

INS5_ACCSCAL_X.d: Accelerometer scaling of X axis.  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS5_ACCSCAL_Y.d: Accelerometer scaling of Y axis  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS5_ACCSCAL_Z.d: Accelerometer scaling of Z axis  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS5_ACCOFFS_X.d: Accelerometer offsets of X axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS5_ACCOFFS_Y.d: Accelerometer offsets of Y axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS5_ACCOFFS_Z.d: Accelerometer offsets of Z axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS5_POS_X.d: X position of the first IMU Accelerometer in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS5_POS_Y.d: Y position of the first IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS5_POS_Z.d: Z position of the first IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS5_ACC_CALTEMP.d: Temperature that the accelerometer was calibrated at

INS5_GYR_ID.d: Gyro sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

INS5_GYROFFS_X.d: Gyro sensor offsets of X axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS5_GYROFFS_Y.d: Gyro sensor offsets of Y axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS5_GYROFFS_Z.d: Gyro sensor offsets of Z axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS5_GYR_CALTEMP.d: Temperature that the gyroscope was calibrated at

INS5_TCAL_TMAX.d: The maximum temperature that the calibration is valid for. This must be at least 10 degrees above TMIN for calibration

INS_HNTC2_FREQ.d: Harmonic Notch Filter base center frequency in Hz. This is the center frequency for static notches, the center frequency for Throttle based notches at the reference thrust value, and the minimum limit of center frequency variation for all other notch types. This should always be set lower than half the backend gyro rate (which is typically 1Khz).

INS_HNTC2_BW.d: Harmonic Notch Filter bandwidth in Hz. This is typically set to half the base frequency. The ratio of base frequency to bandwidth determines the notch quality factor and is fixed across harmonics.

INS_HNTC2_ATT.d: Harmonic Notch Filter attenuation in dB. Values greater than 40dB will typically produce a hard notch rather than a modest attenuation of motor noise.

INS_HNTC2_HMNCS.d: Bitmask of harmonic frequencies to apply Harmonic Notch Filter to. This option takes effect on the next reboot. A value of 0 disables this filter. The first harmonic refers to the base frequency.

INS_HNTC2_REF.d: A reference value of zero disables dynamic updates on the Harmonic Notch Filter and a positive value enables dynamic updates on the Harmonic Notch Filter.  For throttle-based scaling, this parameter is the reference value associated with the specified frequency to facilitate frequency scaling of the Harmonic Notch Filter. For RPM and ESC telemetry based tracking, this parameter is set to 1 to enable the Harmonic Notch Filter using the RPM sensor or ESC telemetry set to measure rotor speed.  The sensor data is converted to Hz automatically for use in the Harmonic Notch Filter.  This reference value may also be used to scale the sensor data, if required.  For example, rpm sensor data is required to measure heli motor RPM. Therefore the reference value can be used to scale the RPM sensor to the rotor RPM.

INS_HNTC2_MODE.d: Harmonic Notch Filter dynamic frequency tracking mode. Dynamic updates can be throttle, RPM sensor, ESC telemetry or dynamic FFT based. Throttle-based harmonic notch cannot be used on fixed wing only planes. It can for Copters, QuadPlane(while in VTOL modes), and Rovers.

INS_HNTC2_OPTS.d: Harmonic Notch Filter options. Triple and double-notches can provide deeper attenuation across a wider bandwidth with reduced latency than single notches and are suitable for larger aircraft. Multi-Source attaches a harmonic notch to each detected noise frequency instead of simply being multiples of the base frequency, in the case of FFT it will attach notches to each of three detected noise peaks, in the case of ESC it will attach notches to each of four motor RPM values. Loop rate update changes the notch center frequency at the scheduler loop rate rather than at the default of 200Hz. If both double and triple notches are specified only double notches will take effect.

bitmask.label: Use min freq on RPM source failure

INS_HNTC2_FM_RAT.d: The minimum ratio below the configured frequency to take throttle based notch filters when flying at a throttle level below the reference throttle. Note that lower frequency notch filters will have more phase lag. If you want throttle based notch filtering to be effective at a throttle up to 30% below the configured notch frequency then set this parameter to 0.7. The default of 1.0 means the notch will not go below the frequency in the FREQ parameter.

KDE_NPOLE.d: Sets the number of motor poles to calculate the correct RPM value

LAND_SPD_MS.d: The descent speed for the final stage of landing in m/s

LAND_SPD_HIGH_MS.d: The descent speed for the first stage of landing in m/s. If this is zero then WP_SPD_DN is used

LAND_ALT_LOW_M.d: Altitude during Landing at which vehicle slows to LAND_SPD_MS

LGR_DEPLOY_PIN.d: Pin number to use for detection of gear deployment. If set to -1 feedback is disabled. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

LGR_DEPLOY_POL.d: Polarity for feedback pin. If this is 1 then the pin should be high when gear are deployed. If set to 0 then then deployed gear level is low.

LGR_WOW_PIN.d: Pin number to use for feedback of weight on wheels condition. If set to -1 feedback is disabled. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

LGR_WOW_POL.d: Polarity for feedback pin. If this is 1 then the pin should be high when there is weight on wheels. If set to 0 then then weight on wheels level is low.

LGR_DEPLOY_ALT.d: Altitude where the landing gear will be deployed. This should be lower than the RETRACT_ALT. If zero then altitude is not used for deploying landing gear. Only applies when vehicle is armed.

LGR_RETRACT_ALT.d: Altitude where the landing gear will be retracted. This should be higher than the DEPLOY_ALT. If zero then altitude is not used for retracting landing gear. Only applies when vehicle is armed.

LGR_OPTIONS.d: Options to retract or deploy landing gear in Auto or Guided mode

LOG_BACKEND_TYPE.d: Bitmap of what Logger backend types to enable. Block-based logging is available on SITL and boards with dataflash chips. Multiple backends can be selected.

LOG_FILE_BUFSIZE.d: The File and Block backends use a buffer to store data before writing to the block device.  Raising this value may reduce "gaps" in your SD card logging but increases memory usage.  This buffer size may be reduced to free up available memory

LOG_DISARMED.d: If LOG_DISARMED is set to 1 then logging will be enabled at all times including when disarmed. Logging before arming can make for very large logfiles but can help a lot when tracking down startup issues and is necessary if logging of EKF replay data is selected via the LOG_REPLAY parameter. If LOG_DISARMED is set to 2, then logging will be enabled when disarmed, but not if a USB connection is detected. This can be used to prevent unwanted data logs being generated when the vehicle is connected via USB for log downloading or parameter changes. If LOG_DISARMED is set to 3 then logging will happen while disarmed, but if the vehicle never arms then the logs using the filesystem backend will be discarded on the next boot.
LOG_DISARMED.r: 0:Disabled,1:Enabled,2:Disabled on USB connection,3:Discard log on reboot if never armed

options.label: Discard log on reboot if never armed

LOG_REPLAY.d: If LOG_REPLAY is set to 1 then the EKF2 and EKF3 state estimators will log detailed information needed for diagnosing problems with the Kalman filter. LOG_DISARMED must be set to 1 or 2 or else the log will not contain the pre-flight data required for replay testing of the EKF's. It is suggested that you also raise LOG_FILE_BUFSIZE to give more buffer space for logging and use a high quality microSD card to ensure no sensor data is lost.

LOG_FILE_DSRMROT.d: When set, the current log file is closed when the vehicle is disarmed.  If LOG_DISARMED is set then a fresh log will be opened. Applies to the File and Block logging backends.

LOG_MAV_BUFSIZE.d: Maximum amount of memory to allocate to AP_Logger-over-mavlink

LOG_FILE_TIMEOUT.d: This controls the amount of time before failing writes to a log file cause the file to be closed and logging stopped.

LOG_FILE_MB_FREE.d: Set this such that the free space is larger than your largest typical flight log

LOG_FILE_RATEMAX.d: This sets the maximum rate that streaming log messages will be logged to the file backend. A value of zero means that rate limiting is disabled.

LOG_MAV_RATEMAX.d: This sets the maximum rate that streaming log messages will be logged to the mavlink backend. A value of zero means that rate limiting is disabled.

LOG_BLK_RATEMAX.d: This sets the maximum rate that streaming log messages will be logged to the block backend. A value of zero means that rate limiting is disabled.

LOG_DARM_RATEMAX.d: This sets the maximum rate that streaming log messages will be logged to any backend when disarmed. A value of zero means that the normal backend rate limit is applied.

LOG_MAX_FILES.d: This sets the maximum number of log file that will be written on dataflash or sd card before starting to rotate log number. Limit is capped at 500 logs.

LOIT_ANG_MAX.d: Loiter maximum pilot requested lean angle. Set to zero for 2/3 of PSC_ANGLE_MAX/ATC_ANGLE_MAX. The maximum vehicle lean angle is still limited by PSC_ANGLE_MAX/ATC_ANGLE_MAX

LOIT_SPEED_MS.d: Defines the maximum speed in m/s which the aircraft will travel horizontally while in loiter mode

LOIT_ACC_MAX_M.d: Loiter maximum correction acceleration in m/s/s.  Higher values cause the copter to correct position errors more aggressively.

LOIT_BRK_ACC_M.d: Loiter braking acceleration in m/s/s. Higher values stop the copter more quickly when the stick is centered.

LOIT_BRK_JRK_M.d: Loiter braking jerk in m/s/s/s. Higher values will remove braking faster if the pilot moves the sticks during a braking maneuver.

MAV_SYSID.d: Allows setting an individual MAVLink system id for this vehicle to distinguish it from others on the same network.

MAV_GCS_SYSID.d: This sets what MAVLink source system IDs are accepted for GCS failsafe handling, RC overrides and manual control. When MAV_GCS_SYSID_HI is less than MAV_GCS_SYSID then only this value is considered to be a GCS. When MAV_GCS_SYSID_HI is greater than or equal to MAV_GCS_SYSID then the range of values between MAV_GCS_SYSID and MAV_GCS_SYSID_HI (inclusive) are all treated as valid GCS MAVLink system IDs

MAV_GCS_SYSID_HI.d: Upper limit of MAVLink source system IDs considered to be from the GCS. When this is less than MAV_GCS_SYSID then only MAV_GCS_SYSID is used as GCS ID. When this is greater than or equal to MAV_GCS_SYSID then the range of values from MAV_GCS_SYSID to MAV_GCS_SYSID_HI (inclusive) is treated as a GCS ID.

MAV_OPTIONS.d: Alters various behaviour of the MAVLink interface

bitmask.label: Accept MAVLink only from system IDs given by MAV_SYSID_GCS and MAV_SYSID_GCS_HI

MAV_TELEM_DELAY.d: The amount of time (in seconds) to delay radio telemetry to prevent an Xbee bricking on power up

MAV31_EXTRA3.d: MAVLink Stream rate of AHRS, SYSTEM_TIME, WIND, RANGEFINDER, DISTANCE_SENSOR, TERRAIN_REQUEST, TERRAIN_REPORT, GIMBAL_DEVICE_ATTITUDE_STATUS, OPTICAL_FLOW, MAG_CAL_REPORT, MAG_CAL_PROGRESS, EKF_STATUS_REPORT, VIBRATION, and BATTERY_STATUS

MAV31_OPTIONS.d: Bitmask for configuring this telemetry channel. For having effect on all channels, set the relevant mask in all MAVx_OPTIONS parameters. Keep in mind that part of the flags may require a reboot to take action.

bitmask.label: forward mavlink packets that don't pass CRC

MAV32_RAW_SENS.d: MAVLink Stream rate of RAW_IMU, SCALED_IMU2, SCALED_IMU3, SCALED_PRESSURE, SCALED_PRESSURE2, SCALED_PRESSURE3 and AIRSPEED

MAV32_EXT_STAT.d: MAVLink Stream rate of SYS_STATUS, POWER_STATUS, MCU_STATUS, MEMINFO, CURRENT_WAYPOINT, GPS_RAW_INT, GPS_RTK (if available), GPS2_RAW_INT (if available), GPS2_RTK (if available), NAV_CONTROLLER_OUTPUT, FENCE_STATUS, and GLOBAL_TARGET_POS_INT

MAV32_RC_CHAN.d: MAVLink Stream rate of SERVO_OUTPUT_RAW and RC_CHANNELS

MAV32_RAW_CTRL.d: MAVLink Raw Control stream rate of SERVO_OUT

MAV32_POSITION.d: MAVLink Stream rate of GLOBAL_POSITION_INT and LOCAL_POSITION_NED

MAV32_EXTRA1.d: MAVLink Stream rate of ATTITUDE, SIMSTATE (SIM only), AHRS2, RPM, AOA_SSA, LANDING,ESC_TELEMETRY,EFI_STATUS, and PID_TUNING

MAV32_EXTRA3.d: MAVLink Stream rate of AHRS, SYSTEM_TIME, WIND, RANGEFINDER, DISTANCE_SENSOR, TERRAIN_REQUEST, TERRAIN_REPORT, GIMBAL_DEVICE_ATTITUDE_STATUS, OPTICAL_FLOW, MAG_CAL_REPORT, MAG_CAL_PROGRESS, EKF_STATUS_REPORT, VIBRATION, and BATTERY_STATUS

MAV32_OPTIONS.d: Bitmask for configuring this telemetry channel. For having effect on all channels, set the relevant mask in all MAVx_OPTIONS parameters. Keep in mind that part of the flags may require a reboot to take action.

bitmask.label: forward mavlink packets that don't pass CRC

MIS_TOTAL.d: The number of mission mission items that has been loaded by the ground station. Do not change this manually.

MIS_RESTART.d: Controls mission starting point when entering Auto mode (either restart from beginning of mission or resume from last command run)

MIS_OPTIONS.d: Bitmask of what options to use in missions. If the DontZeroCounter counter option is set than on completion of a jump loop the counter is left at zero, so the jump will not happen again if the loop is re-entered.

MNT1_DEFLT_MODE.d: Mount default operating mode on startup and after control is returned from autopilot

MNT1_RC_RATE.d: Pilot rate control's maximum rate.  Set to zero to use angle control

MNT1_RETRACT_X.d: Mount roll angle when in retracted position

MNT1_RETRACT_Y.d: Mount pitch angle when in retracted position

MNT1_RETRACT_Z.d: Mount yaw angle when in retracted position

MNT1_NEUTRAL_X.d: Mount roll angle when in neutral position

MNT1_NEUTRAL_Y.d: Mount pitch angle when in neutral position

MNT1_NEUTRAL_Z.d: Mount yaw angle when in neutral position

MNT1_LEAD_RLL.d: Servo mount roll angle output leads the vehicle angle by this amount of time based on current roll rate. Increase until the servo is responsive but does not overshoot

MNT1_LEAD_PTCH.d: Servo mount pitch angle output leads the vehicle angle by this amount of time based on current pitch rate. Increase until the servo is responsive but does not overshoot

MNT1_SYSID_DFLT.d: Default Target sysID for the mount to point to

MNT1_DEVID.d: Mount device ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

MNT1_OPTIONS.d: Mount options bitmask, note bit 2 only impacts RC targetting mode

bitmask.label: RC lock state from previous mode
Return to neutral angles on RC failsafe
Force FPV (bf) lock on roll and pitch

MNT2_DEFLT_MODE.d: Mount default operating mode on startup and after control is returned from autopilot

MNT2_RC_RATE.d: Pilot rate control's maximum rate.  Set to zero to use angle control

MNT2_RETRACT_X.d: Mount roll angle when in retracted position

MNT2_RETRACT_Y.d: Mount pitch angle when in retracted position

MNT2_RETRACT_Z.d: Mount yaw angle when in retracted position

MNT2_NEUTRAL_X.d: Mount roll angle when in neutral position

MNT2_NEUTRAL_Y.d: Mount pitch angle when in neutral position

MNT2_NEUTRAL_Z.d: Mount yaw angle when in neutral position

MNT2_LEAD_RLL.d: Servo mount roll angle output leads the vehicle angle by this amount of time based on current roll rate. Increase until the servo is responsive but does not overshoot

MNT2_LEAD_PTCH.d: Servo mount pitch angle output leads the vehicle angle by this amount of time based on current pitch rate. Increase until the servo is responsive but does not overshoot

MNT2_SYSID_DFLT.d: Default Target sysID for the mount to point to

MNT2_DEVID.d: Mount device ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

MNT2_OPTIONS.d: Mount options bitmask, note bit 2 only impacts RC targetting mode

bitmask.label: RC lock state from previous mode
Return to neutral angles on RC failsafe
Force FPV (bf) lock on roll and pitch

MOT_YAW_HEADROOM.d: Yaw control is given at least this pwm in microseconds range

MOT_THST_EXPO.d: Motor thrust curve exponent (0.0 for linear to 1.0 for second order curve)

MOT_SPIN_MAX.d: Point at which the thrust saturates expressed as a number from 0 to 1 in the entire output range

MOT_BAT_VOLT_MAX.d: Battery voltage compensation maximum voltage (voltage above this will have no additional scaling effect on thrust).  Recommend 4.2 * cell count, 0 = Disabled

MOT_BAT_VOLT_MIN.d: Battery voltage compensation minimum voltage (voltage below this will have no additional scaling effect on thrust).  Recommend 3.3 * cell count, 0 = Disabled

MOT_BAT_CURR_MAX.d: Maximum current over which maximum throttle is limited (0 = Disabled)

MOT_PWM_TYPE.d: This selects the output PWM type, allowing for normal PWM continuous output, OneShot, brushed or DShot motor output.PWMRange and PWMAngle are PWM special/rare cases for ESCs that dont calibrate normally (some Sub motors) or where each ESC must have its PWM range set individually using the Servo params instead of PWM_MIN/MAX parameters.

MOT_PWM_MIN.d: This sets the min PWM output value in microseconds that will ever be output to the motors

MOT_PWM_MAX.d: This sets the max PWM value in microseconds that will ever be output to the motors

MOT_SPIN_MIN.d: Point at which the thrust starts expressed as a number from 0 to 1 in the entire output range.  Should be higher than MOT_SPIN_ARM.

MOT_SPIN_ARM.d: Point at which the motors start to spin expressed as a number from 0 to 1 in the entire output range.  Should be lower than MOT_SPIN_MIN.

MOT_BAT_CURR_TC.d: Time constant used to limit the maximum current

MOT_THST_HOVER.d: Motor thrust needed to hover expressed as a number from 0 to 1

MOT_HOVER_LEARN.d: Enable/Disable automatic learning of hover throttle

MOT_SAFE_DISARM.d: Disables motor PWM output when disarmed
MOT_SAFE_DISARM.r: 0:PWM enabled while disarmed,1:PWM disabled while disarmed

MOT_YAW_SV_ANGLE.d: Yaw servo's maximum lean angle (Tricopter only)

MOT_SPOOL_TIME.d: Time in seconds to spool up the motors from zero to min throttle.

MOT_BOOST_SCALE.d: Booster motor output scaling factor vs main throttle.  The output to the BoostThrottle servo will be the main throttle times this scaling factor. A higher scaling factor will put more of the load on the booster motor. A value of 1 will set the BoostThrottle equal to the main throttle.

MOT_BAT_IDX.d: Which battery monitor should be used for doing compensation

MOT_SLEW_UP_TIME.d: Time in seconds to slew output from zero to full. This is used to limit the rate at which output can change. Range is constrained between 0 and 0.5.

MOT_SLEW_DN_TIME.d: Time in seconds to slew output from full to zero. This is used to limit the rate at which output can change.  Range is constrained between 0 and 0.5.

MOT_SAFE_TIME.d: Delay after arming before spool transitions are allowed, used to let ESCs/servos complete startup when PWM was disabled while disarmed.

MOT_SPOOL_TIM_DN.d: Time to ramp the throttle ceiling down toward zero. If 0, SPOOL_TIME is used.

MOT_IDLE_SEC.d: Delay after reaching ground idle when armed to allow ESC startup to complete.

MSP_OSD_NCELLS.d: Used for average cell voltage calculation

MSP_OPTIONS.d: A bitmask to set some MSP specific options: EnableTelemetryMode-allows "push" mode telemetry when only rx line of OSD ic connected to autopilot,  EnableBTFLFonts-uses indexes corresponding to Betaflight fonts if OSD uses those instead of ArduPilot fonts. EnableINAVFonts uses INAV fonts and overrides EnableBTFLFonts if that option is enabled.

NET_NETMASK.d: Allows setting static subnet mask. The value is a count of consecutive bits. Examples: 24 = 255.255.255.0, 16 = 255.255.0.0

NET_P1_TYPE.d: Port type for network serial port. For the two client types a valid destination IP address must be set. For the two server types either 0.0.0.0 or a local address can be used. The UDP client type will use broadcast if the IP is set to 255.255.255.255 and will use UDP multicast if the IP is in the multicast address range.

The UDP client type will use broadcast if the IP is set to 255.255.255.255 and will use UDP multicast if the IP is in the multicast address range.

NMEA_RATE_MS.d: NMEA Output rate. This controls the interval at which all the enabled NMEA messages are sent. Most NMEA systems expect 100ms (10Hz) or slower.

NMEA_MSG_EN.d: This is a bitmask of enabled NMEA messages. All messages will be sent consecutively at the same rate interval

NTF_LED_BRIGHT.d: Select the RGB LED brightness level. When USB is connected brightness will never be higher than low regardless of the setting.

NTF_BUZZ_TYPES.d: Controls what types of Buzzer will be enabled

NTF_LED_OVERRIDE.d: Specifies the source for the colours and brightness for the LED.  OutbackChallenge conforms to the MedicalExpress (https://uavchallenge.org/medical-express/) rules, essentially "Green" is disarmed (safe-to-approach), "Red" is armed (not safe-to-approach). Traffic light is a simplified color set, red when armed, yellow when the safety switch is not surpressing outputs (but disarmed), and green when outputs are surpressed and disarmed, the LED will blink faster if disarmed and failing arming checks.

NTF_DISPLAY_TYPE.d: This sets up the type of on-board I2C display. Disabled by default.

NTF_OREO_THEME.d: Enable/Disable Solo Oreo LED driver, 0 to disable, 1 for Aircraft theme, 2 for Rover theme

NTF_BUZZ_PIN.d: Enables to connect active buzzer to arbitrary pin. Requires 3-pin buzzer or additional MOSFET! Some the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

NTF_LED_TYPES.d: Controls what types of LEDs will be enabled

NTF_BUZZ_ON_LVL.d: Specifies pin level that indicates buzzer should play

NTF_BUZZ_VOLUME.d: Control the volume of the buzzer

NTF_LED_LEN.d: The number of Serial LED's to use for notifications (NeoPixel's and ProfiLED)

OA_MARGIN_MAX.d: Object Avoidance will ignore objects more than this many meters from vehicle

OA_OPTIONS.d: Bitmask which will govern vehicles behaviour while recovering from Obstacle Avoidance (i.e Avoidance is turned off after the path ahead is clear).

OA_BR_LOOKAHEAD.d: Object Avoidance will look this many meters ahead of vehicle

OA_BR_CONT_RATIO.d: BendyRuler will avoid changing bearing unless ratio of previous margin from obstacle (or fence) to present calculated margin is atleast this much.

OA_BR_CONT_ANGLE.d: BendyRuler will resist changing current bearing if the change in bearing is over this angle

OA_BR_TYPE.d: BendyRuler will search for clear path along the direction defined by this parameter

OA_DB_SIZE.d: OADatabase maximum number of points. Set to 0 to disable the OA Database. Larger means more points but is more cpu intensive to process

OA_DB_EXPIRE.d: OADatabase item timeout. The time an item will linger without any updates before it expires. Zero means never expires which is useful for a sent-once static environment but terrible for dynamic ones.

OA_DB_QUEUE_SIZE.d: OADatabase queue maximum number of points. This in an input buffer size. Larger means it can handle larger bursts of incoming data points to filter into the database. No impact on cpu, only RAM. Recommend larger for faster datalinks or for sensors that generate a lot of data.

OA_DB_OUTPUT.d: OADatabase output level to configure which database objects are sent to the ground station. All data is always available internally for avoidance algorithms.
OA_DB_OUTPUT.r: 0:Disabled,1:Send only HIGH importance items,2:Send HIGH and NORMAL importance items,3:Send all items

options.label: Send HIGH and NORMAL importance items

OA_DB_BEAM_WIDTH.d: Beam width of incoming lidar data, used to calculate a object radius if none is provided by the data source.

OA_DB_RADIUS_MIN.d: Minimum radius of objects held in database

OA_DB_DIST_MAX.d: Maximum distance of objects held in database.  Set to zero to disable the limits

OA_DB_ALT_MIN.d: OADatabase will reject obstacles if vehicle's altitude above home is below this parameter, in a 3 meter radius around home. Set 0 to disable this feature.

OSD_TYPE.d: OSD type. TXONLY makes the OSD parameter selection available to other modules even if there is no native OSD support on the board, for instance CRSF.

OSD_CHAN.d: This sets the channel used to switch different OSD screens.

OSD_SW_METHOD.d: This sets the method used to switch different OSD screens.
OSD_SW_METHOD.r: 0:switch to next screen if channel value was changed,1:select screen based on pwm ranges specified for each screen,2:switch to next screen after low to high transition and every 1s while channel value is high

options.label: switch to next screen if channel value was changed
select screen based on pwm ranges specified for each screen
switch to next screen after low to high transition and every 1s while channel value is high

OSD_OPTIONS.d: This sets options that change the display

bitmask.label: Convert feet to miles at 5280ft instead of 10000ft

OSD_FONT.d: This sets which OSD font to use. It is an integer from 0 to the number of fonts available

OSD_V_OFFSET.d: Sets vertical offset of the osd inside image

OSD_H_OFFSET.d: Sets horizontal offset of the osd inside image

OSD_W_RSSI.d: Set level at which RSSI item will flash (in positive % or negative dBm values as applicable). 30% or -100dBm are defaults.

OSD_W_NSAT.d: Set level at which NSAT item will flash

OSD_W_BATVOLT.d: Set level at which BAT_VOLT item will flash

OSD_UNITS.d: Sets the units to use in displaying items

OSD_ARM_SCR.d: Screen to be shown on Arm event. Zero to disable the feature.

OSD_DSARM_SCR.d: Screen to be shown on disarm event. Zero to disable the feature.

OSD_FS_SCR.d: Screen to be shown on failsafe event. Zero to disable the feature.

OSD_BTN_DELAY.d: Debounce time in ms for stick commanded parameter navigation.

OSD_W_TERR.d: Set level below which TER_HGT item will flash. -1 disables.

OSD_W_AVGCELLV.d: Set level at which AVGCELLV item will flash

OSD_CELL_COUNT.d: Used for average cell voltage display. -1 disables, 0 uses cell count autodetection for well charged LIPO/LIION batteries at connection, other values manually select cell count used.

OSD_W_RESTVOLT.d: Set level at which RESTVOLT item will flash

OSD_W_ACRVOLT.d: Set level at which ACRVOLT item will flash

OSD_W_LQ.d: Set level at which RC_LQ item will flash (%)

OSD_W_SNR.d: Set level at which RC_SNR item will flash (in db)

OSD_SB_H_OFS.d: Extends the spacing between the sidebar elements by this amount of columns. Positive values increases the width to the right of the screen.

OSD_SB_V_EXT.d: Increase of vertical length of the sidebar itens by this amount of lines. Applied equally both above and below the default setting.

OSD_TYPE2.d: OSD type 2. TXONLY makes the OSD parameter selection available to other modules even if there is no native OSD support on the board, for instance CRSF.

OSD1_CHAN_MIN.d: This sets the PWM lower limit for this screen

OSD1_CHAN_MAX.d: This sets the PWM upper limit for this screen

OSD1_HOME_EN.d: Displays distance and relative direction to HOME

OSD1_THROTTLE_EN.d: Displays actual throttle percentage being sent to motor(s)

OSD1_WIND_EN.d: Displays wind speed and relative direction, on Rover this is the apparent wind speed and direction from the windvane, if fitted

OSD1_ASPEED_EN.d: Displays airspeed value being used by TECS (fused value)

OSD1_ESCTEMP_EN.d: Displays highest temp of all active ESCs, or of a specific ECS if OSDx_ESC_IDX is set

OSD1_ESCRPM_EN.d: Displays highest rpm of all active ESCs, or of a specific ESC if OSDx_ESC_IDX is set

OSD1_ESCAMPS_EN.d: Displays the current of the ESC with the highest rpm of all active ESCs, or of a specific ESC if OSDx_ESC_IDX is set

OSD1_ROLL_EN.d: Displays degrees of roll from level

OSD1_PITCH_EN.d: Displays degrees of pitch from level

OSD1_TEMP_EN.d: Displays temperature reported by primary barometer

OSD1_WAYPOINT_EN.d: Displays bearing and distance to next waypoint

OSD1_BTEMP_EN.d: Displays temperature reported by secondary barometer

OSD1_ATEMP_EN.d: Displays temperature reported by primary airspeed sensor

OSD1_ASPD2_EN.d: Displays airspeed reported directly from secondary airspeed sensor

OSD1_ASPD1_EN.d: Displays airspeed reported directly from primary airspeed sensor

OSD1_CLK_EN.d: Displays a clock panel based on AP_RTC local time

OSD1_CRSSHAIR_EN.d: Displays artificial horizon crosshair (MSP OSD only)

OSD1_CRSSHAIR_X.d: Horizontal position on screen (MSP OSD only)

OSD1_CRSSHAIR_Y.d: Vertical position on screen (MSP OSD only)

OSD1_HOMEDIST_EN.d: Displays distance from HOME (MSP OSD only)

OSD1_HOMEDIST_X.d: Horizontal position on screen (MSP OSD only)

OSD1_HOMEDIST_Y.d: Vertical position on screen (MSP OSD only)

OSD1_HOMEDIR_EN.d: Displays relative direction to HOME (MSP OSD only)

OSD1_CELLVOLT_EN.d: Displays average cell voltage (MSP OSD only)

OSD1_BATTBAR_EN.d: Displays battery usage bar (MSP OSD only)

OSD1_CALLSIGN_EN.d: Displays callsign from callsign.txt on microSD card

OSD1_AVGCELLV_EN.d: Displays average cell voltage. WARNING: this can be inaccurate if the cell count is not detected or set properly. If the  the battery is far from fully charged the detected cell count might not be accurate if auto cell count detection is used (OSD_CELL_COUNT=0).

OSD1_FENCE_EN.d: Displays indication of fence enable and breach

OSD1_RNGF_EN.d: Displays a rangefinder's distance in cm

OSD1_ACRVOLT_EN.d: Displays resting voltage for the average cell. WARNING: this can be inaccurate if the cell count is not detected or set properly. If the  the battery is far from fully charged the detected cell count might not be accurate if auto cell count detection is used (OSD_CELL_COUNT=0).

OSD1_TXT_RES.d: Sets the overlay text resolution for this screen to either SD 30x16 or HD 50x18/60x22 (MSP DisplayPort only)

OSD1_FONT.d: Sets the font index for this screen (MSP DisplayPort only)

OSD1_RC_PWR_EN.d: Displays the RC link transmit (TX) power in mW or W, depending on level

OSD1_RSSIDBM_EN.d: Displays RC link signal strength in dBm

OSD1_RC_SNR_EN.d: Displays RC link signal to noise ratio in dB

OSD1_RC_ANT_EN.d: Displays the current RC link active antenna

OSD1_RC_LQ_EN.d: Displays the RC link quality (uplink, 0 to 100%) and also RF mode if bit 7 of OSD_OPTIONS is set

OSD1_ESC_IDX.d: Index of the ESC to use for displaying ESC information. 0 means use the ESC with the highest value.

OSD2_CHAN_MIN.d: This sets the PWM lower limit for this screen

OSD2_CHAN_MAX.d: This sets the PWM upper limit for this screen

OSD2_HOME_EN.d: Displays distance and relative direction to HOME

OSD2_THROTTLE_EN.d: Displays actual throttle percentage being sent to motor(s)

OSD2_WIND_EN.d: Displays wind speed and relative direction, on Rover this is the apparent wind speed and direction from the windvane, if fitted

OSD2_ASPEED_EN.d: Displays airspeed value being used by TECS (fused value)

OSD2_ESCTEMP_EN.d: Displays highest temp of all active ESCs, or of a specific ECS if OSDx_ESC_IDX is set

OSD2_ESCRPM_EN.d: Displays highest rpm of all active ESCs, or of a specific ESC if OSDx_ESC_IDX is set

OSD2_ESCAMPS_EN.d: Displays the current of the ESC with the highest rpm of all active ESCs, or of a specific ESC if OSDx_ESC_IDX is set

OSD2_ROLL_EN.d: Displays degrees of roll from level

OSD2_PITCH_EN.d: Displays degrees of pitch from level

OSD2_TEMP_EN.d: Displays temperature reported by primary barometer

OSD2_WAYPOINT_EN.d: Displays bearing and distance to next waypoint

OSD2_BTEMP_EN.d: Displays temperature reported by secondary barometer

OSD2_ATEMP_EN.d: Displays temperature reported by primary airspeed sensor

OSD2_ASPD2_EN.d: Displays airspeed reported directly from secondary airspeed sensor

OSD2_ASPD1_EN.d: Displays airspeed reported directly from primary airspeed sensor

OSD2_CLK_EN.d: Displays a clock panel based on AP_RTC local time

OSD2_CRSSHAIR_EN.d: Displays artificial horizon crosshair (MSP OSD only)

OSD2_CRSSHAIR_X.d: Horizontal position on screen (MSP OSD only)

OSD2_CRSSHAIR_Y.d: Vertical position on screen (MSP OSD only)

OSD2_HOMEDIST_EN.d: Displays distance from HOME (MSP OSD only)

PHLD_BRK_ANGLE.d: PosHold flight mode's max lean angle during braking in degrees

PLND_YAW_ALIGN.d: Yaw angle from body x-axis to sensor x-axis.

PLND_LAND_OFS_X.d: Desired landing position of the camera forward of the target in vehicle body frame

PLND_LAND_OFS_Y.d: desired landing position of the camera right of the target in vehicle body frame

PLND_EST_TYPE.d: Specifies the estimation method to be used

PLND_ACC_P_NSE.d: Kalman Filter Accelerometer Noise, higher values weight the input from the camera more, accels less

PLND_CAM_POS_X.d: X position of the camera in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin.

PLND_CAM_POS_Y.d: Y position of the camera in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin.

PLND_CAM_POS_Z.d: Z position of the camera in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin.

PLND_BUS.d: Precland sensor bus for I2C sensors.

PLND_LAG.d: Precision Landing sensor lag, to cope with variable landing_target latency

PLND_XY_DIST_MAX.d: The vehicle will not start descending if the landing target is detected and it is further than this many meters away. Set 0 to always descend.

PLND_STRICT.d: How strictly should the vehicle land on the target if target is lost
PLND_STRICT.r: 0:Land Vertically (Not strict),1:Retry Landing(Normal Strictness),2:Do not land (just Hover) (Very Strict)

PLND_RET_MAX.d: PrecLand Maximum number of retires for a failed landing. Set to zero to disable landing retry.

PLND_TIMEOUT.d: Time for which vehicle continues descend even if target is lost. After this time period, vehicle will attempt a landing retry depending on PLND_STRICT parameter.

PLND_RET_BEHAVE.d: Prec Land will do the action selected by this parameter if a retry to a landing is needed
PLND_RET_BEHAVE.r: 0:Go to the last location where landing target was detected,1:Go towards the approximate location of the detected landing target

options.label: Go to the last location where landing target was detected
Go towards the approximate location of the detected landing target

PLND_ALT_MIN.d: Vehicle will continue landing vertically even if target is lost below this height. This needs a rangefinder to work. Set to zero to disable this.

PLND_ALT_MAX.d: Vehicle will continue landing vertically until this height if target is not found. Below this height if landing target is not found, landing retry/failsafe might be attempted. This needs a rangefinder to work. Set to zero to disable this.

bitmask.label: Allow Precision Landing after manual reposition
Maintain high speed in final descent

PRX_IGN_GND.d: Ignore proximity data that is within 1 meter of the ground below the vehicle. This requires a downward facing rangefinder

PRX_LOG_RAW.d: Set this parameter to one if logging unfiltered(raw) distances from sensor should be enabled

PRX_FILT.d: Cutoff frequency for low pass filter applied to each face in the proximity boundary

PRX_ALT_MIN.d: Minimum altitude below which proximity should not work.

PRX1_TYPE.d: What type of proximity sensor is connected

PRX1_MIN.d: Minimum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX1_MAX.d: Maximum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX1_ADDR.d: The bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

PRX1_RECV_ID.d: The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

PRX2_TYPE.d: What type of proximity sensor is connected

PRX2_MIN.d: Minimum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX2_MAX.d: Maximum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX2_ADDR.d: The bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

PRX2_RECV_ID.d: The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

PRX3_TYPE.d: What type of proximity sensor is connected

PRX3_MIN.d: Minimum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX3_MAX.d: Maximum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX3_ADDR.d: The bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

PRX3_RECV_ID.d: The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

PRX4_TYPE.d: What type of proximity sensor is connected

PRX4_MIN.d: Minimum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX4_MAX.d: Maximum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX4_ADDR.d: The bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

PRX4_RECV_ID.d: The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

PRX5_TYPE.d: What type of proximity sensor is connected

PRX5_MIN.d: Minimum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX5_MAX.d: Maximum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX5_ADDR.d: The bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

PRX5_RECV_ID.d: The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

PSC_D_POS_P.d: Position (vertical) controller P gain. Converts the difference between the desired altitude and actual altitude into a climb or descent rate which is passed to the throttle rate controller. Previously _POSZ_P.

PSC_NE_POS_P.d: Position controller P gain. Converts the distance (in the latitude direction) to the target location into a desired speed which is then passed to the loiter latitude rate controller. Previously _POSXY_P.

PSC_ANGLE_MAX.d: Maximum lean angle autopilot can request. Set to zero to use ANGLE_MAX parameter value

PSC_JERK_NE.d: Jerk limit of the horizontal kinematic path generation used to determine how quickly the aircraft varies the acceleration target

PSC_JERK_D.d: Jerk limit of the vertical kinematic path generation used to determine how quickly the aircraft varies the acceleration target

PSC_D_VEL_P.d: Velocity (vertical) controller P gain. Converts the difference between desired vertical speed and actual speed into a desired acceleration that is passed to the throttle acceleration controller. Previously _VELZ_P.

PSC_D_VEL_I.d: Velocity (vertical) controller I gain. Corrects long-term difference in desired velocity to a target acceleration. Previously _VELZ_I.

PSC_D_VEL_IMAX.d: Velocity (vertical) controller I gain maximum. Constrains the target acceleration that the I gain will output. If upgrading from 4.6 this is _VELZ_IMAX * 0.01.

PSC_D_VEL_D.d: Velocity (vertical) controller D gain. Corrects short-term changes in velocity. Previously _VELZ_D.

PSC_D_VEL_FF.d: Velocity (vertical) controller Feed Forward gain. Produces an output that is proportional to the magnitude of the target. Previously _VELZ_FF.

PSC_D_VEL_FLTE.d: Velocity (vertical) error filter. This filter (in Hz) is applied to the input for P and I terms. Previously _VELZ_FLTE.

PSC_D_VEL_FLTD.d: Velocity (vertical) input filter for D term. This filter (in Hz) is applied to the input for D terms. Previously _VELZ_FLTD.

PSC_D_ACC_P.d: Acceleration (vertical) controller P gain. Converts the difference between desired vertical acceleration and actual acceleration into a motor output. If upgrading from 4.6 this is _ACCZ_P * 0.1.

PSC_D_ACC_I.d: Acceleration (vertical) controller I gain. Corrects long-term difference in desired vertical acceleration and actual acceleration. If upgrading from 4.6 this is _ACCZ_I * 0.1.

PSC_D_ACC_IMAX.d: Acceleration (vertical) controller I gain maximum. Constrains the maximum pwm that the I term will generate. If upgrading from 4.6 this is _ACCZ_IMAX * 0.001.

PSC_D_ACC_D.d: Acceleration (vertical) controller D gain. Compensates for short-term change in desired vertical acceleration vs actual acceleration. If upgrading from 4.6 this is _ACCZ_D * 0.1.

PSC_D_ACC_FF.d: Acceleration (vertical) controller feed forward. If upgrading from 4.6 this is _ACCZ_FF * 0.1.

PSC_D_ACC_FLTT.d: Acceleration (vertical) controller target frequency in Hz. Previously _ACCZ_FLTT.

PSC_D_ACC_FLTE.d: Acceleration (vertical) controller error frequency in Hz. Previously _ACCZ_FLTE.

PSC_D_ACC_FLTD.d: Acceleration (vertical) controller derivative frequency in Hz. Previously _ACCZ_FLTD.

PSC_D_ACC_SMAX.d: Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

PSC_D_ACC_PDMX.d: Acceleration (vertical) controller PD sum maximum. The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output. If upgrading from 4.6 this is _ACCZ_P * 0.1.

PSC_D_ACC_D_FF.d: FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target. If upgrading from 4.6 this is _ACCZ_P * 0.1.

PSC_D_ACC_NTF.d: Accel (vertical) Target notch filter index. If upgrading from 4.6 this is Previously _ACCZ_NTF.

PSC_D_ACC_NEF.d: Accel (vertical) Error notch filter index. If upgrading from 4.6 this is Previously _ACCZ_NEF.

PSC_NE_VEL_P.d: Velocity (horizontal) P gain. Converts the difference between desired and actual velocity to a target acceleration. Previously _VELXY_P.

PSC_NE_VEL_I.d: Velocity (horizontal) I gain. Corrects long-term difference between desired and actual velocity to a target acceleration. Previously _VELXY_I.

PSC_NE_VEL_D.d: Velocity (horizontal) D gain. Corrects short-term changes in velocity. Previously _VELXY_D.

PSC_NE_VEL_IMAX.d: Velocity (horizontal) integrator maximum. Constrains the target acceleration that the I gain will output. If upgrading from 4.6 this is _VELXY_IMAX * 0.01.

PSC_NE_VEL_FLTE.d: Velocity (horizontal) input filter. This filter (in Hz) is applied to the input for P and I terms. Previously _VELXY_FLTE.

PSC_NE_VEL_FLTD.d: Velocity (horizontal) input filter. This filter (in Hz) is applied to the input for D term. Previously _VELXY_FLTD.

PSC_NE_VEL_FF.d: Velocity (horizontal) feed forward gain. Converts the difference between desired velocity to a target acceleration. Previously _VELXY_FF.

RALLY_TOTAL.d: Number of rally points currently loaded

RALLY_LIMIT_KM.d: Maximum distance to rally point. If the closest rally point is more than this number of kilometers from the current position and the home location is closer than any of the rally points from the current position then do RTL to home rather than to the closest rally point. This prevents a leftover rally point from a different airfield being used accidentally. If this is set to 0 then the closest rally point is always used.

RALLY_INCL_HOME.d: Controls if Home is included as a Rally point (i.e. as a safe landing place) for RTL

RC_OVERRIDE_TIME.d: Timeout after which RC overrides will no longer be used, and RC input will resume, 0 will disable RC overrides, -1 will never timeout, and continue using overrides until they are disabled

bitmask.label: Ignore Receiver Failsafe bit but allow other RC failsafes if setup
Skip the arming check for neutral Roll/Pitch/Yaw sticks
Suppress CRSF mode/rate message for ELRS systems
Use Link Quality for RSSI with CRSF
Annotate CRSF flight mode with * on disarm

RC_PROTOCOLS.d: Bitmask of enabled RC protocols. Allows narrowing the protocol detection to only specific types of RC receivers which can avoid issues with incorrect detection. Set to 1 to enable all protocols.

RC_FS_TIMEOUT.d: RC failsafe will trigger this many seconds after loss of RC

RC1_TRIM.d: RC trim (neutral) PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

RC1_MAX.d: RC maximum PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

RC1_REVERSED.d: Reverse channel input. Set to 0 for normal operation. Set to 1 to reverse this input channel.

RC1_DZ.d: PWM dead zone in microseconds around trim or bottom
