RCMAP_ROLL.d: Roll channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Roll is normally on channel 1, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter.  Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

RCMAP_PITCH.d: Pitch channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Pitch is normally on channel 2, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter.  Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

RCMAP_THROTTLE.d: Throttle channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Throttle is normally on channel 3, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter. Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

RCMAP_YAW.d: Yaw channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Yaw (also known as rudder) is normally on channel 4, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter.  Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

RELAY1_FUNCTION.d: The function the relay channel is mapped to.

RELAY1_PIN.d: Digital pin number for relay control. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

RELAY1_DEFAULT.d: Should the relay default to on or off, this only applies to RELAYx_FUNC "Relay" (1). All other uses will pick the appropriate default output state from within the controlling function's parameters. Note that if INVERTED is set then the default is inverted.

RELAY1_INVERTED.d: Should the relay output signal be inverted. If enabled, relay on would be pin low and relay off would be pin high. NOTE: this impact's DEFAULT.

RNGFND1_PIN.d: Analog or PWM input pin that rangefinder is connected to. Analog RSSI or Airspeed ports can be used for Analog inputs (some autopilots provide others also), Non-IOMCU Servo/MotorOutputs can be used for PWM input when configured as "GPIOs". Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins" for analog pin or "GPIOs", if PWM input type, to determine pin number.

RNGFND1_SCALING.d: Scaling factor between rangefinder reading and distance. For the linear and inverted functions this is in meters per volt. For the hyperbolic function the units are meterVolts. For Maxbotix serial sonar this is unit conversion to meters.

RNGFND1_OFFSET.d: Offset in volts for zero distance for analog rangefinders. Offset added to distance in centimeters for PWM lidars

RNGFND1_FUNCTION.d: Control over what function is used to calculate distance. For a linear function, the distance is (voltage-offset)*scaling. For a inverted function the distance is (offset-voltage)*scaling. For a hyperbolic function the distance is scaling/(voltage-offset). The functions return the distance in meters.

RNGFND1_MIN.d: Minimum distance in metres that rangefinder can reliably read

RNGFND1_MAX.d: Maximum distance in metres that rangefinder can reliably read

RNGFND1_STOP_PIN.d: Digital pin that enables/disables rangefinder measurement for the pwm rangefinder. A value of -1 means no pin. If this is set, then the pin is set to 1 to enable the rangefinder and set to 0 to disable it. This is used to enable powersaving when out of range. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

RNGFND1_RMETRIC.d: This parameter sets whether an analog rangefinder is ratiometric. Most analog rangefinders are ratiometric, meaning that their output voltage is influenced by the supply voltage. Some analog rangefinders (such as the SF/02) have their own internal voltage regulators so they are not ratiometric.

RNGFND1_PWRRNG.d: This parameter sets the estimated terrain distance in meters above which the sensor will be put into a power saving mode (if available). A value of zero means power saving is not enabled

RNGFND1_GNDCLR.d: This parameter sets the expected range measurement that the range finder should return when the vehicle is on the ground.

RNGFND1_ADDR.d: This sets the bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

RNGFND1_POS_X.d: X position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFND1_POS_Y.d: Y position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFND1_POS_Z.d: Z position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFND1_WSP_MAVG.d: Sets the number of historic range results to use for calculating the current range result. When MAVG is greater than 1, the current range result will be the current measured value averaged with the N-1 previous results

RNGFND1_WSP_MEDF.d: Sets the window size for the real-time median filter. When MEDF is greater than 0 the median filter is active

RNGFND1_WSP_FRQ.d: Sets the repetition frequency of the ranging operation in Hertz. Upon entering the desired frequency the system will calculate the nearest frequency that it can handle according to the resolution of internal timers.

RNGFND1_WSP_AVG.d: Sets the number of pulses to be used in multi-pulse averaging mode. In this mode, a sequence of rapid fire ranges are taken and then averaged to improve the accuracy of the measurement

RNGFND1_WSP_THR.d: Sets the system sensitivity. Larger values of THR represent higher sensitivity. The system may limit the maximum value of THR to prevent excessive false alarm rates based on settings made at the factory. Set to -1 for automatic threshold adjustments

RNGFND1_RECV_ID.d: The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

RNGFND1_SNR_MIN.d: RangeFinder Minimum signal strength (SNR) to accept distance

RNGFND1_GRF_RET.d: Selects which single return to use.

RNGFND1_GRF_ST.d: Minimum acceptable return signal strength in dB. Returns weaker than this will be ignored. Set to 0 to disable filtering.

RNGFND1_GRF_RATE.d: The update rate of the sensor in Hz. Must match the

RNGFND2_PIN.d: Analog or PWM input pin that rangefinder is connected to. Analog RSSI or Airspeed ports can be used for Analog inputs (some autopilots provide others also), Non-IOMCU Servo/MotorOutputs can be used for PWM input when configured as "GPIOs". Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins" for analog pin or "GPIOs", if PWM input type, to determine pin number.

RNGFND2_SCALING.d: Scaling factor between rangefinder reading and distance. For the linear and inverted functions this is in meters per volt. For the hyperbolic function the units are meterVolts. For Maxbotix serial sonar this is unit conversion to meters.

RNGFND2_OFFSET.d: Offset in volts for zero distance for analog rangefinders. Offset added to distance in centimeters for PWM lidars

RNGFND2_FUNCTION.d: Control over what function is used to calculate distance. For a linear function, the distance is (voltage-offset)*scaling. For a inverted function the distance is (offset-voltage)*scaling. For a hyperbolic function the distance is scaling/(voltage-offset). The functions return the distance in meters.

RNGFND2_MIN.d: Minimum distance in metres that rangefinder can reliably read

RNGFND2_MAX.d: Maximum distance in metres that rangefinder can reliably read

RNGFND2_STOP_PIN.d: Digital pin that enables/disables rangefinder measurement for the pwm rangefinder. A value of -1 means no pin. If this is set, then the pin is set to 1 to enable the rangefinder and set to 0 to disable it. This is used to enable powersaving when out of range. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

RNGFND2_RMETRIC.d: This parameter sets whether an analog rangefinder is ratiometric. Most analog rangefinders are ratiometric, meaning that their output voltage is influenced by the supply voltage. Some analog rangefinders (such as the SF/02) have their own internal voltage regulators so they are not ratiometric.

RNGFND2_PWRRNG.d: This parameter sets the estimated terrain distance in meters above which the sensor will be put into a power saving mode (if available). A value of zero means power saving is not enabled

RNGFND2_GNDCLR.d: This parameter sets the expected range measurement that the range finder should return when the vehicle is on the ground.

RNGFND8_PWRRNG.d: This parameter sets the estimated terrain distance in meters above which the sensor will be put into a power saving mode (if available). A value of zero means power saving is not enabled

RNGFND8_GNDCLR.d: This parameter sets the expected range measurement that the range finder should return when the vehicle is on the ground.

RNGFND8_ADDR.d: This sets the bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

RNGFND8_POS_X.d: X position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFND8_POS_Y.d: Y position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFND8_POS_Z.d: Z position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFND8_WSP_MAVG.d: Sets the number of historic range results to use for calculating the current range result. When MAVG is greater than 1, the current range result will be the current measured value averaged with the N-1 previous results

RNGFND8_WSP_MEDF.d: Sets the window size for the real-time median filter. When MEDF is greater than 0 the median filter is active

RNGFND8_WSP_FRQ.d: Sets the repetition frequency of the ranging operation in Hertz. Upon entering the desired frequency the system will calculate the nearest frequency that it can handle according to the resolution of internal timers.

RNGFND8_WSP_AVG.d: Sets the number of pulses to be used in multi-pulse averaging mode. In this mode, a sequence of rapid fire ranges are taken and then averaged to improve the accuracy of the measurement

RNGFND8_WSP_THR.d: Sets the system sensitivity. Larger values of THR represent higher sensitivity. The system may limit the maximum value of THR to prevent excessive false alarm rates based on settings made at the factory. Set to -1 for automatic threshold adjustments

RNGFND8_RECV_ID.d: The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

RNGFND8_SNR_MIN.d: RangeFinder Minimum signal strength (SNR) to accept distance

RNGFND8_GRF_RET.d: Selects which single return to use.

RNGFND8_GRF_ST.d: Minimum acceptable return signal strength in dB. Returns weaker than this will be ignored. Set to 0 to disable filtering.

RNGFND8_GRF_RATE.d: The update rate of the sensor in Hz. Must match the

RNGFND9_PIN.d: Analog or PWM input pin that rangefinder is connected to. Analog RSSI or Airspeed ports can be used for Analog inputs (some autopilots provide others also), Non-IOMCU Servo/MotorOutputs can be used for PWM input when configured as "GPIOs". Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins" for analog pin or "GPIOs", if PWM input type, to determine pin number.

RNGFND9_SCALING.d: Scaling factor between rangefinder reading and distance. For the linear and inverted functions this is in meters per volt. For the hyperbolic function the units are meterVolts. For Maxbotix serial sonar this is unit conversion to meters.

RNGFND9_OFFSET.d: Offset in volts for zero distance for analog rangefinders. Offset added to distance in centimeters for PWM lidars

RNGFND9_FUNCTION.d: Control over what function is used to calculate distance. For a linear function, the distance is (voltage-offset)*scaling. For a inverted function the distance is (offset-voltage)*scaling. For a hyperbolic function the distance is scaling/(voltage-offset). The functions return the distance in meters.

RNGFND9_MIN.d: Minimum distance in metres that rangefinder can reliably read

RNGFND9_MAX.d: Maximum distance in metres that rangefinder can reliably read

RNGFND9_STOP_PIN.d: Digital pin that enables/disables rangefinder measurement for the pwm rangefinder. A value of -1 means no pin. If this is set, then the pin is set to 1 to enable the rangefinder and set to 0 to disable it. This is used to enable powersaving when out of range. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

RPM1_TYPE.d: What type of RPM sensor is connected

RPM1_SCALING.d: Scaling factor between sensor reading and RPM.

RPM1_MAX.d: Maximum RPM to report. Only used on type = GPIO.

RPM1_MIN.d: Minimum RPM to report. Only used on type = GPIO.

RPM1_MIN_QUAL.d: Minimum data quality to be used

RPM1_PIN.d: Which digital GPIO pin to use. Only used on type = GPIO. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

RPM1_ESC_MASK.d: Mask of channels which support ESC rpm telemetry. RPM telemetry of the selected channels will be averaged

RPM1_ESC_INDEX.d: ESC Telemetry Index to write RPM to. Use 0 to disable.

RPM1_DC_ID.d: DroneCAN sensor ID to assign to this backend

RPM2_ESC_INDEX.d: ESC Telemetry Index to write RPM to. Use 0 to disable.

RPM2_DC_ID.d: DroneCAN sensor ID to assign to this backend

RSSI_TYPE.d: Radio Receiver RSSI type. If your radio receiver supports RSSI of some kind, set it here, then set its associated RSSI_XXXXX parameters, if any.

RSSI_ANA_PIN.d: Pin used to read the RSSI voltage or PWM value. Analog Airspeed ports can be used for Analog inputs (some autopilots provide others also), Non-IOMCU Servo/MotorOutputs can be used for PWM input when configured as "GPIOs". Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins" for analog pin or "GPIOs", if PWM input type, to determine pin number.

RSSI_PIN_LOW.d: RSSI pin's voltage received on the RSSI_ANA_PIN when the signal strength is the weakest. Some radio receivers put out inverted values so this value may be higher than RSSI_PIN_HIGH. When using pin 103, the maximum value of the parameter is 3.3V.

RSSI_PIN_HIGH.d: RSSI pin's voltage received on the RSSI_ANA_PIN when the signal strength is the strongest. Some radio receivers put out inverted values so this value may be lower than RSSI_PIN_LOW. When using pin 103, the maximum value of the parameter is 3.3V.

RSSI_CHANNEL.d: The channel number where RSSI will be output by the radio receiver (5 and above).

RSSI_CHAN_LOW.d: PWM value that the radio receiver will put on the RSSI_CHANNEL or RSSI_ANA_PIN when the signal strength is the weakest. Some radio receivers output inverted values so this value may be lower than RSSI_CHAN_HIGH

RSSI_CHAN_HIGH.d: PWM value that the radio receiver will put on the RSSI_CHANNEL or RSSI_ANA_PIN when the signal strength is the strongest. Some radio receivers output inverted values so this value may be higher than RSSI_CHAN_LOW

RTL_ALT_M.d: The minimum alt above home the vehicle will climb to before returning. If the vehicle is flying higher than this value it will return at its current altitude.

RTL_ALT_FINAL_M.d: Altitude the vehicle will move to as the final stage of Returning to Launch or after completing a mission. Set to zero to land.

RTL_CLIMB_MIN_M.d: The vehicle will climb this many meters during the initial climb portion of the RTL

RTL_SPEED_MS.d: The speed in m/s which the aircraft will attempt to maintain horizontally while flying home. If this is set to zero, WP_SPD will be used instead.

SCHED_DEBUG.d: Set to non-zero to enable scheduler debug messages. When set to show "Slips" the scheduler will display a message whenever a scheduled task is delayed due to too much CPU load. When set to ShowOverruns the scheduled will display a message whenever a task takes longer than the limit promised in the task table.

SCHED_LOOP_RATE.d: This controls the rate of the main control loop in Hz. This should only be changed by developers. This only takes effect on restart. Values over 400 are considered highly experimental.

SCHED_OPTIONS.d: This controls optional aspects of the scheduler.

SCR_VM_I_COUNT.d: The number virtual machine instructions that can be run before considering a script to have taken an excessive amount of time

SCR_HEAP_SIZE.d: Amount of memory available for scripting

bitmask.label: No Scripts to run message if all scripts have stopped

log runtime memory usage and execution time
Save CRC of current scripts to loaded and running checksum parameters enabling pre-arm
Disable heap expansion on allocation failure

SCR_USER1.d: General purpose user variable input for scripts

SCR_USER2.d: General purpose user variable input for scripts

SCR_USER3.d: General purpose user variable input for scripts

SCR_USER4.d: General purpose user variable input for scripts

SCR_USER5.d: General purpose user variable input for scripts

SCR_USER6.d: General purpose user variable input for scripts

SCR_DIR_DISABLE.d: This will stop scripts being loaded from the given locations

SCR_LD_CHECKSUM.d: Required XOR of CRC32 checksum of loaded scripts, vehicle will not arm with incorrect scripts loaded, -1 disables

SCR_RUN_CHECKSUM.d: Required XOR of CRC32 checksum of running scripts, vehicle will not arm with incorrect scripts running, -1 disables

SCR_THD_PRIORITY.d: This sets the priority of the scripting thread. This is normally set to a low priority to prevent scripts from interfering with other parts of the system. Advanced users can change this priority if scripting needs to be prioritised for realtime applications. WARNING: changing this parameter can impact the stability of your flight controller. The scipting thread priority in this parameter is chosen based on a set of system level priorities for other subsystems. It is strongly recommended that you use the lowest priority that is sufficient for your application. Note that all scripts run at the same priority, so if you raise this priority you must carefully audit all lua scripts for behaviour that does not interfere with the operation of the system.
SCR_THD_PRIORITY.r: 0:Normal,1:IO Priority,2:Storage Priority,3:UART Priority,4:I2C Priority,5:SPI Priority,6:Timer Priority,7:Main Priority,8:Boost Priority

SERIAL1_OPTIONS.d: Control over UART options. The InvertRX option controls invert of the receive pin. The InvertTX option controls invert of the transmit pin. The HalfDuplex option controls half-duplex (onewire) mode, where both transmit and receive is done on the transmit wire. The Swap option allows the RX and TX pins to be swapped on STM32F7 based boards.  NOTE that two bits have moved from this parameter into MAVn_OPTIONS!

bitmask.label: Don't forward mavlink to/from (moved to MAVn_OPTIONS >4.7)

SERIAL2_OPTIONS.d: Control over UART options. The InvertRX option controls invert of the receive pin. The InvertTX option controls invert of the transmit pin. The HalfDuplex option controls half-duplex (onewire) mode, where both transmit and receive is done on the transmit wire. The Swap option allows the RX and TX pins to be swapped on STM32F7 based boards.  NOTE that two bits have moved from this parameter into MAVn_OPTIONS!

SERIAL_PASS1.d: This sets one side of pass-through between two serial ports. Once both sides are set then all data received on either port will be passed to the other port

SERIAL_PASS2.d: This sets one side of pass-through between two serial ports. Once both sides are set then all data received on either port will be passed to the other port. This parameter is normally reset to -1 on reboot, disabling passthrough. If SERIAL_PASSTIMO is set to -1 then it is not reset on reboot.

SERIAL_PASSTIMO.d: This sets a timeout for serial pass-through in seconds. When the pass-through is enabled by setting the SERIAL_PASS1 and SERIAL_PASS2 parameters then it remains in effect until no data comes from the first port for SERIAL_PASSTIMO seconds. This allows the port to revent to its normal usage (such as MAVLink connection to a GCS) when it is no longer needed. A value of 0 means no timeout. A value of -1 means no timeout and the SERIAL_PASS2 parameter is not reset on reboot.

SERVO_RATE.d: Default output rate in Hz for all PWM outputs.

SERVO_DSHOT_RATE.d: DShot output rate for all outputs as a multiple of the loop rate. 0 sets the output rate to be fixed at 1Khz for low loop rates. This value should never be set below 500Hz.

SERVO_DSHOT_ESC.d: DShot ESC type for all outputs. The ESC type affects the range of DShot commands available and the bit widths used. None means that no dshot commands will be executed. Some ESC types support Extended DShot Telemetry (EDT) which allows telemetry other than RPM data to be returned when using bi-directional dshot. If you enable EDT you must install EDT capable firmware for correct operation.

SERVO_GPIO_MASK.d: Bitmask of outputs which will be available as GPIOs. Any output with either the function set to -1 or with the corresponding bit set in this mask will be available for use as a GPIO pin

SERVO_RC_FS_MSK.d: Bitmask of scaled passthru output channels which will be set to their trim value during rc failsafe instead of holding their last position before failsafe.

SERVO_32_ENABLE.d: This allows for up to 32 outputs, enabling parameters for outputs above 16

SERVO1_MIN.d: minimum PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

SERVO1_MAX.d: maximum PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

SERVO1_TRIM.d: Trim PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

SERVO1_REVERSED.d: Reverse servo operation. Set to 0 for normal operation. Set to 1 to reverse this output channel.

SERVO1_FUNCTION.d: Function assigned to this servo. Setting this to Disabled(0) will setup this output for control by auto missions or MAVLink servo set commands. any other value will enable the corresponding function

SERVO_BLH_MASK.d: Enable of BLHeli pass-thru servo protocol support to specific channels. This mask is in addition to motors enabled using SERVO_BLH_AUTO (if any)

SERVO_BLH_AUTO.d: If set to 1 this auto-enables BLHeli pass-thru support for all multicopter motors

SERVO_BLH_TEST.d: Setting SERVO_BLH_TEST to a motor number enables an internal test of the BLHeli ESC protocol to the corresponding ESC. The debug output is displayed on the USB console.

SERVO_BLH_TMOUT.d: This sets the inactivity timeout for the BLHeli protocol in seconds. If no packets are received in this time normal MAVLink operations are resumed. A value of 0 means no timeout

SERVO_BLH_TRATE.d: This sets the rate in Hz for requesting telemetry from ESCs. It is the rate per ESC. Setting to zero disables telemetry requests

SERVO_BLH_DEBUG.d: When set to 1 this enabled verbose debugging output over MAVLink when the blheli protocol is active. This can be used to diagnose failures.

SERVO_BLH_OTYPE.d: When set to a non-zero value this overrides the output type for the output channels given by SERVO_BLH_MASK. This can be used to enable DShot on outputs that are not part of the multicopter motors group.

SERVO_BLH_PORT.d: This sets the mavlink channel to use for blheli pass-thru. The channel number is determined by the number of serial ports configured to use mavlink. So 0 is always the console, 1 is the next serial port using mavlink, 2 the next after that and so on.
SERVO_BLH_PORT.r: 0:Console,1:Mavlink Serial Channel1,2:Mavlink Serial Channel2,3:Mavlink Serial Channel3,4:Mavlink Serial Channel4,5:Mavlink Serial Channel5

SERVO_BLH_POLES.d: This allows calculation of true RPM from ESC's eRPM. The default is 14.

SERVO_BLH_3DMASK.d: Mask of channels which are dynamically reversible. This is used to configure ESCs in '3D' mode, allowing for the motor to spin in either direction. Note that setting an ESC as reversible with this option on AM32 will result in the forward direction of the ESC changing. You can combine with parameter with the SERVO_BLH_RVMASK parameter to maintain the same direction when the ESC is in 3D mode as it has in unidirectional (non-3D) mode.

SERVO_BLH_BDMASK.d: Mask of channels which support bi-directional dshot telemetry. This is used for ESCs which have firmware that supports bi-directional dshot allowing fast rpm telemetry values to be returned for the harmonic notch.

SERVO_BLH_RVMASK.d: Mask of channels which are reversed. This is used to configure ESCs to reverse motor direction. Note that when combined with SERVO_BLH_3DMASK this will change what direction is considered to be forward.

SERVO_FTW_MASK.d: Servo channel mask specifying FETtec ESC output.

SERVO_FTW_RVMASK.d: Servo channel mask to reverse rotation of FETtec ESC outputs.

SERVO_ROB_POSMIN.d: Position minimum at servo min value. This should be within the position control range of the servos, normally 0 to 4095

SERVO_ROB_POSMAX.d: Position maximum at servo max value. This should be within the position control range of the servos, normally 0 to 4095

SERVO_SBUS_RATE.d: This sets the SBUS output frame rate in Hz.

SERVO_VOLZ_MASK.d: Enable of volz servo protocol to specific channels

SERVO_VOLZ_RANGE.d: Range to map between 1000 and 2000 PWM. Default value of 200 gives full +-100 deg range of extended position command. This results in 0.2 deg movement per US change in PWM. If the full range is not needed it can be reduced to increase resolution. 40 deg range gives 0.04 deg movement per US change in PWM, this is higher resolution than possible with the VOLZ protocol so further reduction in range will not improve resolution. Reduced range does allow PWMs outside the 1000 to 2000 range, with 40 deg range 750 PWM results in a angle of -30 deg, 2250 would be +30 deg. This is still limited by the 200 deg maximum range of the actuator.

SID_AXIS.d: Controls which axis are being excited.  Set to non-zero to see more parameters
SID_AXIS.r: 0:None,1:Input Roll Angle,2:Input Pitch Angle,3:Input Yaw Angle,4:Recovery Roll Angle,5:Recovery Pitch Angle,6:Recovery Yaw Angle,7:Rate Roll,8:Rate Pitch,9:Rate Yaw,10:Mixer Roll,11:Mixer Pitch,12:Mixer Yaw,13:Mixer Thrust,14:Measured Lateral Position,15:Measured Longitudinal Position,16:Measured Lateral Velocity,17:Measured Longitudinal Velocity,18:Input Lateral Velocity,19:Input Longitudinal Velocity

SID_MAGNITUDE.d: Magnitude of sweep in deg, deg/s and 0-1 for mixer outputs.

SID_F_START_HZ.d: Frequency at the start of the sweep

SID_F_STOP_HZ.d: Frequency at the end of the sweep

SID_T_FADE_IN.d: Time to reach maximum amplitude of sweep

SID_T_REC.d: Time taken to complete the sweep

SID_T_FADE_OUT.d: Time to reach zero amplitude at the end of the sweep

SIM_DRIFT_SPEED.d: Gyro drift rate of change in degrees/second/minute

SIM_DRIFT_TIME.d: Gyro drift duration of one full drift cycle (period in minutes)

SIM_ENGINE_MUL.d: Thrust from Motors in SIM_ENGINE_FAIL will be multiplied by this factor

SIM_WIND_SPD.d: Allows you to emulate wind in sim

SIM_WIND_DIR.d: Allows you to set wind direction (true deg) in sim

SIM_WIND_TURB.d: Allows you to emulate random wind variations in sim

SIM_WIND_TC.d: this controls the time over which wind changes take effect

SIM_BATT_VOLTAGE.d: Simulated battery resting voltage (no load sag). Defaults to and clipped to the battery model's maximum voltage. Changes re-initialize the state of charge, and values below the maximum indicate a partially-charged battery. For batteries with unlimited capacity, see `SIM_BATT_CAP_AH`. Value ignored when receiving battery state updates from an external source.

SIM_BATT_CAP_AH.d: Simulated battery capacity. Changes re-initialize the state of charge of the battery. Set to 0 for unlimited capacity. Value ignored when receiving battery state updates from an external source.

SIM_SONAR_GLITCH.d: Probablility a sonar glitch would happen

SIM_SONAR_RND.d: Scaling factor for simulated sonar noise

SIM_RC_FAIL.d: Allows you to emulate rc failures in sim
SIM_RC_FAIL.r: 0:Disabled,1:No RC pusles,2:All Channels neutral except Throttle is 950us

options.label: All Channels neutral except Throttle is 950us

SIM_GRPE_ENABLE.d: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the gripper servo simulation

SIM_GRPE_PIN.d: The pin number that the gripper emp is connected to. (start at 1)

SIM_GRPS_ENABLE.d: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the gripper servo simulation

SIM_GRPS_PIN.d: The pin number that the gripper servo is connected to. (start at 1)

SIM_GRPS_GRAB.d: PWM value in microseconds sent to Gripper to initiate grabbing the cargo

SIM_GRPS_RELEASE.d: PWM value in microseconds sent to Gripper to release the cargo

SIM_IE24_ENABLE.d: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the FuelCell simulator

SIM_IE24_STATE.d: Explicitly specify a state for the generator to be in

SIM_IE24_ERROR.d: Explicitly specify an error code to send to the generator

SIM_PARA_ENABLE.d: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the Parachute simulation

SIM_PARA_PIN.d: The pin number that the Parachute pyrotechnics are connected to. (start at 1)

SIM_PLD_ENABLE.d: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the Preland simulation

SIM_PLD_HEIGHT.d: Precland device center's height above SITL origin. Assumes a 2x2m square as station base

SIM_PLD_YAW.d: Precland device systems rotation from north

SIM_PLD_RATE.d: Precland device rate. e.g led patter refresh rate, RF message rate, etc.

SIM_PLD_TYPE.d: Precland device radiance type: it can be a cylinder, a cone, or a sphere.

SIM_PLD_SHIP.d: This makes the position of the landing beacon follow the simulated ship from SIM_SHIP. The ship movement is controlled with the SIM_SHIP parameters

bitmask.label: Swap first 4 and last 4 servos (for quadplane testing)
Demix heli servos and send roll/pitch/collective/yaw

SIM_RICH_ENABLE.d: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the RichenPower simulator

SIM_RICH_CTRL.d: The pin number that the RichenPower spinner servo is connected to. (start at 1)

SIM_SB_MASS.d: mass of blimp not including lifting gas

SIM_SB_ARM_LEN.d: distance from center of mass to one motor

SIM_SB_MOT_THST.d: thrust at max throttle for one motor

SIM_SB_YAW_RT.d: maximum yaw rate with full left throttle at target altitude

SIM_SB_COL.d: center of lift position above CoG

SIM_SB_FLR.d: amount of additional lift generated by the helper balloon (for the purpose of ascent), as a proportion of the 'neutral buoyancy' lift

SIM_SERVO_SPEED.d: servo speed (time for 60 degree deflection). If DELAY and FILTER are not set then this is converted to a 1p lowpass filter. If DELAY or FILTER are set then this is treated as a rate of change limit

SIM_SHIP_PSIZE.d: Diameter of the circle the ship is traveling on

SIM_SHIP_OFS_X.d: Defines the offset of the ship's landing pad w.r.t. the ship's origin, i.e. where the beacon is placed on the ship (X-axis)

SIM_SHIP_OFS_Y.d: Defines the offset of the ship's landing pad w.r.t. the ship's origin, i.e. where the beacon is placed on the ship (Y-axis)

SIM_SHIP_OFS_Z.d: Defines the offset of the ship's landing pad w.r.t. the ship's origin, i.e. where the beacon is placed on the ship (Z-axis)

SIM_SLUP_LINELEN.d: Slung Payload line length in meters

SIM_SLUP_DRAG.d: Slung Payload drag coefficient.  Higher values increase drag and slow the payload more quickly

SIM_SLUP_SYSID.d: Slung Payload MAVLink system id to distinguish it from others on the same network

SIM_SPR_ENABLE.d: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the Sprayer simulation

SIM_SPR_PUMP.d: The pin number that the Sprayer pump is connected to. (start at 1)

SIM_SPR_SPIN.d: The pin number that the Sprayer spinner servo is connected to. (start at 1)

SIM_TA_ENABLE.d: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the simulated tonealarm

SIM_TETH_ENABLE.d: Enable or disable the tether simulation

SIM_TETH_DENSITY.d: Linear mass density of the tether wire

SIM_TETH_LINELEN.d: Maximum length of the tether line in meters

SIM_TETH_SYSID.d: MAVLink system ID for the tether simulation, used to distinguish it from other systems on the network

SIM_TETH_STUCK.d: Enable or disable a stuck tether simulation

SIM_TETH_SPGCNST.d: Spring constant for the tether to simulate elastic forces when stretched beyond its maximum length

SIM_TETH_DMPCNST.d: Damping constant for the tether to simulate resistance based on change in stretch

SIM_VICON_POS_X.d: SITL vicon position on vehicle in Forward direction

SIM_VICON_POS_Y.d: SITL vicon position on vehicle in Right direction

SIM_VICON_POS_Z.d: SITL vicon position on vehicle in Down direction

SIM_VICON_YAW.d: SITL vicon yaw angle in earth frame

SIM_VICON_YAWERR.d: SITL vicon yaw added to reported yaw sent to vehicle

SIM_VICON_P_SD.d: SITL vicon position standard deviation for gaussian noise

SIM_VICON_V_SD.d: SITL vicon velocity standard deviation for gaussian noise

SIM_VICON_QUAL.d: SITL vicon odometry quality field sent in MAVLink ODOMETRY message (-1=failure, 0=unknown, 1-100=quality)

SIM_VOLZ_ENA.d: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the Volz simulator

SIM_VOLZ_MASK.d: mask of servo output channels to override with values from Volz protocol.  Note these are indexed from 0 - so channel 3 (usually throttle) has value 4 in this bitmask (1<<2).

SIM_VOLZ_FMASK.d: fail servo at current position.  Channel 1 is bit 0.

SPRAY_ENABLE.d: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the sprayer

SPRAY_PUMP_RATE.d: Desired pump speed when travelling 1m/s expressed as a percentage

SPRAY_SPINNER.d: Spinner's rotation speed in PWM (a higher rate will disperse the spray over a wider area horizontally)

SPRAY_SPEED_MIN.d: Speed minimum at which we will begin spraying

SPRAY_PUMP_MIN.d: Minimum pump speed expressed as a percentage

SRTL_ACCURACY.d: SmartRTL accuracy. The minimum distance between points.

SRTL_POINTS.d: SmartRTL maximum number of points on path. Set to 0 to disable SmartRTL.  100 points consumes about 3k of memory.

STAT_BOOTCNT.d: Number of times board has been booted

STAT_RESET.d: Seconds since January 1st 2016 (Unix epoch+1451606400) since statistics reset (set to 0 to reset statistics, other set values will be ignored)

STAT_DISTFLWN.d: Estimate of total distance flown since statistics reset

TCAL_ENABLED.d: Enable temperature calibration. Set to 0 to disable. Set to 1 to use learned values. Set to 2 to learn new values and use the values

TCAL_TEMP_MIN.d: Minimum learned temperature. This is automatically set by the learning process

TCAL_TEMP_MAX.d: Maximum learned temperature. This is automatically set by the learning process

TCAL_BARO_EXP.d: Learned exponent for barometer temperature correction

TEMP_LOG.r: 0:Disabled,1:Log all instances,2:Log only instances with sensor source set to None

options.label: Log only instances with sensor source set to None

TEMP1_BUS.d: Temperature sensor bus number, typically used to select from multiple I2C buses

TEMP1_ADDR.d: Temperature sensor address, typically used for I2C address

TEMP1_SRC.d: Sensor Source is used to designate which device's temperature report will be replaced by this temperature sensor's data. If 0 (None) then the data is only available via log. In the future a new Motor temperature report will be created for returning data directly.
TEMP1_SRC.r: 0:None,1:ESC,2:Motor,3:Battery Index,4:Battery ID/SerialNumber,5:CAN based Pitot tube,6:DroneCAN-out on AP_Periph,7:Servo motor,8:Servo PCB

TEMP1_SRC_ID.d: Sensor Source Identification is used to replace a specific instance of a system component's temperature report with the temp sensor's. Examples: TEMP_SRC = 1 (ESC), TEMP_SRC_ID = 1 will set the temp of ESC1. TEMP_SRC = 3 (BatteryIndex),TEMP_SRC_ID = 2 will set the temp of BATT2. TEMP_SRC = 4 (BatteryId/SerialNum),TEMP_SRC_ID=42 will set the temp of all batteries that have param BATTn_SERIAL = 42.

TEMP1_PIN.d: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for temprature monitoring. Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

TEMP1_A0.d: a0 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP1_A1.d: a1 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP1_A2.d: a2 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP1_A3.d: a3 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP1_A4.d: a4 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP1_A5.d: a5 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP1_MSG_ID.d: Sets the message device ID this backend listens for

TEMP1_RTD_NOM.d: Nominal RTD resistance used to calculate temperature, typically 100 or 1000 ohms.

TEMP1_RTD_REF.d: Reference resistance used to calculate temperature, in ohms

TERRAIN_ENABLE.d: enable terrain data. This enables the vehicle storing a database of terrain data on the SD card. The terrain data is requested from the ground station as needed, and stored for later use on the SD card. To be useful the ground station must support TERRAIN_REQUEST messages and have access to a terrain database, such as the SRTM database.

TERRAIN_SPACING.d: Distance between terrain grid points in meters. This controls the horizontal resolution of the terrain data that is stored on te SD card and requested from the ground station. If your GCS is using the ArduPilot SRTM database like Mission Planner or MAVProxy, then a resolution of 100 meters is appropriate. Grid spacings lower than 100 meters waste SD card space if the GCS cannot provide that resolution. The grid spacing also controls how much data is kept in memory during flight. A larger grid spacing will allow for a larger amount of data in memory. A grid spacing of 100 meters results in the vehicle keeping 12 grid squares in memory with each grid square having a size of 2.7 kilometers by 3.2 kilometers. Any additional grid squares are stored on the SD once they are fetched from the GCS and will be loaded as needed.

TERRAIN_OPTIONS.d: Options to change behavior of terrain system. The Accept Old Terrain Data option can be used to accept terrain data generated from before the terrain database bugs were fixed. The bugs in the data were all fixed from 24th February 2026. If you really want to risk using the old terrain data then you can set this option, otherwise remove the old terrain data by formatting your microSD card or renaming the /APM/TERRAIN folder. Then downloaded updated data from https://terrain.ardupilot.org, or let the automatic terrain download repopulate your terrain data.

TERRAIN_MARGIN.d: Margin in centi-meters to accept terrain data from the GCS. This can be used to allow older terrain data generated with less accurate latitude/longitude scaling to be used

TERRAIN_OFS_MAX.d: The maximum adjustment of terrain altitude based on the assumption that the vehicle is on the ground when it is armed. When the vehicle is armed the location of the vehicle is recorded, and when terrain data is available for that location a height adjustment for terrain data is calculated that aligns the terrain height at that location with the altitude recorded at arming. This height adjustment is applied to all terrain data. This parameter clamps the amount of adjustment. A value of zero disables the use of terrain height adjustment.

TERRAIN_CACHE_SZ.d: The number of 32x28 cache blocks to keep in memory. Each block uses about 1800 bytes of memory

TMODE_ENABLE.d: tmode (or "toy" mode) gives a simplified user interface designed for mass market drones. Version1 is for the SkyViper V2450GPS. Version2 is for the F412 based boards

TMODE_MODE1.d: This is the initial mode when the vehicle is first turned on. This mode is assumed to not require GPS

TMODE_MODE2.d: This is the secondary mode. This mode is assumed to require GPS

TMODE_ACTION1.d: This is the action taken when the left action button is pressed

TMODE_ACTION2.d: This is the action taken when the right action button is pressed

TMODE_ACTION3.d: This is the action taken when the power button is pressed

TMODE_ACTION4.d: This is the action taken when the left action button is pressed while the left (Mode) button is held down

TMODE_ACTION5.d: This is the action taken when the right action is pressed while the left (Mode) button is held down

TMODE_ACTION6.d: This is the action taken when the power button is pressed while the left (Mode) button is held down

TMODE_LEFT.d: This is the action taken when the left (Mode) button is pressed

TMODE_LEFT_LONG.d: This is the action taken when the left (Mode) button is long-pressed

TMODE_TRIM_AUTO.d: This is the amount of automatic stick trim that can be applied when disarmed with sticks not moving. It is a PWM limit value away from 1500

TMODE_RIGHT.d: This is the action taken when the right (Return) button is pressed

TMODE_FLAGS.d: Bitmask of flags to change the behaviour of tmode. DisarmOnLowThrottle means to disarm if throttle is held down for 1 second when landed. ArmOnHighThrottle means to arm if throttle is above 80% for 1 second. UpgradeToLoiter means to allow takeoff in LOITER mode by switching to ALT_HOLD, then auto-upgrading to LOITER once GPS is available. RTLStickCancel means that on large stick inputs in RTL mode that LOITER mode is engaged

TMODE_VMIN.d: This is the battery voltage below which no output limiting is done

TMODE_VMAX.d: This is the battery voltage above which thrust min is used

TMODE_TMIN.d: This sets the thrust multiplier when voltage is high

TMODE_TMAX.d: This sets the thrust multiplier when voltage is low

TMODE_LOAD_MUL.d: This scales the load test output, as a value between 0 and 1

TMODE_LOAD_FILT.d: This filters the load test output. A value of 1 means no filter. 2 means values are repeated once. 3 means values are repeated 3 times, etc

TMODE_LOAD_TYPE.d: This sets the type of load test

VISO_POS_X.d: X position of the camera in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin.

VISO_POS_Y.d: Y position of the camera in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin.

VISO_POS_Z.d: Z position of the camera in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin.

VISO_SCALE.d: Visual odometry scaling factor applied to position estimates from sensor

VISO_DELAY_MS.d: Visual odometry sensor delay relative to inertial measurements

VISO_VEL_M_NSE.d: Visual odometry velocity measurement noise in m/s

VISO_POS_M_NSE.d: Visual odometry position measurement noise minimum (meters). This value will be used if the sensor provides a lower noise value (or no noise value)

VISO_YAW_M_NSE.d: Visual odometry yaw measurement noise minimum (radians), This value will be used if the sensor provides a lower noise value (or no noise value)

VISO_QUAL_MIN.d: Visual odometry will only be sent to EKF if over this value. -1 to always send (even bad values), 0 to send if good or unknown

VTX_ENABLE.d: Toggles the Video Transmitter on and off

VTX_POWER.d: Video Transmitter Power Level. Different VTXs support different power levels, the power level chosen will be rounded down to the nearest supported power level

VTX_BAND.r: 0:Band A,1:Band B,2:Band E,3:Airwave,4:RaceBand,5:Low RaceBand,6:1G3 Band A,7:1G3 Band B,8:Band X,9:3G3 Band A,10:3G3 Band B

VTX_FREQ.d: Video Transmitter Frequency. The frequency is derived from the setting of BAND and CHANNEL

VTX_OPTIONS.d: Video Transmitter Options. Pitmode puts the VTX in a low power state. Unlocked enables certain restricted frequencies and power levels. Do not enable the Unlocked option unless you have appropriate permissions in your jurisdiction to transmit at high power levels. One stop-bit may be required for VTXs that erroneously mimic iNav behaviour.

bitmask.label: Add leading zero byte to requests
Ignore status updates in CRSF and blindly set VTX options

VTX_MAX_POWER.d: Video Transmitter Maximum Power Level. Different VTXs support different power levels, this prevents the power aux switch from requesting too high a power level. The switch supports 6 power levels and the selected power will be a subdivision between 0 and this setting.

WINCH_RATE_MAX.d: Winch deploy or retract rate maximum.  Set to maximum rate with no load.

WINCH_POS_P.d: Winch control position error P gain

WP_RFND_USE.d: This controls if waypoint missions use rangefinder for terrain following

WP_JERK.d: Defines the horizontal jerk in m/s/s used during missions

WP_TER_MARGIN.d: Waypoint Terrain following altitude margin.  Vehicle will stop if distance from target altitude is larger than this margin (in meters)

WP_SPD.d: Speed in m/s which the aircraft will attempt to maintain horizontally during a WP mission

WP_RADIUS_M.d: Distance from a waypoint, that when crossed indicates the wp has been reached.

WP_SPD_UP.d: Speed in m/s which the aircraft will attempt to maintain while climbing during a WP mission

WP_SPD_DN.d: Speed in m/s which the aircraft will attempt to maintain while descending during a WP mission

WP_ACC.d: Horizontal acceleration in m/s/s used during missions

WP_ACC_Z.d: Vertical acceleration in m/s/s used during missions

WP_ACC_CNR.d: Maximum cornering acceleration in m/s/s used during missions.  If zero uses 2x accel value.

WVANE_ENABLE.d: Enable weather vaning.  When active, the aircraft will automatically yaw into wind when in a VTOL position controlled mode. Pilot yaw commands override the weathervaning action.
WVANE_ENABLE.r: -1:Only use during takeoffs or landing see weathervane takeoff and land override parameters,0:Disabled,1:Nose into wind,2:Nose or tail into wind,3:Side into wind,4:tail into wind

options.label: Only use during takeoffs or landing see weathervane takeoff and land override parameters

WVANE_GAIN.d: This converts the target roll/pitch angle of the aircraft into the correcting (into wind) yaw rate. e.g. Gain = 2, roll = 30 deg, pitch = 0 deg, yaw rate = 60 deg/s.

WVANE_ANG_MIN.d: The minimum target roll/pitch angle before active weathervaning will start.  This provides a dead zone that is particularly useful for poorly trimmed quadplanes.

WVANE_HGT_MIN.d: Above this height weathervaning is permitted.  If a range finder is fitted or if terrain is enabled, this parameter sets height AGL.  Otherwise, this parameter sets height above home.  Set zero to ignore minimum height requirement to activate weathervaning.

WVANE_SPD_MAX.d: Below this ground speed weathervaning is permitted. Set to 0 to ignore this condition when checking if vehicle should weathervane.

WVANE_VELZ_MAX.d: The maximum climb or descent speed that the vehicle will still attempt to weathervane. Set to 0 to ignore this condition to get the aircraft to weathervane at any climb/descent rate.  This is particularly useful for aircraft with low disc loading that struggle with yaw control in decent.

WVANE_TAKEOFF.d: Override the weather vaning behaviour when in takeoffs
WVANE_TAKEOFF.r: -1:No override,0:Disabled,1:Nose into wind,2:Nose or tail into wind,3:Side into wind,4:tail into wind

WVANE_LAND.d: Override the weather vaning behaviour when in landing
WVANE_LAND.r: -1:No override,0:Disabled,1:Nose into wind,2:Nose or tail into wind,3:Side into wind,4:tail into wind

bitmask.label: Use pitch when nose or tail-in for faster weathervaning

ZIGZ_AUTO_ENABLE.d: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) ZigZag auto feature

ZIGZ_SPRAYER.d: Enable the auto sprayer in ZigZag mode. SPRAY_ENABLE = 1 and SERVOx_FUNCTION = 22(SprayerPump) / 23(SprayerSpinner) also must be set. This makes the sprayer on while moving to destination A or B. The sprayer will stop if the vehicle reaches destination or the flight mode is changed from ZigZag to other.

ZIGZ_WP_DELAY.d: Waiting time after reached the destination

ZIGZ_SIDE_DIST.d: The distance to move sideways in ZigZag mode

ZIGZ_DIRECTION.d: The direction to move sideways in ZigZag mode

ZIGZ_LINE_NUM.d: Total number of lines for ZigZag auto if 1 or more. -1: Infinity, 0: Just moving to sideways
